review_text: [college infrastructure]: as per my opinion college infrastructure is fabulous and all the necessary facilities and equipment are available inside campus like wifi,stationary shop,cafe ,fruit stall and etc. all though college infrastructure. | [academics]: i study in cse department of university and i really enjoy the academics. the academics of university is well set for students. and the academics is really outstanding and it makes you job-ready.the curriculum is updated. | [placements]: as per my the quality of placements are good. the placement of chitkara university is better that other private colleges. the average package is about 6 lakh in cse branch and as per record 95% students are placed every year. | [campus life]: the campus life of chitkara university is really outstanding and it is mind blowing. the environment in the campus is always filled with joy and enthusiasm. even teachers are friendly and they also engage with students.
college_name: Chitkara University Institute of Engineering and Technology Jhansla

review_text: [college infrastructure]: there is a huge campus. there are many fields like athletics ground, football ground, swimming pool of 10 feets. the college has all the necessary facilities. there was canteen also and which was very hygienic. | [academics]: the quality of teaching is that some of the teachers are unable to make the students understand about the topics. i am sure about my study that it makes my job ready. yes curriculum is updated. overall academics are good. | [placements]: campus placement or campus recruiting is a program conducted within universities or other educational institutions to provide jobs to students nearing completion of their studies. in this type of program, the educational institutions partner with corporations who wish to recruit from the student population.

[college infrastructure]: the campus is well-equipped with high internet speed. good facility of library, and well structured laboratory for the students. infrastructure is so nice that film shooting are also done in our campus. | [academics]: best campus for exposure. my department was research-oriented. so many students went aboard from their research work and training purpose. special faculty was also alloted for training and recruitment. | [placements]: the highest package was 33 lpa and lowest package wad 17 lpa. most of the students got internships and got pre-placement offers , some student got placement in it companies, health insurance company. | [campus life]: well educated students and researchers were there in the campus. researchers were also invited for providing the training and placing the students in the right direction. scholarship are also provided. | [anything else]: greatly maintained.

pros/cons: teachers are really good, supportive and helpful library is available 24/7 and we can take books from there placement is good when compared to other private colleges the infrastructure is not good when looked from outside although it is govt college but fee is increasing every year by 15% there is no air conditioner for classes but are available in labs | placement experience: from seventh semester students become eligible for campus placement . companies which visit include amazon , deolite ,kpmg, flipkart,myntra and many more . in computer science and it about 85 percent get placed but in core the number of placement is very less about 60-70 percentage is for ece and for other it is below 50% . this year placement was very low due recession. all the above details is according to previous year i am planning to get placement in it .
college_name: University Institute of Engineering, Chandigarh University - [UIE], Mohali

review_text: [college infrastructure]: yes my college has all the necessary infrastructure, facilities like gym, swimming pool, tennis court, volleyball ground, basketball ground and other equipments like laboratory and computer labs.the campus is fully cleaned and hygienic | [academics]: right now i am studying btech cse which i find more interesting and the quality of education is totally outstanding. i think at this moment i am half ready for job but will be fully prepared soon according to me. | [placements]: according to my perspective the placement cell has a good image in the mind of students. we are offered 100% placements with good packages that help in securing our future and we observe it a positive point. | [campus life]: my college campus is very attractive it has a good infrastructure containing different kinds of facility that help me securing my future and studying well it . alse i see that our faculty teachers and staff are very cooperative and supportive.
college_name: Chitkara University Institute of Engineering and Technology Jhansla

review_text: [college infrastructure]: infrastructure is the best part to be appreciated in nit-a. it has a world-class infrastructure. not all colleges can afford it. nit agartala has the best modern infrastructure compared to at least 20 nits, being established in 1965. coming to the hostels, they are the beautiful places that you get to spend a part of your b.tech life. i assure you that nit-a hostels are of such a kind. first-years get rnt hostel, which will blow your mind. such a beautiful place to spend. also the academic ones! | [academics]: most of the core faculty hold doctorates. they are ready to teach whatever you want. the curriculum is excellent. as per the new syllabus, entire 7th and 8th semester of electrical engineering is dedicated to internship and to gain industrial skills. after all its engineering. it's your responsibility to build up your skills whenever you get free time. resources inside nit-a are adequate to make you industry-ready. labs are of a very good standard and always open for practice. definitely, you ca | [placements]: as per director sir's words, nit-a has a placement percentage of 80 and is expected to be raised to 85% this year. in simple words, you will surely be placed with a very decent package of 9l above if you maintain a cgpa of 8.0+. no need to worry about the placements. the average package rose to 6 lacs compared to 5.11 lacs last year. | [campus life]: there are many cultural and technical clubs to join if you wish. you have plenty of chances to show off yourself. there are two annual fests. aayam-the technical fest. moksha-the cultural fest. they are held in odd and even semesters respectively. there are plenty of dhabas, cafes, parlours where we can eat tasty food. there is also a paid south canteen, where we get tasty tiffins and south style curries. you will enjoy the campus!!

[college infrastructure]: very good infrastructure with all facilities. every classroom contains central ac with smartboards. a very good library with number of books are maintained. each domain will have their own respective labs in the academic block. | [academics]: the backbone of iiit naya raipur is the academics and curriculum. each branch will have excellent curriculum and it is updated every year which includes all the recent developments in the field. iiit naya raipur is the first college in central india with data science and artificial intelligence branch. | [placements]: our college has 100% placements every year and the average salary is around 10lpa and the highest salary till now is around 60lpa. it increases every year, and the process will be easy and pleasant for all the students. | [campus life]: as there are only 3 branches in our college till now, you may not see the campus with full of students. but still, you can enjoy with your friends and don't feel disappointed. also, you get full support from the faculty side.
college_name: Dr Shyama Prasad Mukherjee International Institute of Information Technology Naya Raipur

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the campus is so huge as it's area is around 820 acres .campus is fully air conditionered ,as all classrooms and labs have air conditioner.wifi facility is also available in campus .there is a ground to play football but other grounds are on construction and will be constructed very soon .medical dispensary is available 24*7 in campus other indoor games like chess ,pool and carrom are also present .security is also on next level both for hostel and canpus . | [academics]: faculties in our college are qualified with mtech degree from top nit and iit ,research work is also available in college as many students are interested in research also .curriculum makes student industry ready because of topics and subjects mentioned in syllabus .professors teach in short way which is very simple and student can grab it easily . | [placements]: maximum no of student get placed every year as highest package offered by a company was 44lpa and average package is 8-10 lpa .the companies such as microsoft ,de shaw ,goldman sachs visits the college .the lowest packages was lpa .internship opportunities are also offered by companies such as amazon ,microsoft ,oyo.etc .in 2017 it is shifted to it's permanent campus which also attract many companies . | [campus life]: campus life is best .as technical events ,cultural events ,festival celebration and workshops are part of extra curricular activities.support staff is always ready to help and the startup cell will defenietly help you if you will take idea to them .our college is in jodhpur and the location of college is also good.seniors are also active and supportive as they organise many sessions for development of students .

[college infrastructure]: the campus boasts state of the art facilities , providing a conductive learning environment , modern classrooms and well equipped laboratories enhance the education experience . a vast library offers a wealth of resources | [academics]: the dedication to scholarly pursuits is truly commendable . a vibrant intellectual community fosters a rich learning environment . exploring new ideas and research is highly encouraged within the institution . | [placements]: securing a rewarding milestone . dedicated preparation and a strong skill sets are essential foe success . exploring various career paths can help identify the best fit . networking with professional offers valuable insights
college_name: NMAM Institute of Technology Karkala Taluk

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the college has all the necessary amenities with a good environment. there are three cafes which include cfe96, dewsis, and yamuna cafe. several classes are there which can accommodate more than 80 students. overall it has a beautiful environment with several trees. | [academics]: i am doing btech at this college and from the name as it suggests it includes more tech-oriented subjects. the college provides with all the necessary corses in the four years which help students rack big companies. the teaching is wonderful and the professors are very interactive and helping. | [placements]: mnnit is famous for its placement records from the last few years. there is a placement cell that conducts various events to give training to its students so that it can bridge the gap between the students and the employers.
college_name: Motilal Nehru National Institute of Technology Allahabad Prayagraj

review_text: [college infrastructure]: i find the infrastructure average.it consists of a lot of discarded materials which can be used properly if it is renovated and can be of great help.the campus is equipped with spacious classrooms, well-equipped laboratories,and modern amenities,providing students with the resources they need to excel in their academic pursuits. | [academics]: the university's faculty members are highly qualified and experienced, providing students with a supportive learning environment.the academics area is good.i found the faculties quite helpful and hardworking.a few things can make more interesting maybe some more communication between students and teachers. | [placements]: the university has a dedicated placement cell that works closely with students to enhance their employability skills and connect them with potential employers.the university's emphasis on industry -oriented education and practical training plays a crucial role in preparing students for successful placements. recruiters are from big mnc's .

i mean in the case of metallurgy department the highest package our dedicated senior has got is 15 lakh that's so unfair dude. i mean can you imagine if you get 10 on 10 cgpa and you get 15 lakh salary, i mean hell yes it's possible only in metallurgy. i asked my seniors about this internship thing, one thing he told about me is industrial training which is compulsory and over 1 month you should work for it to complete or if you have interest you can do one or more internship.before approaching you have to get it approved by head of department, in our college it's anand sirwho is incharge of it.so ya that's how it works.ohh ya one important thing i forgot to tell you that intenship that you're willing to do, someone should be working there in that particular instituition and also you should have contact with them or you can even email professor who are working there.let me be quite honest with my opinion when i got admission to this college i was very excited and also i felt bad i didn't get mechanical branch but it's ok on the side note metallurgy department is quite crazy lectures are lenient and i am honest that subject is quite boring for me exam structure is divided into as follows quiz 1,quiz 2+ assignments+ surprise tests=25% mid semester exam=25% end semester exam=50% there you go i think i have answered your question way better than anyone else.

[college infrastructure]: college has great infrastructure, away from city . campus is clean and well maintained. facilities including stationary, cafeteria and much more are within the campus. laboratories are well equipped and classes are well structured. | [academics]: teachers are well qualified and dedicated. curriculum is well planned and balanced between academics and practical implementations. not only do we prepare ourself to face interviews but are also prepared to work within the strict deadlines of corporate life. | [placements]: great placements. even at the time of pandemic various companies reached campus offline and online to give an equal chance to each student. we have startups and mnc's reaching campus which further includes mass recruiters and product based companies visiting campus.

[college infrastructure]: the infrastructure is modern and well developed. we have all the basic amenities available on campus. the hostels could be better. wifi is available on campus for students and faculty. the quality of mess food is average though. | [academics]: the btech computer science curriculum is updated regularly. the teaching material and curriculum is focused on industry-relevant skills and requirements. the quality of teaching is decent but can be improved. | [placements]: placements are average as of now but are steadily improving. the university is relatively new so that is to be expected. however, the university is working towards bringing more internship and placement opportunities for students. | [campus life]: campus life is fun and engaging. we have several facilities available on campus, especially for sports. however, the campus location is rather isolated from the main city so students find it difficult to travel to and fro.

the college enjoys a rich alumni base from all over the world. most of the faculty members are well-read and helpful and can be approached by students anytime. although faculty to student ratio is a bit less. on an average, there are 70 students in a class. the software branches like cse, it and et have decent placement trends. the college also offers engineering courses from branches like oil plastic biochemical etc. that we don't find in many colleges. terms exams are of a moderate level that tests your concepts although you can pass them easily if you study the concepts even after studying for a week before examsyearly fees of the college for a year is 135000 for the first year and 120000 for subsequent years along with hostel and mess fees of around 60000 per year. effectively one btech here costs around 7 lakhs. although the fees might seem a bit hiked for a govt. college but if you are hailing from ews category you get a scholarship from up government scheme. the scholarship ranges from 40000 to 100000 according to your category and fees.

pros/cons: faculties are well qualified , most have phd from iits. they will genuinely help you if you approach them. they will even help you personally if you approach them. they library is small but has books related to academics ,even if you submit books lately, no fine is imposed generally. dance, music clubs of this college are pretty active . though the college is running on a temporary campus ,sports facility especially football ,cricket ,swimming are good. campus is very small as it is currently running on temporary campus. no social life. hostel life is also very bad. in labs, many components don't work and nobody fixes it. we have to go through alot of trouble during experiments of circuital subjects like digital, analog etc. | placement experience: placements are good .from 7th semester ,students are eligible for campus placements. companies like jio ,samsung , adobe ,microsoft ,tcs have visited our college . highest package of 22 lpa and average of 12lpa is currently the statistics of our placement. around 89 percent of students get placed. i am also planning to get a placement from the college.
college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology - [IIITK], Kalyani

review_text: the fee is quite high than government colleges but lower than some other private colleges. details about various scholarships schemes with eligibility criteria are available on the website, changes are made periodically, therefore can't be specific about the scholarships. to my knowledge college used to provide scholarship to a student from each year with family annual income less than 2lakhs which is no longer available.annual fests and tech fests are top notch. the library is well set up with all books of requirement. the classrooms are well ventilated and ac ducts are installed. regarding sports, it is a bit disappointing. games like football or cricket can't be played due to lack of a bigger playground. club activities are regular and highly engaging into different fields.
college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT]

review_text: [college infrastructure]: this campus has been the best of all the amity university campuses. the campus has all the facility and infrastructure. the classrooms are best in class, fully equipped with all the technologies in it. there is presence of library with multiple thousand of books. presence of labs, sports centres, etc. makes it a whole package. neatness, cleanliness and high maintenance prevails thrroughout the campus. | [academics]: i studied b.a. (hons) - french. the quality of teaching was very good. the curriculum is very well planned, it covers whole field of the subject literally. the completion of course sincerely ensures that, one will be very proficient in french language, which readies one to be job-ready. | [placements]: the placements are good enough. one who is sincere and a good student definitely catches the opportunities provided for placements. the highest package rewarded was above ten lakhs. while the average package was above five lakhs. the placements were easy and interesting and quite adventurous. there was full support from the university's side. | [campus life]: the college life is above expectations here. nothing is missing from the the campus. everything is present within. i loved the availability of various services within. | [anything else]: everything is good and likeable. only thing, i will say that the fee is on the higher side.

pros/cons: the campus is really beautiful campus it is also called one of the green campus the hostel life really good experience in reva hostel the infrastructure is very good the teaching is also good some students i really don't like in the college the full day of the college it is bad experience the jam in the food court really irritates me | course curriculum overview: they are various curriculum activity in reva university they are good number of hackathon conducted very good experience about hackathon i will get here they even take u to visit industry the exams they conduct will be have very good gap

[college infrastructure]: yes it has. everything is good. especially washrooms. they are always clean. few of the furnitures will be broken but they would change it within 1 or 2 days. the entire campus is filled with greens and it good to have a walk around inside the campus. | [academics]: computer science communication engineering because i feel interested towards it. yeah my syllabus is an updated version. and it proves to be really helpful to provide solutions for current problems statements. | [placements]: good. they would give placement training for students since 1st semester by training us on the topics of apptitude, programming and other communication development skills subjects. this indeed gives students a strength to face the interview without any stress and tension. | [campus life]: it is the best

review_text: [college infrastructure]: it has good good infrastructure and great atmosphere and it has all the necessary facilities and well equipped and everything is provided there but the is there is no proper signals and no proper wifi facilities and good and hygiene food is provided | [academics]: i have bba in this college and quality of teaching is ok and all the teachers all good but few lectures are not good they only care for there job. i don't think so this college gives more importance to engineering students | [placements]: tue quality of placements are really really bad they will not management related jobs rather they provide engineering related jobs,maybe i think 50% of students are placed through the college, average salary they provide is 5lpa | [campus life]: it's is good i have met lot of friends and bunked many classes and worst i experience is from their stupid rules and regulations which is of no use and support from teachers is not too good it's ok-ok and it's safe for students can handle emergencies

[college infrastructure]: the nit silchar campus is one of the beautiful campus of all the nits in india with full of green forests and lakes.the campus includes hostels, library,sports facilities for the welfare of the students | [academics]: i am currently in the second year of the electrical department. the teachers of the electrical department are really strict and an average in nature.they are very punctual and discipline and they are very strict in the 75percent attendance. | [placements]: the placement of the college is good.the percentage of the electrical department is more than 90 percent with highest package of 55lpa.the average package of the student in electrical department is 15-20lpa.

i choose this course because i wanted to be a data scientist. faculty is good in akgec they are supportive each and every time . teachers are highly qualified and teaching method is also good . in akgec there are two sessional tests and one pre university test in a semester and are not so difficult to passfees for first year is about 1.4 lacs without hostel and university exam fees . scholarships for those students who got admission through counselling and got fee waiver in counselling and there is as such no scholarship for without counselling students

[college infrastructure]: college infrastructure is one of the major reason choosing nit,rourkela over other colleges. the campus have great sport facilities and swimming pool. the classrooms are quite spacious and well maintained. the campus have a good green environment. | [academics]: academics is maintained to be at par with other top colleges in india. in first year every teacher is teaching us quite good and helping us by proving pdfs. they are quite friendly and solve any doubts we ask. | [placements]: placement in nit rourkela is improving every year. the highest placement is usually grabbed by cse student. there are no restrictions generally in placement on the basis of branch. the percentage of student placed is always quite high. | [campus life]: campus life is a major factor for student for choosing nit rourkela. it have great sports facilities and food outlets. students can engage in many club activities. there are many clubs engaging in various field of interests. the teacher and support staff are quite friendly.

[college infrastructure]: our college has all the necessary infrastructure different departments for different courses well maintained labs but there are no air conditionered lab but lecture theaters are air conditionered and well cleaned . | [academics]: good academics but in my branch no such headache for 75 percentage of attendance nobody bother to attend all lecture and tutorials because of it . in other branches the attendance criteria is strictly followed ya worth of money because they provide us placement | [placements]: this is good almost 85%placements in chemical other prepare for either gate or ies and also the salary package is good enough . but we don't get enough salary in core companies so we have to get placed in non core companies. | [campus life]: this is awesome as college has all the grounds for sports and all clubs facilities like dance drama ncc etc so that we can improve overall personality development also all festivals are arranged by us so that we can how to manage .
college_name: Dr B R Ambedkar National Institute of Technology Jalandhar

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the college campus is huge. it has amazing grounds where several sports can be played. there is also a basketball court and a volleyball court. the hostel is also present inside the campus and the cafeteria is decent. | [academics]: the quality of teaching is good. the college focuses on extra curricular activities and technical knowledge both. professors are very experienced and have a great knowledge about their subjects. technical workshops are also organized for development of the student. | [placements]: placements of our college are average. most of the companies offer is about 4 lakhs. mostly students are recruited by mass recruiters. placement activity is managed by the placement cell of the college. the highest package last year was 12 lakhs | [campus life]: college life is amazing as several events and workshops are arranged by the seniors and professionals.

[college infrastructure]: we have 24/7 wi-fi and the labs and good library facilites with anytime access. we also have good facilities at hostel there's no complain at all about the facilities and infrastructure. the quality of facilities we get at hostel is good and about food it's totally different from our home food ofcourse but it's nice. | [academics]: very good knowledge and qualified teachers and who help and guide students to clear their doubts and guide them throughout the course. about semester exams it's completely dependent on our work and if we workhard we don't find papers so tough they are of good standard. | [placements]: software developer engineering, product engineering, data analytics roles, and other core companies core companies are also coming for recruiting and in the previous year placement stats were really good this year's it's going well 50% of them are placed till now with the highest package 52 lpa last year.

[college infrastructure]: the infrastructure is very good having all the facilities. the wifi speed can be increased but overall really nice. the living places are really clean and the food hygiene is very high. good food is given at affordable prices | [academics]: i am studying b. tech cse because i find computers and technology very exciting. if we talk about quality of teaching in lpu, then its quite good. most of the professors are having a pg degree and they are very skillful. | [placements]: placement of lpu is average if we consider average package offered to a student. but,most of the students has a job in their hand when they pass out. top recruiting companies in lpu are amazon, flipkart, adobe, etc. | [campus life]: the best thing about my college is environment and infrastructure which is really awesome. there are several activities in which a student can be a part of like annual sports events, youth vibe, one india one world and may more.

[college infrastructure]: it has all the facilities required for the students as being the finest institute if the research field is concerned. institute has 24 hrs wi-fi facility, very very well-equipped labs with all the facilities required and library with a lot of books. iit delhi being the well-known brand is very well-equipped with all the facilities regarding sports and games. | [academics]: the faculty in the dms is very well qualified and skilled and they has good exposure and experience in the industries. they are always there for students not only for academic help but also for personal mentorship. the teaching techniques involve mostly interactive and urge students to think innovative | [placements]: institute has a record of 100% placements of students who are participated in the placement process. the highest average ctc is around 18.5 lpa while the highest package offered is being 32lakhs. top recruiting companies were reckitt benckiser, oneplus, accenture, deloitte, etc.

pros/cons: green environment is peaceful and there is decent cleanliness the strictness is not so much like we can go out and in leniently the crowd here is really educated, multitalented people and not only nerds attendance is really strict, below 75 your grade is lowered the course feels outdated, it won???t help in placement, its like 30 year old syllabus with little improvements the ratio of male to female is like 10:1, like any other engineering college, but they are all ambitious people | placement experience: the placements have been lowered by 10-20% recently, but they are getting better. you will get a job if you manage cgpa above 7 and are decent with your skills, but then don't dream for 20lpa + jobs, you need to work upon yourself in silence to get 25-50 lpa packages and they do happen in our colleges, highest package in electrical 2024-25 was 52 lpa and in ece cse they had 61 lpa. companies start coming in 3rd year for internships, only 10% manage to get intern in 3rd year, companies like microsoft, atlassian, flipkart, mercedes, jp morgan etc. they do come. placement perecentage is like 75-80% in electrical and it is very low in civil (around 40-50%), in cse it is like 90% or even higher. my plan is to get an intern asap. (maybe in 2nd year theough offcampus)
college_name: Punjab Engineering College University of Technology - [PEC], Chandigarh

review_text: [college infrastructure]: gla university has all necessary infrastructure, facilities, and equipment available for students. the facilities that are gla is providing is computer labs, gla secured fortigate wifi,digital projectors boards for ppt representatiions. | [academics]: i have completed my bba hons in finance and international business domain because these two domains have higher scope in upcoming future. the quality of teaching is up to the mark because all the faculties aur highly qualified. | [placements]: placement quality of gla university in bba is very high also with high lpa. approx 90% students were placed in bba hons. 8.5lpa was the highest at initial stage only. the placement process is way to easy and relaible also gla is very supportive university in mathura with nacc a+ rating | [campus life]: best featues of my gla campus is there 2 canteen, 1 complex , basketball court, q auditorium where all big events organized, 10+ computer labs and many more. teachers are very supportive also if any student facing any problem faculty consultants are also available. | [anything else]: i just want to add on that gla university mathura is great college with nacc a+ ratings also every student gots the best college life here itself.

[college infrastructure]: college infrastructure is of mediocre type which provide you basic necessary facilities to reside within the campus with ok ok type. however renuvation is done time to time to maintain the basic infrasructure. | [academics]: academics is above good level as compared to normal colleges where professor are well qualified with certain level of experience in teaching fiels with variety of project are research by them. core academics are hectic than non core academics. | [placements]: placement varies according to different year but this collrge manages to provides 70% placement to students with decent packages in their domain and enginering branches. noncore hire and give more packages as compare to core profile. | [campus life]: campua life is of below average facilities which include a canteen, amul parloue, central garden,ccc,football ground,tennis court,basketball court,indore gym,average speed wifi ,small cricket ground only,

pros/cons: the departmental profesors are good and friendly the sports facilities are really nice and anyone can go and participate the people here are also very friendly and chill the placement pressure is very high and not everyone is getting good placements the ac facility is not available in hostels the food was not good in the freshers hostel | placement experience: the students can apply for internship via career development centre in the beginning of their pre final year. they can go for off campus intern whenever they want. most of the goo companies would come and mostly for software finance consulting etc. the placements start in the second last semester as far as i know. the competition for placement is getting really high these days
college_name: IIT Kharagpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITKGP], Kharagpur

review_text: [college infrastructure]: infrastructure is the most advanced . the laboratories were the best among all. sports club also had best trainers. living spaces were fine , but they maintained the hygiene to their best. the hostel families and security were top class | [academics]: i was studying computer science. in 3rd year i had the opportunity to select my own interest of courses. i got placed also in the 3 rd year itself and the teachers were very friendly towards the students and were always ready to help the at any point of time | [placements]: this college has a very good placement record i got placed in the 3rd year and i got 8 lakhs per annum salary this college gives 100% placements to their students and best companies come to this college for placement

i got a scholarship of 35 % per sem otherwise normal fees of btech are 1 lakh 20 thousand per semester. but lpu provides various scholarships like if you got 80% and above in 12th you will get a 15% scholarship so your fees get reduced to 1 lakh per sem which is equal to any private collegeevery week ca is taken of 30 marks on laptops and all the faculties are well educated and well behaved and with good experience and the exams are taken mid-term and end sem are quite easy to pass. lpu has around 2000+ teachers.

my jee rank was not so good that i could be admitted to nit. then by seeing ranking in wbjee college list, i found an autonomous college with good reputation about placement, attendance ratio and there is no special uniform for collegecourse syllabus is up to date according to the present scenario. some faculties are good helpful and interactive but some teach really bad. you will most likely use books and online stuffs for studing the course

[college infrastructure]: the college infrastructure is very beautiful with world class sports and academics facilities and equipments. all classrooms are ac equipped with monitor and projector facilities. the labs are fully functional and one of the top labs in india. the campus is filled with sports places where you can play sports like cricket, badminton, basketball, snooker, lawn tennis, table tennis etc. each hostel has a dedicated canteen which follow complete hygiene and provide delicious food. | [academics]: i studied masters in aerospace engineering under propulsion specialisation. my reason to choose this branch was the high class teachings offered in iit bombay. the quality of teaching was world class with some of the most top professors teaching from all over india. | [placements]: the placement body at iit bombay is one of the most organised, helpful and informative bodies across all engineering colleges in india. they provide assistance from making resumes to interview preparation.

[college infrastructure]: average college infrastructure often focus on outdated facilities, poorly maintained buildings, and inadequate resources. students frequently report dissatisfaction with limited classroom space, aging furniture, and unreliable internet connectivity, which can hinder their learning experience. libraries might lack updated resources, with insufficient books, journals, and online databases. inadequate laboratory equipment, outdated technology, and limited access to modern tools in science and engineering departments are also common concerns, impacting hands-on learning opportunities. campus amenities, such as cafeterias, recreational facilities, and study areas, might be scarce or poorly managed. issues like unhygienic washrooms, lack of air conditioning, and insufficient seating in common areas further diminish the college experience. safety concerns may arise from poor lighting and security measures around the campus, creating a sense of vulnerability among students. transportation and accessibility issues, including limited parking and unreliable public transportation options, can add further stress for students. moreover, a lack of investment in infrastructure improvements suggests that college administration might prioritize other areas over student comfort and educational support. ultimately, substandard infrastructure impacts not only students' daily lives but also their academic performance and overall satisfaction with their college experience. | [academics]: a college with a tough academic reputation can be both a rewarding and challenging experience for students. courses at such institutions are often rigorous, demanding a high level of commitment, discipline, and intellectual engagement. students frequently report that the workload is intense, with frequent assignments, exams, and projects that require consistent effort and time management skills. the grading standards tend to be strict, with professors expecting deep comprehension and critical analysis rather than simple memorization. while these rigorous academic standards can foster a strong work ethic and resilience, some students may find the pressure overwhelming. the competitive environment can lead to stress and burnout, especially when students struggle to balance academics with extracurriculars or personal obligations. office hours and faculty support are essential, but some students feel that professors may not always be available or approachable, which adds to their challenges. despite the demanding curriculum, many students acknowledge the value of the education they receive, noting that it prepares them well for future careers or graduate studies. however, the high academic expectations may not be suitable for everyone, and prospective students should consider whether they thrive in high-pressure environments before committing to such a college. | [placements]: colleges with decent placement records offer students solid, if not exceptional, opportunities for employment post-graduation. while not every student lands a top-tier position, most graduates find jobs that match their qualifications and provide a reasonable starting point in their chosen fields. placement cells at these institutions typically have connections with a variety of companies, from mid-sized firms to a few reputable industry players, which ensures a steady stream of job options across different sectors. companies visiting these campuses often hire for entry-level roles with average salary packages that are competitive but may not be industry-leading. internship opportunities are usually available and provide valuable experience, but the college may not always have as many high-profile internship partners as top-tier institutions do. support for skill development and interview preparation is generally available, though it might vary by department. for students with realistic expectations, a college with decent placements can be a good option, especially if they plan to gain experience and later pursue higher studies or explore other career pathways. the placements might not guarantee top positions, but with the right effort, students can secure roles that allow them to grow professionally and build their careers progressively. | [anything else]: good for placement pov.
college_name: Netaji Subhas University of Technology New Delhi

review_text: there are only 3 fest conducted could be annual or tech fests and they are conducted in february or march and they are of 3 days. library: there is the availability of 20lakh+ books in the lpu library. the library system subscribes to several books, magazines, journals, and newspapers. the library system also offers ict systems to its users such as e-books, e-journals, and databases. the e-resources subscribed by the university library are ieee, springers, ebsco, capitaline, manupatra, jgate plus, delnet, jstor, ebooks, etc. amenities in classrooms: as the classrooms are the standard, well fitted with students desks/benches with seats, podium, whiteboards, projectors, soundsystems, cctv camera, etc. spots and extracurricular activities: it has the grounds for cricket, football, table tennis, basketball, and volleyball and it has an indoor stadium too for indoor games and also it has a swimming pool for students. organization: this university has several social groups.i choose this course because i want to be a software engineer. the faculty students ratio is 40 :1 in the practical session or in general. faculty: in lpu, many of the faculty members are ph.d. holders and iits. they are very well in their respective fields. the exam pattern is somehow very difficult as it has 2mins. time limitations, so it will be hard for many of the students. the passing criterion is students must have at least 75% attendance to attend university exams and also depend on class assignments.

review_text: [college infrastructure]: lpu campus is quite huge in all respect , it has excellent infrastructure , according to me lpu is not a just a university but it is a mini township by itself.lpu is also know as the largest university having land 700+ acer . the lpu university is so vast that it has postal code and office. | [academics]: currently i am persuing bachelor degree in the field of computer science . because as senior suggest me that the best educational is provided in lpu are in the field of computer science. faculty in the field of computer science are mostly new . they don't have lot of experience. talking about curriculum which is updated every years. yes they conduct many placements classes so that every one can placed in company | [placements]: lpu doesn't have phenomenal placement record and quite recently amazon have taken 15 students, students for 3rd year got placement in many big companies , highest package in current years is 42 lakhs per annum in the field of computer science.yes lpu mainly focused on placement of every student so they conduct many pep classes which help during the placement time. | [campus life]: being here from 24 month i will says college life is fine. there are many pros and cons some of pros are : 1) great infrastructure 2) good internet connection 3) good environment 4)great amount of extra curricular activities and done of the cons are: 1) need more improvement in faculty 2) pure vegetarian campus 3) lots of students yes it is 100% safe ,it can handle any type of emergency.

review_text: the social and campus life is one of the best in india. there are loads of events and fests in our college. the annual cultural fest is engifest, which is also believed to be one of the biggest north india cultural fests. the tech-fest is invictus which includes loads of competitions, hackathons, fun events and games. sports facilities are the very best. huge grounds and courts for each individual sport and even a huge indoor complex are there.i chose this course over software engineering because i really wanted to study maths (i'm good at it too ). most of the faculties are phd holders. many are good with their subjects and teach well while some don't (exceptions are always there). there were only 2 exams mid sem and end sem in the whole semester.

pros/cons: very big and beautiful campus lot of new things to explore they do not have many rules here and its a very free and easygoing campus . people are allowed to do anything outside the classrooms good reputation if we get graduation here though we don???t get a highly paid work just after ug but if we did our masters too our value is higher. as snu is a part of hcl group it???s mandatory to choose bus for day scholars and no private vehicles are allowed inside the campus and the bus fees are very high and its a fixed amount regardless of the distance the fees structure is very high and its not worth to pay so much just for a ug course as the placement will also not be that good very few people study here and the strength is very small so socialising is difficult and we do not get many contacts | course curriculum overview: we have exams every 20 days our semester happens only for 4 months which is a very short period and its very hectic but this tension and workload will actually help in our future to face any challenges

review_text: [college infrastructure]: yes, it has pretty decent infrastructure. clean and well-maintained. toilets are good and hallways are spacious. hostel room are not good but cleanliness is maintained. good amount of greenery in the campus. | [academics]: the quality of teaching in electronics department is very good in general, especially formicroeconomics and macroeconomics. curicullum is updated to a certain extent.i still don't think it makes you job ready.but it is more of a general phenomenona across colleges | [placements]: placement is not 100% guaranteed, in case the student doesn't take enough effort. but the placement cell makes sure that they guide all students and make the process better. packages were as high as 29 lpa, and cse students bag the best packages. | [campus life]: campus has quite a good vibe.the food of canteen was bad earlier. but after the changer tender in recent times ,it is much better.the canteen and amphi are like a hub for hanging. around. the teachers are quite supportive,staffs are just like those of any other government office nothing different .

pros/cons: the faculty is experienced in terms of academics and industry. they are supportive in terms of doubt solving in exams and other assessments. the clubs and societies of the college are active and hold events every month. this creates a fun environment among students where it is a fun event or an educational event. infrastructure is well maintained as there are air conditioners in classrooms and seminar hall. classrooms and labs are spacious have good quality of furniture. the canteen and library are also maintained. the crowd is not that good in the college sometimes complaints from students are not taken into consideration. faculty is very strict with events that sometimes restrict the fun level of the events | placement experience: final year students are mostly placed in the college with average package of 8 lpa for management programs and 20 lpa for btech programs. companies like deloitte, kpmg and other well know companies come for recruitment. around 50-60 percent students get placed in management programs and 80-90 in technical programs. a lot of students plan for higher degrees like mba and i'm als aiming for that.

review_text: [college infrastructure]: bennett is famous for its great well maintained infrastructure. it have highly equipped classrooms accommodated by wifi and updated curriculum. here you also get libraries, laboratories and a great environment to study. | [academics]: studying mba from this college is one of the good decisions of my life. it offers you regular interactive sessions, and collaborative projects which enhance understanding and helps.in developing skills of students, besides theoretical knowledge. | [placements]: it have good industry connections which enables students to learn directly from the industries thereby providing them hands on experience related to their fields. with several internship and mentorship programs it makes students ready for job. | [campus life]: the campus here is a mix of extracurricular activities, and social experiences. it comes with a variety of clubs, cultural events, and sports, fostering personal growth and leadership skills.

dtu has three festivals, beginning in the final week of january and running until the end of february. dtu's literary festival, tech fest, and cultural festival are among the finest planned in the country. anyone is welcome to join the college's technical and non-technical societies. these societies serve to increase your general confidence while also developing your personality and expertise. dtu has various societies, including pratibimb, nss, ecell, ieee, iet, and many more. there is a very big cricket ground, an indoor sports room with badminton courts and table tennis, a basketball court, a gymnasium, a rugby field, and a gym on campus for encouraging sports activities among students. there is also a very big library with a lot of academic books which you can issue and take home for a specific period of time. there are also books for higher studies in the research section of the library.following the completion of the third year, students are eligible to sit in on the placement season. when it comes to tech jobs, placements at dtu are fantastic. the number of available positions in the non-tech and core sectors is rather low. top recruiters come to the college and allow students from various branches to take their tests. companies like apple, adobe, google, and microsoft are also coming to dtu for recruitment. the t&p department is responsible for conducting the placement process successfully and is very supportive. by the time the placement season ends, almost 90% of the students at dtu are placed somewhere. the highest package of dtu was around 1.5 crores, with the average being 15 lakhs for all branches and 18 lakhs for the computer science branch.

[college infrastructure]: all the necessary instruments for lab, projector for classroom, separate wifi, charger socket everything is provided in the classroom. there is also a sports center, canteens are there which is very low in cost and are making good hygiene food. | [academics]: yes, the academics are here are best in my view of point as everything and every topics are covered in the classrooms. use of projectors, slideshow, practical in laboratories are there. this college has very good teaching quality. by performing practical this make our base knowledge more strong and it makes ready for job. | [placements]: placements in these college is with full 100% efficiency.. as this college is very big and it is government college so many companies came here for hiring svnit students and they provide high amount of package per annum as the average salary is 8 lakhs per annum which is good enough and yes college is very much active and supportive.

review_text: there are many internship opportunities available in this college, but i prefer to focus on my study and get experience from good companies. i have created the project which is based on the ecommerce website. on this website, we have a login & up module card. many more. one company like this provided me with the opportunity of an internship in their projects.i choose this score because i am interested to learn new things. si has an interest in computer applications, learning new technologies, editing, and graphic designing. i also submitted some projects on this course completion. faculties of this college or ph.d. holders. they have some degrees from this college.

[college infrastructure]: the campus is beautiful and full of green spaces, which made it easy to relax and enjoy the outdoors between classes. the professors were incredibly knowledgeable and always willing to go the extra mile to help students succeed. there are plenty of extracurricular activities to choose from, including sports teams, clubs, and volunteer opportunities. overall, we have a great experience at college. | [academics]: i am currently studying mechanical engineering because it has always been a passion of mine. the quality of teaching has been exceptional so far. the professors are knowledgeable and always willing to help. as far as the curriculum goes, i do believe that it is updated and includes all the recent developments in the field. i feel confident that what i am learning will make me job-ready upon graduation. | [placements]: hi there! i attended nit silchar, and i must say that the quality of placements was pretty good. as far as i know, around 80% of the students were placed, which is quite impressive. the highest salary package offered was around 20 lpa, and it went to a student from the cse department. the average salary offered was around 6-7 lpa, which is quite decent, i think speaking from personal experience, the placement process was quite smooth and hassle-free. the college was really supportive. | [campus life]: as a student at nit silchar, i can confidently say that our campus is both beautiful and functional. the best feature is definitely the natural surroundings, with lush greenery and a serene atmosphere that makes studying and living here a joy. however, one downside is the lack of modern facilities, which can be frustrating at times. in terms of activities, there is always something going on at nit silchar. whether you're interested in sports, music, or social events, there is a club or society.

we have a flexible system where we can choose the course faculty and particular slot for each semester. the faculty are friendly you can ask doubt they will help you. mentors will be provided for each student. if you put in your efforts you can pass.the club there is like the coders club maths club literary club. the library is there inside the campus you can borrow books whenever you want it has all department reference books. each year they will have sports day, college day, and cultural.

moksha is the annual fest organized by the students of the college it's a 3 day cultural and tech fest designed for the students to enjoy their college life. in the recent one, kk and lost stories performed so yeah the fest was amazing. there is a central library on the campus and also a small one present in the hostel as well. there are tons of sports facilities present on the campus the only problem is restrictions in the hostel. there are tons of societies in the college like the 40s of them are there. there is tech, cultural, art, and debate various of them.i chose cse because i wanted to learn about computer science(not coding u can learn that regardless of the branch). faculty to the student is kinda screwed like it's not too poor but also not too good since the college has increased its seats quite a lot in recent times so the ratio has fallen. just like every other reputed college quality of faculty is good but there may be exceptions. pressure in college is not too high just regularly studying a little bit to do the work.

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the college has amazing infrastructure. almost places are air conditioned with the exception of few labs for exhaust purposes. there are structures made in amazing architectural style, especially the student plaza which is a student hangout area. campus is wifi-enabled, low speed. food is decently good. a la carte facilities also available in messes. hostel cleanliness is maintained, with presence of private baths. sports facilities are average with the exception marena which is world class. | [academics]: the quality of teaching is decent. but college does provide free coursera certification which is amazing and also the .edu email id which can access a lot of student benefits. the curriculum is updated every two years, but in terms of keeping in track with the latest trend, leaves much to desire. the student clubs here are beneficial for placement preparation as the student culture is very strong with people in top companies and universities giving recommendations. | [placements]: the placements are decent with computer science branches usually having the highest averages. not sure about other branches but people do prefer to go abroad and pursue masters. computer science usually has around 100% placement with 10+ lpa ctc as median. | [campus life]: manipal has one of the best campus lives possible in any indian college. it has a strict no ragging culture plus the seniors also don't care about ragging since they are better than that. the campus green, on a hill with amazing views. the beaches are also quite close with a 10 mins bus ride. there are also hills around the area which makes a fun trekking place. modern in many aspects with all major food chains available and malls. the downside are clubs being present which might be distractive.

[college infrastructure]: the institute is well managed in terms of infrastructure with necessary equipment and facilities. the classrooms, especially for the architecture department have projector facilities, drafting tables, storage facilities etc. the institute also provides hostels. there are separate hostels for the male and female students with mess provided near these. the cleanliness and hygiene is good in hostel areas for ladies. not sure about the boys hostels. | [academics]: i am studying architecture here cause of its blend with the tech field and the art field. the faculties are supportive despite how challenging this field is. the curriculum gets updated every 5 years. the campus life and academics makes you ready for the challenges the professional realm offers. | [placements]: the placements for architecture is not very pleasing. there are rarely any placement opportunities and even then the students wouldn't prefer these. but the placements for b.tech is very good with high salary packages and opportunities. | [campus life]: the campus life is fun with canteens, basketball courts, football fields, swimming pool etc. the sports and club activity here

review_text: the fees structure is sorted in every 6 months to pay the fees of your semester which depends on the course you have selected. total cost of my course till graduation is about 2 lakh excluding security fees they charge 15000 for security purposes which will they return you once the course is completed.the course curriculum is very easy and since i love studying history the course seemed very easy for me and the faculty members are all very nice. they teach us well and also have nice behaviour towards the students.

the computer science course is in one of the most demanded and versatile courses available in the university. this course appeals to my area of interest and job scope. the faculty-to-student ratio is about 1:70 depending upon the branch. all the faculty are ph.d. and teach with years of experience and in a way that is easy and comprehensible for students. the mid sem and end-sem exams have a gap of 1-1.5 months. the semester feels short and so do the holidays.the two main cultural fests of nsut are moksha and resonance which are held in summer and winter respectively. the library has a plethora of books on all subjects. however, issuing books is cumbersome. the library guard does not allow one to enter with their own books. the apj lecture halls have smart boards, ac, good lighting, and foldable seats. there are also separate charging sockets available at each seat.

pros/cons: i liked the campus and the overall environment around myself the faculty are really helpful and kind.they clear my doubt the sports facilities of the college is extremely good the fees is on the higher side as compared to other colleges the campus size of the college is soo small the parking area is not much needs to improve | placement experience: as i am a first year student so i don't know much about in which year i will be eligible for placement.but placement are pretty good as the average package stands around 8 lakhs per annum and it is worth taking admission

review_text: [college infrastructure]: great amenities , infrastructure overall .great ambience overall .well equipped labs and playground .great classrooms ,labs well equipped. hostel and mess are at par compared to other colleges. overall very good. | [academics]: quality education ,professors are highly educated and the curriculum is advance to meet the gap in the engineering course .coding culture is quite popular as the senior junior interaction as quite good. | [placements]: average package this year was fourteen lakhs per annum of cse branch .which is actually comparable to most of the prestigious institutes of the country like nits and iiit .placement cell of the college is great. | [campus life]: the lush green campus of lnm institute of information technology gives you the great experience for your undergraduate program .the students, faculty, management dept all of them are quite supportive .
