[college infrastructure]: our college has all the necessary infrastructure, facilities and equipment to provide students with a really good learning experience. the classrooms are very spacious and comfortable with wide smart boards and projectors. the laboratories are well equipped. we have a large library where you can read and study at peace. the campus has a pretty large ground for miscellaneous sports activities and a basketball court. the hostels are not that great. the living spaces are moreover very clean. | [academics]: i am a second year be telecommunication engineering student. the quality of teaching in my department is really good. the teachers always help us out with whatever we need in our academics. the learning process has been very smooth even through online classes during this pandemic. i feel that our curriculum is very relevant to today's world and is up to date. | [placements]: since i am just completing my second year, i haven't experienced how placements are. but from whatever my seniors have told me, i know that a lot of good companies come to our college and the highest package goes up to 31.5lac p.a. | [campus life]: we have a beautiful college campus. you will feel peace amongst nature. there is greenery everywhere. the campus is very well maintained. when it comes to activities, we have a number of cultural societies and there are events organized very often. i have had a great and fun experience so far. the location is a very convenient and safe area with everything you need around you. there is a hospital right beside the college as well, due to which any emergencies can be handled well.

[college infrastructure]: as a political science student at jadavpur university, i find the infrastructure good for a public institution. the library is amazing with a vast book collection and classrooms are decent though not modern. | [academics]: academically, this college is excellent. the teaching quality is very good here and open discussions are also encouraged by our teachers. the syllabus has a good balance of theory and practical learning. | [placements]: placements for arts students, especially in political science are quite limited. unlike technical departments, there are not many direct job opportunities. most of the students prepare for higher studies or government exams. | [campus life]: i am having a vibrant campus life. there is always something happening like fests, debates and cultural events that bring everyone together. the atmosphere is energetic and i have met a lot of diverse and open-minded people.

the name of the annual fest is eclecia and the name of the tech fest is dakshh. but no fest is conducted since covid pandemic and some unfortunate causes. so, till now i am not able to experience that. the library is very big and every book is present, what one needs in their academic journey. the interdepartmental sports week is conducted every year and the sports teams are very active inside as well as outside of the college. there are some cultural clubs like anubhav(drama), pravasana(photography), resonance (music), etc present in college. technical clubs like geeks, gaming are also present.i like the course and this course has a very vast field in academics as well as in jobs. the faculty-student ratio is perfect. like they assign every faculty for 15 students. all faculties are highly educated. most of them teach very well and everyone has a unique method. the exam should happen after 4.5 months. they notify every student for 1 month. the difficulty is pretty moderate, which means if you understand the module then you'll pass.

[college infrastructure]: the university has really gone to lengths to make sure that state-of-the-art equipment, laboratories, and modernized infrastructure are provided to its students and faculty. adding on to that, there are shuttles winding their way through the campus to assist students to reach from one place to another, especially when the scorching heat of vellore takes a toll on you. | [academics]: as far as academics are concerned, the university provides the basic and fundamental concepts in the student's respective field through a wide range of courses that one can pick (apart from the mandatory ones). however, much like any other private college in the country, it lacks to persuade the students to explore more than what the faculty is aware of. this in turn can also be blamed on the time constraints every faculty has to finish a course in a particular semester. | [placements]: the university is well known for its stellar placement record. it tries to make sure that each and every student who has registered for placements gets a job. the placement cell's good relations with most companies and its good public image can be a reason behind this. | [campus life]: the campus by far is one of the major attractions of vit vellore. given its cosmopolitan nature, multiple clubs and societies, their events conducted by the department of students' welfare, and extravagant technical and cultural festivals, the campus life is what one might call 'colorful'.
college_name: Vellore Institute of Technology Chennai Campus

review_text: [college infrastructure]: yes the college is quite good with all the necessary facilities and they are very easily accessible. the classrooms are good with projectors ac and fan all along the library is very good as it is the biggest of north india. | [academics]: the teaching is very good as the faculties are phd holders are very much proficient in teaching the subjects and the curriculum is updated on regular basis with more and more facilities and opportunities for students. | [placements]: the placements are good as there are multiple companies who come every year to hire talented students and the curriculum is so good that the students are easily getting good packages and almost everyone gets placed in the end | [campus life]: campus life is good as there are multiple events which are happening on a very daily basis. the events are very much cooperative and collaborative as the students get a alot of opportunities. and it is very good for us

[college infrastructure]: college has excellent infrastructure and all available services that a student needs. with a gigantic library and amazing sports facilities, this college gives students real chance to pursue whatever they feel like | [academics]: well i am studying chemical engineering and as far as the quality of education is concerned its top notch as expected from the pride of india l, nitt. we have acs in almost all the rooms and projector too. | [placements]: placements for all batches has always been excellent as all sorts of companies approach this number 1 nit. packages ranging from 8 to highest 35 somethingish lakh for chemical engineering is seen on an average. | [campus life]: just one word when it comes to campus life - chill. its reallh relaxing with all the greenary around and me and the bois just go around cycling in the campus. the weather is always merry and its not that hot either.

[college infrastructure]: we have online classes so no experience but i heard from senior that it's have good infrastructure and so on that basic i can say this but in youtube videos i watched some video related to that so good infastrcture | [academics]: i am studying cse core at vellore campus coz of the high package this year the highest pakage was 75 lakh given to 2 students and that really matter man coz that much amount of money. u will get from a private collage matters | [placements]: in 2022 there are 2700+ super dreams offfer and 3600 dream offer super dreams means 10 above ctc and from 6 to 10 that is dream offer and the highest package of this year ir 2022 was 75 lakh. given by de shaw ig so | [campus life]: due to online class no chances so i can't say anything about that coz but what i heard from senior that it's very hectic never join vit man in a sem u will get 3 times exam. and in a day definitely u will have 2 exam so no campus life

review_text: [college infrastructure]: as we are in a institute of importance our campus has outstanding infrastructure also we have second beautiful campus amongst all nits . we have all departments and practical labs . also we have smart tvs for better and practical understanding | [academics]: this is good but not best as we expect from nits some faculty doesn't provide proper lecture so that we understood better . also our attendance have a problem because of attendance problem we are not able to do extra curriculum activities. | [placements]: this is average in my branch because we do not have enough companies for our placements . also the faculty member do not focus on our placements but our placement representative work on it to improve placement in core companies . | [campus life]: as there are many friends in college and being endulge in extra curriculum activities i enjoy my college life most . i manage technical fest cultural fest and being event management head i also manage technical event and many more activities in college.
college_name: Dr B R Ambedkar National Institute of Technology Jalandhar

review_text: [college infrastructure]: college infrastructure is overall good. classrooms , libraries , seminar room etc. are well furnished and well maintained. hostel rooms are well spacious and proper ventilation is provided in the rooms. the food provided in hostel mess is hygienic but totally tasteless. | [academics]: i am pursuing my btech in electronics and communication engineering in the galgotias university. the overall academic is good . the good thing is that you can also select the elective subjects you want to read and teachers from second year onwards. | [placements]: for computer science and it students placements is good but for non cs branches hardly any core company visit the campus for placements. big it giants companies like infosys, cognizant, accenture, deloitte etc do bulk hiring and select hundreds of students each year. average salary offered is around 4 lakhs per annum. last year amazon select 2 computer science students at the highest package of 31 lakhs. around 60 percent students are placed by the end of year. | [campus life]: the best thing i like about the university is its campus life. there are students from various countries and states studying in the university which give you good exposure to culture, traditions of various places. there are numerous clubs in the university like dance, poetry, arts, techno club etc. in which you can take part. but only problem is its location . for buying anything you have to go 5-6 km.

[college infrastructure]: the jadavpur university has quite old buildings. but, it has a central library with separate libraries in each department. it has 4-5 canteens at different points in the campus. it also has an open air theatre, a gym, and a basketball court adjacent to the main ground. | [academics]: at masters level, jadavpur university has great academics. the professors are well qualified but some really find it difficult to teach due to lack of experience. masters in economics has very few options in terms of papers that can be selected and the way of teaching is also very old school. | [placements]: placements here are average. you get a package of 10 lpa if you are lucky enough. but, that too in a batch of 50-60 only around 10-11 get that because most of them are not eligible to sit for interviews as it is mandatory to pass in all papers and most of the students fail in 1 or 2 papers.

[college infrastructure]: yes my college has good infrastructure, facilities and equipments but they are of no use as tge teacher are not using them properly, and they expect from us to use tge resources properly the hostel is the worst thing in lpu | [academics]: i am studying btech cse and dont know why.. because they are giving placements to undeserved btech students and only startup companies are coming there. and also a lot of sales companies too. worst in placements. | [placements]: placements are very poor in her.. only if you are 10 pointer then only u can get good placements,else for average students it is null. and placement process is also worst.. tgere is no systematic way of placements.

[college infrastructure]: the infrastructure is very beautiful. it has spacious classrooms, labs, central library and a department dedicated library in all the academic blocks. it has specious gardens and sports ground including volleyball, basketball, cricket, badminton etc. it has all the extra curricular clubs like ninad music club, natraj dancing club, drishti photography club, chess club, art club, choti theatre club and a mooch more. it also has esteemed organization running by students i.e, entrepreneurship cell. | [academics]: it has e-co friendly environment between teachers and students. the way of teaching is very good. notes are updated. they help us in every aspect we need them. practicals help us to relate and understand industry work and gain experience. | [placements]: placements are awesome for computer science engineering students and also better for management pg and ug students. for other courses like biotechnology etc it is somehow better. year by year the administration is improving in it. | [campus life]: the campus is life, however is good. hostels are neat and tidy. food is good in comparison to other universities hostel. hostel warden and other teaching staff are very cooperative and we'll mannered.

[college infrastructure]: the college infrastructure is so good . the class rooms have the highly modern technology equipments . the college also has the hostel facility to the students with the mess facility too . it even have the medical facilities for the students . | [academics]: the teachers are good , but some are very strict with the academics . the examinations are little tough compared to the other educational institutions . but the quality of education is good , some teachers are really experts . | [placements]: the placements percentage of vit is also good comparatively . the students with the high gpa have the high percentage to get placed in the placements . the recruiters are from the top reputation helded companies also .

[college infrastructure]: talking about infrastructure, especially the smart classes and the apj hall are well maintained with smart boards but the block classrooms are just average as it doesn't have smart boards in it. including library, gym, sports room, canteen, laboratory everything is pretty good. although the campus is too big , as it takes a lot of time to go from one place to another.wifi accessibility is free for each and every student. their is staff available at every place inside the college for cleaning purpose, so it's neat and clean even inside the restrooms and the canteen. | [academics]: quality of teaching varies form proffessor to proffessor. specifically talking there are few teachers that are way too good in teaching. i am studying electronics and communication engeenering from nsut, because i do have interest in this field. i really do think that this field is getting more and more developed currently. through ece i can get job either in tech or core depending upon the skills that i have learned. but academic study is not sufficient enough to get me a job-ready. | [placements]: from the past placement offers, i can say that the quality of placement is surely getting improved as many new offers are being availed to students from variety of companies. it's very difficult to get placed through campus, so up to 20% students get the opportunity. average salary starts from 5lpa and goes up to 70lpa in ece department. | [campus life]: one of the best feature of college campus is that they organise many types of fest(eg.moksha) , and worst feature is that majorly exams get preponed, and event get cancelled (eg. resonance, freshers party)
college_name: Netaji Subhas University of Technology New Delhi

review_text: [college infrastructure]: college infrastructure is just amazing. when i first visited lpu i was awestruck. the campus is huge, lush green with a mall and a mini uni hospital of its own aswell.... which is superbbb. lpu has many food places as well. | [academics]: studies here are great. teachers are very supportive and support modern ideology. i love the atmosphere here. its always very positive and uplifting, which i feel is the best part here. i am sure if you are here because you want to accomplish your big dreams, then let me tell you, you will make it happen through your hard work and lpu's placement support. | [placements]: yes, i would say overall a good placement experience. many big industries do visit every year and students do get placed in one or another company. many of my friends got jobs in bangalore and hyderabad. | [campus life]: i had great time! it was a second home to me. i enjoyed my hostel life as well. tried food from all the food joints in lpu and i must say they are uncountable yet delicious. i remember, in winters we used to sit in the parks and enjoy the cool breeze.

[college infrastructure]: the college infrastructure is really good. girls hostel is also very hygienic. campus always stay neat and clean. girls hostel is very strict. girls have to enter hostel before 6 pm which isn't fair. guidelines are good. but strictness in girl's hostel is very high. | [academics]: i am studying 5 year dual degree bs ms mathematics and computing. for 1st year students, the subjects are wisely chosen. the classes are always on time. the faculty keep on updating their way of teaching in every few months. | [placements]: i am a first year student... i don't know about placements very well... but the average package is around 4 to 5 lakhs. there are students who get jobs worth 20 to 25 lakhs.. and also some who get package around 1 to 2 lakhs. | [campus life]: campus life is awesome with friends and a study environment. we can do many things at campus. we also get free time to do other things like drama, arts etc. college fest take place twice a year i.e every sem. the campus area is safe.. transportation is also good.

there are a variety of different courses and specifications and one can choose to also do different electives or minors alongside your major. there is a lot of crowd in both the chennai and vellore campuses. the actual faculty-to-student ratio might even be worse than your high school, as each class can have upto 70 students. the qualifications and teaching abilities of the faculty also varies greatly some are excellent the entrance exam is very easy when compared to jee or bitsat but as they do not tell you your marks obtained they are not transparent. the exams during the course can range from mildly easy to slightly hard all depending on your faculty and your efforts.there are two annual fests in both chennai and vellore campuses, one tech-based and the other entertainment based ( both are fun). the vellore campus has gravitas and rivera while the chennai one has technovit and vibrance. you can play most sports here but the facilities are not that good. there are loads of clubs to join and one can make their own clubs with faculty permissions.

[college infrastructure]: spread across 625 acres has all necessary facilities , campus wide internet facilities ( 5000 lan points and 90 wi-fi access points ), campus is ordered by tea gardens, natural beauty and peace and largest digital library in asia named after former president apj kalam | [academics]: quality of teaching is same as what a nit has and all faculties are phd degree holder, classes are held through projectors , has sport facilities ( cricket, football, basketball, badminton, kabaddi etc ) gymnasium is also there also internships are given to students 14 students got 6 month internship from college | [placements]: out of 77 eligible students in cse total 82 were placed (5 students with cgpa less than 6(1/2) were also placed , average package for cse was rs 8,70,000 / annum , for ece it was 6,38,000 / annum , highest placement offer was rs 33,00,000 per annum from arcasium india pvt ltd & amazon offered 28,00,000/annum average package for all branch was 6,29,000 / annum | [campus life]: best feature :- good placement , studies, teachers, college life & bad feature :- average infrastructure (being old nit)

[college infrastructure]: our college is having very good infrastructure. all classrooms, corridors, quadrangle are very well constructed. labs, libraries, canteens are very well equipped. wifi facility all around the college campus is so strong. | [academics]: academics wise our college is very best. all lecturers are very well experienced and resourceful. libraries are really having the vast collection and also we even have the e-book facility. i really admire my college co curriculum. | [placements]: placement facility in our college is very good. but for our mechanical branch it's quite little difficult. but for other batches yearly output is very much nice. alumnis and board members are very much supportive. | [campus life]: whole environment is peaceful. i really like it.

the civil department is very good in this college and faculty member are well and good traine faculty members are their way of teaching is very good and every student easily understand the things of the facult what they teach .life at the campus is very good so many extra calcular activities are happening like tarang ,snarachana ,technofest ,and sports fest ,in dj nights and some singer also come in the college for the performance.

campus life of dtu is very good the main fest is engifest which is attended by around 1.5 lakhs people each year. along this, there are other smaller but good events almost every month. the facilities like the library are available which has thousands of books, journals, magazines etc. dtu has lots of societies like tech teams dealing with racing cars , uav' etc which represents our college in both india and abroad. it also has a very big sports complex which is well maintained and has various courts like that of basketball, volleyball, badminton, kabaddi, running track, etc and a good m with all the equipment.i am in pursuing mechanical engineering i took this course because dtu's mechanical engineering is one of the best in india. here each class consist of 70 to75 students. the professors are very well qualified and student-friendly. with respect to exams, i can say that they are twice a semester which is mid-sem and end-sem exams and if you will not study then it will be difficult to get a good cgpa.

[college infrastructure]: the college boasts good infrastructure, including well-equipped labs, modern classrooms, a digital library, and decent hostel facilities.the college boasts good infrastructure, including well-equipped labs, modern classrooms, a digital library, and decent hostel facilities. the campus is wi-fi enabled, which helps students with research and academic projects. | [academics]: the teachers are very knowledgeable and provide a good quality of learning experience. they are very helpful in many extra curricular activities and provide good company for the college environment. the semester exams are good and we only need to study the questions from the last 8 years. the faculty will provide us with notes and question banks for our semester exams. the students are well prepared for the industries they will be entering after graduation. i really enjoyed my industrial trips to different industries. | [placements]: last year, 70% of my seniors were offered salaries of 20 lacs by on-campus recruiters. recruiters included microsoft, accenture, wipro, tcs, grape city, deltax, and many others. my course requires an internship, and most of are doing their internships at top companies.
college_name: Yeshwantrao Chavan College of Engineering Nagpur

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the infrastructure of academic block is quite good. all classroom have air conditioner with high speed wifi. there is a computer programming lab and two ece labs which is comprised of all necessary equipment. a library in which there are all types of books of computer science, electronics and research paper are also available from various prestigious institute. hostel is spacious & clean regularly.basketball, volleyball, badminton, table tennis courts are present. food served is hygienic & tasty | [academics]: computer science course is highly appreciated. all professor are phd holders & they have a vast experience in their relevant field like computer vision,data science,ai&ml,iot,etc. every year curriculum is updated according to market need. students can choose electives after 2nd year according to their desired sub domain. i think the course curriculum will definitely make the student be ready for high paid job. | [placements]: presently campus placement is not so good but it is increasing day by day. this year the highest package is 56 lakh ctc and many also got decent package ranging from 12 to 23 lakhs. average package is around 9 lakhs. it is far better than previous year. institute is trying to contact various reputed company for placement drive session. many students opts for higher studies like doing their ms from foreign prestigious university. also many students start their own venture as a entrepreneur. | [campus life]: due to new college,there is not too much campus life but you can definitely enjoy with your colleagues & seniors. presently it is located in a isolated area.but the city is developing day by day and all necessary market facilities are coming here itself. here all have friendly nature comprising of staffs, professor, students. it is totally safe place. security is also tight. for handling any type of emergency it is always ready.
college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology Sri City

review_text: pros/cons: great campus, very green and fresh. helps feelings refreshed very good sports and gym facilities helping to maintain your health good amount of events so the students can have fun no non veg in mess. the meals are not properly balanced nutritionally only 3 outpasses a month. college should not control the amount of out passes i want to taken uniform culture. it's a very old practice and should not be followed for adults in modern era. they expect us to act like adults when treating us like childrrn | placement experience: 5th sem we become eligible for college placements. the companies and other details i don't have much idea. percentage as well but i think it's around 60 percent of who get placed. offers are pretty low since the market is down

[college infrastructure]: yes, college has all the required, updated infrastructure with enough space to perform the activities. all the equipments are upgraded tike to time. classrooms have proper chairs and benches with occupancy of more than 60 students. there are various smart classes for visual learning classes a d auditoriums of various occupancy to conduct events and seminars. hostel has a great ambience maintaining all the required hygiene. food hygiene is given special concern and rarely there is any issue. | [academics]: i studied mechanical engineering according to my interest. the quality of teaching is above average where students are imbibed with enough theoretical as well as practical knowledge simultaneously. faculties are highly qualified mostly from iits and are good in communicating. the curriculum is updated as we used to follow fully flexible credit system, the same curriculum which is followed by some of the elite colleges around the globe. yes, the courses are at per industrial requirements. | [placements]: the quality of placement is excellent as almost all the students who sit for placement bag atleast 1 offer. majority are offered with more than 1 job. above 90 percent students are placed with the circuital branches bagging 100 percent placement. the highest package was 50 lpa offered to a cse student. the average package offered is 5.5 lpa. the placement process was a big hectic as 3 to 4 companies used to visit on daily basis despite that it was pleasant. yes, the college was very supportive | [campus life]: campus life is amazing. the best feature is the diversity of people there coming from all over the globe. the disadvantage is the number of students they take in. students can engage in all kind of activities they wish to participate like music, sports, organising events, debate, etc. students are very keen and busy learning and teachers usually support the students except ttwo or three. the support staffs are strict. the location is great and even agter being in outskirts, rarely problem occurs

[college infrastructure]: infrastructure of lpu is awesome everything is available on campus starting from hospital, mall, food court, wifi, smart classroom, labotaries are also very good. lpu has india's biggest central library where every book's are available. lpu also has many small playground and 1big play ground and every thing ia available there like basketball court , tennis court, etc.lpu has 8 boys hostel and 6 girls hostels and every hostel is great net and clean not only that all the facilities are good there. | [academics]: currently i am pursuing b.tech cse. i choosed cse as my carrer because in cse the future is bright and there are many jobs available. i choosed lpu because lpu provides quality of education like- syllabus are updated, faculties are we'll educated and higher educated also.lpu has many labs like-mac lab, sap lab. one great things is from the 1st year lpu provides communication and verbal classes for developing student's for placement's. | [placements]: placements of lpu is good. average packegs are good and higest packages are also good. around 500+ companies visit at lpu and give good package. above 90% students get placed in every year in 2019 higest package was 42 lakh from cse.

[college infrastructure]: the college has excellent infrastructure, which is ever improving onwards. the food is versatile, balanced, and tasty. we get lan connection in in hostel, nd free wifi in the library. the living spaces are very hygienic. | [academics]: the curriculum is very interesting. the teachers are very cooperative and helpful. if youre interested in oyur corresponding branch, you will find the curriculum extremely enjoyable. in any case, there are seniors having your back too. | [placements]: placements are top notch when it comes to nit rourkela. companies, both domestic and foreign come to nit rourkela for placements. the slaries are on par with most old iits , so it is very worth taking admissions into nit rourkela.

[college infrastructure]: yes our college has all the facilities and equipment.we have wifi,smart boards, projector in every classes.there is ac in each classes but they are not working.it was the disappoint.the classes are vey clean and neat.the atmosphere inside the college was very good.it is coverd with plants and trees.there is no allow of vehicles inside the college due to that there is no pollution inside the college.3 are there for the canteens are there for the students and the food and everything is avaliable. | [academics]: iam studying bachelor of technology (computer science).the teaching was very good and professor in our college have well experience in teaching.we didn't need to worry about the academic it was very good.every month so many projects, activities and sports are conducting. | [placements]: the placements was average.the students with arrears cannot attend the placements.the companies coming to our college and selecting students.mostly cse students have more placements opportunity than other groups students.last year 700 students were selected from cse braanch.mostly they were placed in multi national companies like accenture,wipro,cape gemini, cognizant and etc | [campus life]: the college campus was good.the best features are stadium, volleyball courts, basketball courts and shuttle courts.the students can engage in sports and other activities also the support staff are good co-operate with us.for every student the mentor is there they will take care of them.solves their problems.its located in avadi,tiruvallur,tamil nadu.it is safe beside there is flyover also no transport problem.if you have any health problem there is hospital inside the campus.they will take care.
college_name: Vel Tech Rangarajan Dr Sagunthala R and D Institute of Science and Technology Chennai

review_text: [college infrastructure]: college infrastructure is so damm good . there are different departments for different branches and courses . there are many labs just like physics lab , computer lab, language labs and many other for different purpose of work | [academics]: academics are also so good that makes us study in a happy environment . there are so many qualified teachers and too many professors of higher experience . all teachers pay us totally attention and make sure to teach us in their best possible way. | [placements]: placements are average as average package is of 4-5 lacks per annum . also some companies offer a higher package of 15+lacks. mass recruiting companies are about 85% and rest 15 % are top mncs which offers high package.
college_name: Chitkara University Institute of Engineering and Technology Jhansla

review_text: [college infrastructure]: although college infrastructure is not too good but as it is in a developing phase we can expect it attain a standard level soon in upcoming years,living space which include hostels are do well maintained but the number of student per room is exceptionally high which is 5 apart from this cleanliness and hygiene is 10/10 | [academics]: i am pursuing b.tech in computer science and am currently in the second semester, we talk about curriculum point of view it is very much decent but i guess our curriculum is not so updated ,and research facilities are very much satisfactory but not too good,and as usual it make you 60% job ready rest you have to do yourself | [placements]: although placement is decent but we have to compare with other nits ,it stand nowhere over all median salary is 8 lpa including all branched in cse it is 12 lpa highest on campus was from de shaw 35 lpa however only one student grabbed it | [campus life]: as it lags in infrastructure we face lot of issues in organising events ,if talk about location it is considered hostile from other state people point of view but that is a wrong intuiton of this college some issues do happen but those are very rare

[college infrastructure]: our college is the best ever college in our area. as we all know the nit always have its standard. the infrastructure of our college is so good and the facilities provided with both our college and hostels are amazing. the living space is so clean and the food is hygienic in canteens too. | [academics]: i have studied b.tech mechanical engineering because i loved mechanical from my childhood and studied hard and smart and secured good marks. quality of teaching is so good and the staffs working here are well experienced one. | [placements]: the quality of placements is awesome at our colleges. many placements cells are maintained for the students enrich in both soft skill and technical working. obviously 100% students were placed with the highest salary package of 35 lpa and the average salary package of 8 lpa.

[college infrastructure]: yes it has all facilities like computer lab,library etc and all equipments and having a good infrastructure. classrooms are well settled and contain wifi and smart board and always neat and clean........ | [academics]: i am pursueing btech from cse(computer science and engineering) only from this college because it has excellent placement in terms of this trade. yes our curriculum is always updated and study makes ready for job.... | [placements]: placement is excellent and every year 90% of the students gets placement . highest package is near about 35 lakh from amazon and average package is 3-10 lakh and yes college is supportive during interview and placement process is not so hard but moderate.... | [campus life]: campus is well structured. students can join any club like for coding, drama and sports. students and teachers are very supportive always keen to help us and it is in dehradun, mussorie road, yes , it is very safe location rather dehradun is very safe and yes it can handle emergencies....

[college infrastructure]: most classrooms in the new blocks are well equipped with blackboards, smartboards and good benches. that's where most of the classes for first and second years take place. the labs are good, but difficult to get into and use. library is not as big as in iits, but provides a large number of books on various subjects, alongside e-resources such as access to a large number of online journals and papers. the library also has a rich collection of old books that are well-maintained. | [academics]: i am studying my bachelors' in electronics and communication engineering. i feel more importance is being given to attendance, discipline, punctuality etc rather than polishing one in one's field of interest. i felt my academic knowledge was lacking compared to students in iits, nits and other reputed colleges in india. not all faculty are able to deliver the subject matter well. i found myself severely lacking in marketable skills at the end of my second year. | [placements]: placements have been always good in the college, owing to the rigorous training given by the placement cell of the college. training is offered in software and programming skills, as well as for core companies. apart from technical training, the placement cell also offers compulsory soft skill training, business english training, etc that prepares one for interviews, group discussion. the cell offers 100% placement guarantee, while following the policy of first confirmed single offer policy. | [campus life]: many laboratories, a good library, playgrounds, courts, gym are all available. the sports related areas are all on the hostel side of the college. one technical fest and one non-technical fest is conducted every year, in a sort of muted, disciplined manner. the clubs keep hosting small events all long the year. the global leaders' forum conducts a mun and tedx talk every year in the college. however, the peer group is a good influence, which is one of pros/cons: the campus is amazing and is very big, hostels and lecture halls surrounded by literal jungles. it also has amazing views from different locations of triund mountain range. the alumni network is very strong and the overall college culture between students of different years is amazing. it is a place where you grow up from a kid to a mature person. the campus is pretty neat and clean, it's hard to find litter on roads and ground. the campus also has most of facilities regarding sports. the college board is not much of a listener to a lot of problems and it's getting stricter day by day. the professors are not the best, you might find great professors at nit hamirpur but most of them are just the typical government job employees trying to pass time | placement experience: the students become eligible for placements when they start final year. on an average there's more than 100 companies that come for placements and they almost hire 90% of the batch. the highest package i have heard was 30lpa for software engineering role.

pros/cons: i loved the culture between seniors and junior interaction. also the professors professors and their work area and other interdisciplinary exposure research opportunities in core available at even bachelors level placements and companies in core is going to be limited for this branch the core companies pay scale is less the labs could be improved at international level in comparison | placement experience: in the third semester itself the students are eligible for internships and at the beginning of 4 year they apply this it for placements the placement committee circulates emails to the student for the different companies and their criteria and then the students can choose the companies write their test and sit for internship and placements

review_text: [college infrastructure]: lovely professional university is an institute with a wide and vibrant infrastructure. it is very big in size claimed to be 600 acres. it has one of the biggest libraries of north india which is open from morning 9am to 12am. it has all the required laboratories. there are classrooms in big number. it has hostels and apartments for residence. | [academics]: in academics, lpu does a very good job. it is a naac accredited university with a++ grade. its academic curriculum is based with ugc. it has all the facilities such as labs, benches, smartboards, projectors, etc. lpu focuses well on keeping its academic curriculum up to date. teachers are good and help students with doubts and guidance. | [placements]: placements are very good for all the programs in lpu. cse and business branches are the programs which get highest packages in placement drives. highest package ever taken by an lpu students is 3 crore, as advertised. aerospace branch also has a descent placement record. | [campus life]: lpu's campus is absolutely great with a variety of fun and leisure activities that student can have fun in. it has playgrounds, skating area, park, mall where students are always present to have fun. there are food courts where students can enjoy delicious food of different states of india. people of different cultures come together to learn and enjoy here. | [anything else]: one of the finest private institutions in india.

as the college is situated in bangalore there are many internship opportunities inside the college and also outside the college. there were many research projects also going inside the college in which we can take part.the placement is very good for upper branches like for cse ece ise branches for these branches average package is about 15lpa. almost 85-90% of students in each class get placement. i want to be placed in good company.

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the campus is amazing. the infrastructures are all well and working. there is lan port in hostel and free wifi in library. the whole campus is very clean and beautiful. everything is well-maintained. hygiene is maintained thoroughly. | [academics]: i'm studying in electronics and instrumentation engineering. it's a good branch. the industry is building very fast around it. our teachers are all very good at teaching. our curriculum is updated regularly to keep up with the developments happening in all fields. | [placements]: very good. great package is offered. hundreds of companies visit campus to offer several hundred placements. about 75-80% students were placed. the highest salary was ~40l for computer science engineering and the average salary is about 10l.

[college infrastructure]: infrastructure of the college is in good condition. classrooms contain smart board, projectors & are fully air conditioning. rooms & corridors are beautifully clean. but washrooms are not so clean everytime. sports centre is very big in size & also contain every essential indoor games.the canteen is economic & is rich in taste. laboratories for chemistry are not fully equipped essential chemicals. hostel & all premises contain wifi facilities. computer centre is new and have many computers, lapy | [academics]: i am studying master's in chemistry because i want to pursue higher education and go into research field in future. the teachers teach in good way. but some teachers do not take classes regularly. so, i am not fully satisfied with teaching. syllabus is same as in university of delhi but extra courses are included due to today's job demand as ipr, computer, green chemistry etc. so, the study is according to jobs sector point of view. | [placements]: placements are of generally b.tech and m. tech students. because chemistry students generally go for higher education rather than placements. but in this college due to being of engineering college/university placement is available for masters student as well. even my seniors got placed in reputed companies. also teacher provided internships at drdo during summer vacations. now, a portal has been made for all college students for placements. this is a great initiative. | [campus life]: campus life is great as campus is very beautiful and big. everyday there is some function either motivational, education, musical or other activities. many societies are present in college. the college is known for its fest. it contains many beautiful canteens. and also contain a caffe. library is beautifully designed in every manner. college contains many beautiful flowers. hostel timing for girls are 11:30 pm. so it is pretty good to wander in night in campus. campus have cute dogs & peackocks

[college infrastructure]: for the cse department, there is a separate building in iit kanpur where only cse students can sit. in the building, there are around 180 desktops with i5 processors and 12th generation, and the labs are fabulous for my department. the quality of hostels is good, but only in the first year. after the first year, you will find that hall 12 is good; other halls are not, and in hall 12, you will find a single room after the first year but not others. but the quality of food after 1st is average and tested also. | [academics]: most teachers are helpful, but not all teachers, although they are well qualified and knowledgeable, and the teaching quality is excellent , but yes, only a few teachers. this course curriculum is relevant, and it makes the students industry-ready. the semester exams are literally tough, not easy. if you are at iit kanpur, then you have to study in any case. | [placements]: in my department, the placements are very good, with around 95 to 98% of students placed, and the average package is around 40 lpa, with the highest being 3.2 crore, which is international. the top recruiting companies are texas instruments, optiver, quadeye, rubrik, tower research, google, microsoft, etc. internships are also excellent; around 90% of students get an internship every year, and the same companies come for internships as i have written down above. the top roles are hft and sde.

review_text: [college infrastructure]: college infrastructure offers state-of-the-art facilities that include modern classrooms, well-equipped laboratories, a vast library, advanced computer centers, and excellent sports and recreational amenities. the campus provides a vibrant and conducive environment for both academic and personal growth. | [academics]: institution maintains a strong focus on quality education. the curriculum is regularly updated to match industry standards, and the faculty is experienced and supportive. students benefit from interactive learning methods, workshops, seminars, and practical exposure. | [placements]: placements are decent, with a fair number of students securing jobs through campus recruitment drives. while top-tier companies may visit less frequently, there are still opportunities for placements in reputed organizations, particularly for high-performing students. the placement cell is active and helps students with training and interview preparation.
college_name: Yeshwantrao Chavan College of Engineering Nagpur

review_text: [college infrastructure]: every academic block is well-maintained, and labs are equipped with the latest instruments. the computer labs are updated with new systems and fast internet. the library is large, quiet, and filled with plenty of resources for study and research. hostel facilities are comfortable and safe, with proper food and wi-fi for students. sports grounds and cultural spaces keep the environment balanced between academics and activities. | [academics]: maximum professors are knowledgeable and helpful. they focus on making sure students grasp concepts properly rather than just rushing through the syllabus. faculty members often guide students through project work, hackathons, and research opportunities. they also give advice on higher studies and career choices, both in india and abroad. guest lectures, industry visits, and workshops are organized frequently to provide practical knowledge. | [placements]: placements are one of its biggest strengths of my college. top recruiters include amazon, microsoft, infosys, tcs, hcl, and aditya birla group. the average package is around ?6 lpa, while the highest offers cross ?20 lpa for cse and it students. core companies also hire from civil, mechanical, and chemical branches with decent packages. the training provided by the placement cell helps students improve communication, aptitude, and coding skills.

[college infrastructure]: good campus. could have been better if more greenery was here. due to construction of the new building there's dust all over the campus which does not feel good. otherwise the campus is fine . good wifi throughout the campus. | [academics]: the course is good. since in first year not a great idea about the course of bio tech but first year curriculum is good. teachers are experienced and teach very well. yes the curriculum is up to date . | [placements]: the overall placements are good in pes. recently eleven students backed a package of more than 40 lakh per annum mostly computer science students. the average package in the university is between five to eight lakhs.

[college infrastructure]: srm has a well defined layout in terms of infrastructure. the campus is spread over a large area, with the provision of all the basic facilities. there are huge grounds and indoor courts for sports; gymnasium is made for the students of the college. the library is technically advanced and has a good collection of books. the classrooms are well-maintained with smart boards installed, and the washrooms are clean. canteen has diverse varieties of food which are hygienic and affordable. | [academics]: cse has proven to be a good course for me as it aligns with my interests and liking for computers and programming. the course is well updated, and even the curricula followed in the college fall in line with the skills required in the job sector. the course has a lot of research and practicality which definitely makes one job-ready. the teachers are cooperative and experienced, and they mentor each student at each and every point. | [placements]: approximately, 70 % of the students get hired in the recruitment drive held by the college. the placement drive is held in the most efficient manner. the average package is around 5 lpa which is a good kick start for any student in their respective fields. the college has a placement cell which is responsible for inviting firms to the college and guiding the fellow mates in getting placed,hence making the college supportive. the placements must be inclusive of all the courses,which is a demerit. | [campus life]: though the college is isolated from the main part of the city, but it is glorious from the inside. there are sports activities in the college and many cultural societies like dance and music societies. moreover, there are various clubs which keep on participating in educational competitions. various seminars are held and several fests are conducted throughout the year making the college life happening. there's a medical room to handle any emergency, as and when it arises!
college_name: SRM Institute of Science and Technology Chennai

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the college infrastructure is excellent, boasting lush greenery, ample space, well-equipped labs, air-conditioned classrooms, a large sports playground, on-campus hostel facilities, and access to medical services. additionally, the canteen offers good-quality food at a lower price than the market. | [academics]: i am pursuing electrical engineering due to my childhood interest in this field. nirma university offers a minor degree, allowing the pursuit of a dual degree. the teaching quality is excellent, and the 85% attendance criteria ensure active class participation. the curriculum is regularly updated, and student feedback is collected at the end of each semester. it is designed to prepare students for interviews with relevant companies. | [placements]: our college provides extensive placement opportunities, with nearly 90% of students securing placements through campus drives. in the computer branch, the highest package reaches around 80 to 90 lakhs per annum, while the average package is 8 to 10 lakhs per annum. other branches also see an average package of 7 lakhs per annum offered by companies. the placement process is straightforward, and the placement cell provides robust support. | [campus life]: the college campus excels in both curriculum and non-curriculum activities. it hosts technical and non-technical clubs, financially supported by the university. in technical clubs, students engage in various competitions like robocon, electric vehicle manufacturing, drone competitions, underwater vehicle manufacturing, etc. with faculty support, they participate in competitions worldwide. the university's highway proximity ensures easy vehicle access.
college_name: Institute of Technology Nirma University Ahmedabad

review_text: [college infrastructure]: saveetha engineering college college was a prestigious institution with a rich history, but its infrastructure was in dire need of an upgrade. the buildings were old, the classrooms were cramped, and the facilities were outdated. | [academics]: saveetha university is a renowned institution in india, known for its academic excellence and innovative approach to education. here's an overview of the academics at saveetha they are trained the students up to the current trend technology | [placements]: saveetha university has an impressive placement record, with many top companies recruiting its students. here are some details about the placement at saveetha: prepare for the recruitment process. good placement | [campus life]: good

[college infrastructure]: facilities and infrastructure available for our courses are well-equipped laboratories and fully wi-fi surrounded classrooms with all the latest technology. the campus and hostel are connected to high-speed wi-fi. asia's biggest library and auditorium are available. the hostel is very good. | [academics]: currently i m learning btech in computer science with specialization in artificial intelligence and machine learning, and the quality of study here is too good. yes, i think our college is updated and includes all the recent developments in the field | [placements]: the institute has successfully concluded its 2023 placement drive with more than 734 recruiters, who made around 7305 offers. srm university highest package stood at inr 57 lpa and the srm ist average package stood at inr 7.58 lpa. | [campus life]: campus life at college can be a vibrant and enriching experience. it's a time when students often engage in a wide range of activities beyond their academic studies.
college_name: SRM Institute of Science and Technology Chennai

review_text: [college infrastructure]: my campus's infrastructure is really good and there are also building new buildings and hostels and developing campus. srm campus is also quite big and have many kind of amenities provided by them for students. we has hospitals, shopping complex, cafes and many fastfood chains and a big sports ground and many courts, etc. classrooms are quite well maintained and have all equipments available in labs and sports. we have so many hostel which occupy most of the people and they are building. mess food is also ok ok. | [academics]: i am doing btech cse with specialisation in ai&ml. i always wanted to learn coding and now is the future of ai that's why i chose this course. my teachers are well educated and carry great knowledge but sometime i face problem in class because of my teacher's accent and little bit of language barrier. our curriculum is up to date with the industries and our field. we are persuade to do coding according to what market demands. | [placements]: i don't know much about placement but as i know from my seniors that so many mncs come for giving placement but there is one system of cgpa which divide students into different category of packages and companies. so if you are serious about placement then you also have to pay attention for cgpa regardless of your skills. | [campus life]: you will experience a great campus life and make memories there and get a chance to meet people from different region of the country and will make great connections. | [anything else]: i you are seeing too much about money then you will experience that its an expensive college not because of its fees but because of cost of hostels and living outside. you will easily spend 3 lac per year easily just for stay and food.
college_name: SRM Institute of Science and Technology Chennai

review_text: [college infrastructure]: this is the best college of ghaziabad for a reason because they provide world class facilities and environment to the students and everything is just perfect for the students and they do not feel bad for anything. | [academics]: all the faculties and outside lecturer are quite good and all their programs are very organised and they always tries to bring best out of the students which gives positive attitude towards students and it's good. | [placements]: i am happy with my placement they provided me campus placement with the tata group and they offered a very good package of 15 lakh per annum and will me increase after a period of time. totally worth it.
college_name: Ajay Kumar Garg Engineering College Ghaziabad

review_text: i chose this course because i had a keen interest in both mathematics and computer science. i was more interested in learning the mathematical foundations of computer science, rather than just the application of it. faculty: student ratio is very low. there are generally more than 100 students in a usual class. faculty members are highly qualified and have done their ph.d. and post docs from renowned indian and international universities. the teaching method is basically going through slides in subjects other than maths and for maths, they write on the projectors and boards. there is 1 minor and 1 major evaluation. in some semesters, there can be 2 minors and 1 major, it is announced beforehand. exams are very difficult to score and it is quite hard to maintain a decent gpa here.annual cultural fest - rendezvous tech fest - tryst there are more than 6000 books and more than 100000 journals in the library of iit delhi and books can be made available in 1 monthly period on demand. the lecture hall complex is very big and all the classes are held there. there are lifts, washrooms, and drinking water on each floor. also, all the rooms have centralized ac. there are big sports grounds. there is a separate court for each sport and a newly built mittal sports complex for indoor games as well. all the clubs are maintained by students. in some big clubs like edc, sinc, robotics, etc., a professor is also assigned to supervision. other clubs are facc, pfc, devclub, ancc, dance, music, drama, etc. to conclude, there is something for everybody.
college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]

review_text: the course curriculum is quite different from other colleges. we can select all subjects to be learned each semester. here the credit system is there. so we have to complete at least 180 credits. the system is called a fully flexible credit system. there are courses that are core and electives. core subjects should be compulsorily completed. there are mainly two internals and one final exam. besides, assignments and quizzes will be there. projects are there for selected coursesfor getting admission in vit first we have to write the vitee entrance exam conducted by vit. as eligibility, you should score at least 75% in the board exam. according to the rank that u have got they will allow you in any one slot out of the 5 slots according to that we can select the courses.

[college infrastructure]: the college boasts a well-structured campus with excellent infrastructure. the classrooms are spacious and equipped with the latest teaching aids. the labs are well-maintained, supporting hands-on learning. the library is extensive and well-organized. the sports facilities are commendable. | [academics]: this college offers a robust academic program with a focus on intensive learning. professors are supportive and provide helpful feedback, but the workload is demanding and requires dedicated efforts from students. | [placements]: the placement cell was very supportive and efficient. with numerous career fairs and personalized counseling, students had ample opportunities to showcase their skills and secure positions for their students. | [campus life]: engaging campus atmosphere with various clubs and activities to make the most of college.

the fee for the whole 4-year course is nearly 10 lacs. the yearly fees would be around 2.25-2.50 lacs. students can avail of scholarships through their merit and eligibility criteria. the students are eligible for placements after the 6th semester. almost 90% of students get placed.moksha is the largest fest that is the key highlight of the college. the college is well equipped with sports facilities including indoor as well as outdoor sports. the college also has a gym facility, and a library with almost all kinds of required books available.

[college infrastructure]: the college campus is a fully wifi facility for the students . and with the well equipped modern laboratory with the best laboratory sources . the food is good quality and have the herbal garden with the good environment . | [academics]: academic faculty and teachers are good. they cover the syllabus on time but the semester duration is shorter as compared to the subjects. the first semester ends in three months but the content is to much. they take all tests and exams at once they are supportive. they interact with students | [placements]: about 70 percentage of the students from our course got placed in many top companies with the highest annual salary package like 18 lpa . the college has a training and placement cell for the students . | [campus life]: campus life is amazing | [anything else]: nothing else

[college infrastructure]: dru is one of the largest campuses in the country. it has a huge sports ground and all sports facilities. it has ac equipped classrooms and newly made sps halls to have classes they also have beautiful dtu hostels dtu library is one of the biggest libraries in the country. it has books on all possible topics. and all the facilities are well maintained | [academics]: academics in not that good if you want to learn most of the stuff you have to do own your own. teaching quality is okay but it could be much better most of the teachers are researchers themselves and are less bothered about students. curriculum is often backwards and most of the learning happens in labs | [placements]: placements are good very well known companies come here from all corners of the world. almost all students get placed with good packages. not only that many students get into very reputed universities abroad. average package is about 8-12 lpa | [campus life]: college campus is really big it has many beautiful places to see. students can engage in various activities like sports, literature, drama, poetry, projects and so much more. teachers howe8are not very supportive and are only indulged in their own research | [anything else]: they should add more rigorous and more uodated courses like machine learning and ai for all branches to make placement easy for them and teachers should realise that students are not experts and might need their help.

pros/cons: it has one of the most beautiful campus and it it is surrounded by greenery all around. there is 95% placement in mechanical engineering which is my department. we have grand fests like hillfair which is cultural fest and nimbus which is technical . we have various cultural and technical clubs and societies which we can join according to our interest and we can join it by giving interviews taken by our seniors.apart for them we have sports club ,gym with various equipments hostel food is good ,we get all the basic nutrients required in hostel and four meels is there. apart from them we have good food shops in campus like verka , amul ,pooja shopping centre ,food court ,food plaza there is a lot of restrictions to girls . intiming is 6:30 for the first year and 9:30 for the rest . and no outings are allowed at the initial period in campus there is road connectivity issue as airport as well as railway station are very from college and there is very small market nearby many people here fall in drug abuse and even the professors are very strict and not student friendly | placement experience: placement rate is good in mechanical engineering which is almost 90 percent and average package is 6-8 lakh . from seventh semester students become eligible for college placement. and many good companies visit like jsw ,adani ,realiance and many more. highest package is 1 crore but it was in it sector. after getting degree i will opt for placements

life is really good here at lpu, social life is really amazing you can know people of different cultures and states. the library consists of books in large amounts, you can issue them whenever needed. there is a huge craze for sports among students.as for the faculty members, the teachers are excellent and well-qualified. teachers are very friendly and you can ask your doubts whenever and wherever needed, teachers are always ready to help. teaching methods are excellent.

the social life is kind of chilled and kind of hectic as well. the college is good and big. the library is too good. the classroom is large and equipped. the sports and extra circular activities on the campus are a bit good and social groups for teaching poor students are therei chose btech since no other option for me. here around 200 students in chemical, mechanical, civil, electronics, electrical and computer science engineering. the faculties are graduated and mtech in a particular subject is done by them

the course structure of the whole b. tech is so perfect and is designed accordingly to industrial requirements. no unnecessary things are in the course structure. the faculties are highly trained and educated along with that they are also too supportive. two exams per semester (mid sem and end sem)campus life is quiet. in a general perspective, the campus doesn't immunes it's the surrounding environment. cultural, technical, and many fests are organized to keep students active in co-curricular activities. the library is available inside the institute premises

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the college has a great infrastructure, with a well-maintained campus and a variety of facilities. the classrooms are spacious and equipped with projectors and proper seating, and the labs have up-to-date systems and equipment for hands-on learning. the library is extensive, offering a large collection of books and online resources. there are also excellent sports facilities, including courts for various games and a gym. | [academics]: i am currently studying computer science and engineering. the quality of teaching is good, with most professors being knowledgeable and supportive. they often use real-world examples and encourage class discussions to make the material more engaging. i believe the curriculum is up-to-date and incorporates recent developments in the field. the courses provide a strong foundation in theory, and we are encouraged to work on practical projects and case studies. this focus on both theoretical and practical knowledge helps to make the curriculum relevant and prepares students to be job-ready after graduation | [placements]: the college's placement cell, in collaboration with faculty, conducts mock interviews, aptitude training, and resume workshops to prepare students for the job market. students often become eligible for placements from the 7th semester. the training starts as early as the campus is well-maintained, with good facilities like a library, cafeteria, and sports ground. the location is safe, and the management handles emergencies efficiently. overall, it?s a good place to learn and grow. | [anything else]: overall, it's been a wonderful journey filled with learning, fun, and memorable experiences.

exam consists of questions from mathematics physics chemistry and english general which are answered by using skills and here in college the exam has two parts one for lab and other is theory. slot based question paper and difficulty level is based on slot and faculty rivera. this is a 4 day fest and biggest event in the college and with that many cultural fest and event take place and gravitas. sahiti. many more like this singing dance club fest are held and thousands of people take part and sports events are held here

[college infrastructure]: the infrastructure of the campus is well-built labs equipped with the latest technology, libraries with massive up-to-date collections, big classrooms equipped with projectors, a well-built auditorium and a sports complex. the hostel facilities provided by the campus are top-notch. the hostels have been divided into blocks, with ug students being assigned e block, u block and s block. the rooms are well-equipped with beds, fans, cupboards, and light. the rooms in e block are twin-sharing, while single occupancy rooms are available in u & s block. | [academics]: most of the faculty are reachable and hill. they put more stress on learning the subject than getting good grades. most of the faculty members are from reputed universities across the world. the subject is taught by experts in the respective fields in the country. some courses are too easy for us with basic and memory-based questions in the exams which can be improved. the frequency of the exams depends on the course instructor but typically we have an exam every two weeks for every subject on average. | [placements]: the campus has a well-functioning placement cell that assists students. students are eligible to sit for placements in the last semesters, given that they have completed the required number of credits. however, a unique point about the institute is that since this is a research-oriented institute, most students prefer to go for higher studies. last year's data shows that only 6 students sat for placements, out of which 5 were placed with an average package of 15 lpa. the remaining vast majority of students went down two ways: either continuing to do their masters at iisc itself or going for phd in their field. | [anything else]: iisc is best for research.

[college infrastructure]: they claim that they have world-class facilities is also fake. hostellers are made to pay 1.5 lpa, and in the end, we had to pay extra for laundry, water, electricity, etc. buses are the dirtiest and not clean, and even the bathrooms are very dirty. mess food also is pathetic. no sports and games are available at all. | [academics]: teachers here are the worst. previously we had good teachers but currently, they have appointed random teachers with very limited knowledge. the course curriculum is not relevant. the teachers teach what they're in the mood for and conduct the exam on that and give everyone a 10 cgpa. | [placements]: let me let you all know that the claims made and all the positive reviews written by students are fake and were forced by the college in return for marks. that itself talks a lot about the college. the placements are the worst. bad companies come with bad offers. a lot of students were made to stand in petrol stations and traffic signals with placards. such horrible jobs were offered. | [campus life]: the college campus is best for students and teachers are very supportive to students in making the events and activities in college surrounding the college | [anything else]: no

[college infrastructure]: a strict and hygienic college. yes, a good atmosphere. all good facilities are available here. no free wifi for mobile phones. all servents are good in cleaning and they were often clean our college in a good manners. | [academics]: in our future electronics and computer science place a major role.yes some recent developments are applied. in campus interview we definitely find some good company offers. we have placed in a good company through our college. | [placements]: good and well qualified companies are taking part for our campus interviews.out of 100, maximum 40 or 50 may be selected. may be 6 lakhs per year for higher company. 7000 is average salary. its an easy process but it will be so fearful to face that. sometimes my college staff supports us. | [campus life]: sometimes its best and sometimes its worth. workshop events are handled a lot. sudents are always in a unsupportive manner and they are unfriendly to each other. some staffs being friendly and some are rude. and they often scold us a lot in different way.

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the collage infrastructure is really good enough. the collage has library and classrooms with air conditioner in it. the canteen of our collage is really good they have lot of food options available. but they only accept upi. | [academics]: the academics of our collage is really good enough. almost all the teachers are phd holders. the teachers also provide notes of every topic they teach in class. and the teacher is also really friendly with the students. | [placements]: the placement is really good of our collage. top campanies visite our collage campus to recruit the students. last year the average placement is 11 lpa and the heighest placement of our collage is 67 lpa.
college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology Noida

review_text: there is annual sports fest held in our college called 'spardha'. its india's largest sports fest. there is a cultural fest called kashiyatra, in which there are participants from all over the country. and then there is our techno-management fest called technex which is asia's third largest techno-management fest. apart from this there are some other small fests also like fmc weekend, vista, cult weekend, etc. apart from this small inter hostel sports matches are held. the faculty of our college is also good. all the professors are p. hd from one or the other universities including some renowned universities of usa, uk and germany. they teach very well. they are resarch oriented. they have very good industry experience and also aim to make their students expert in their fields. they work on projects with the students. the projects are based on the real life industry problems. it gives exposure to students.
college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology

review_text: [college infrastructure]: every block and classroom is well endowed with wi-fi, and every subject that needs practical experience has its own lab, which is a really good thing. hostels are also good and budget-friendly. for example, the non-ac hostel price is 95000 per year, whereas the ac hostel will cost you 190000 per year. | [academics]: the teaching quality is at its best here. teachers pay strict attention to their studies and are also very strict about exams. the course curriculum is a little bit lengthy but in the long run, it is very beneficial. side by side with study, they also prepare you for the corporate world with your communication skills and all. semester exams are quite difficult as there are very strict norms for examination. most of the students pass with good marks, like on average, 70?80%. | [placements]: we have not personally experienced the placement procedure, however, we have learned that the highest package offered for our course was 18 lpa. this package, although not considered very high according to current trends and the industry average, has been shared with us by our seniors who recommend exploring off-campus opportunities. | [anything else]: good college in noida.

[college infrastructure]: infrastructure of the college is very big and attractive. all the necessary facilities and equipments like smart boards, lights, fans, seats, ac, projector etc are available for the students. living spaces is clean and safe also food is hygienic. | [academics]: the quality of teaching is very nice because staff are highly experienced and qualified. they help students to grow up their skills and build their career. curriculum is updated and also it includes all the recent developments in the field. | [placements]: highest salary package was offered by 48 lacs per annum and the average salary package was offered 4 lacs per annum. placement process is simple and pleasant. faculty are supportive and helpful in the study and also in other activities. | [campus life]: location of the college is safe and beautiful also it can handle all the emergencies. many activities are conducted for the students to grow their skills and their confidence.
college_name: International Institute of Information Technology Bangalore

review_text: [college infrastructure]: our college infrastructure is very good. we have well equipped ac classes with every classes having smart board.we have a central library in a main block and other block libraries at other blocks and we have a type of mall known as gd subway and have a main canteen. | [academics]: i am currently pursuing bchelor of technology in computer science and engineering specialization in aiml by ibm . we have a number of experienced teachers and staffs .majority of them are the ex proffessors of iits and nits. | [placements]: placements from the past 5 to 10 years,gla had a very good placements record .the average package from here was 6 lpa-7 lpa. majority of the companies are cognizant, vipro, tcs, accenture, etc visit the campus every year.

[college infrastructure]: amity university noida campus is a remarkable example of great infrastructure. one can find everything on campus premises, from eating joints such as subway and domino's to great wifi coverage on the whole campus grounds. for hostlers there is even a utility store wh smith where one can find all the basic amenities. the central library is an epitome of excellence. hygiene is one of amity's major concern and they focus on providing a clean and well maintained environment for the students. | [academics]: i am a student of the english department at amity university noida which goes by the name, amity institute of english studies and research(aiesr), over my time of 3 years at this department never once have i felt the need of having more as the teachers are extremely helpful and have great knowledge about their subjects. the curriculum is very intriguing and is updated according to the latest guidelines. for 6 semesters the students will always have exposure to new genres. | [placements]: placement at amity university are satisfactory and it mostly depends on the course as well as on the department you are studying in. each and every department has a placement cell which brings organisations and companies in for the students and guide them throughout the whole act of placement. the stipend offered is largely dependent on the company's policy but amity's placement rate has been consistently good over the past few years. | [campus life]: amity takes a high interest in the activities outside academics and encourages the students to do the same. there is sangathan, an annual sports event and an ayf (amity youth fest) .
