review_text: [college infrastructure]: there are ample amount of facilities available in the college and the hostel. we have a gym, a pool table, a table tennis table and a badminton and basketball court available. the ac is a big problem as it gets a bit too hot in summers here and most of the ac's don't work. | [academics]: i'm in first year and pursuing b.tech in computer science and the curriculum for b.tech is quite good here, there are almost weekly mcq quizes here to help the student realise how is his performance. the teachers are also very supportive. | [placements]: the placements here have been good and the best part is that it is becoming better every year and the college also puts in a great effort to bring good companies here. the highest package this year was approximately 31 lpa.

the fee structure is expensive but if the student is good in studies then the college will provide scholarships to them. college accepts payment through net banking, actually, i don't know about the offline payment method. fees structure is divided into two parts : 1) tuition fee and examination fees. 2) hostel and mess fees.for taking admission into the iitb first we have to give jee mains exam which is held for 12th science student after qualifying to jee main students are eligible for jee advanced and based on that ranking will decide. and based on the ranks jose which is a council for the iit admissions will allot the iit over the 19 iits.
college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB]

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the college infrastructure is good but old , the old hostel buildings are not so good in terms of infrastructure , but the new hostels which are built are very nice , the classrooms are okay , but this year acs and smart boards are being installed so that is a good point. | [academics]: the quality of academics at iit varanasi is good , the professors here are well qualified , the course curriculum is also good in my opinion , the course structure is also good and from this year all the branches courses are converted to 4yr. | [placements]: the placement is very good at iit bhu comparing to other iit s , also the average package at iit bhu is around the 22.8 lakhs per annum for this year , so this is very good . also around 1200-1300 students got placed this year which is a very good number
college_name: Indian Institute of Technology Banaras Hindu University Varanasi

review_text: [college infrastructure]: you have hundreds of complaints about the university but inspite of all,this is worth it. some faculties are just in name teachers, but some are brilliant. campus is awesome. freedom and accountability go hand in hand. | [academics]: i am currently studying ba (or your course) at this college. the overall quality of teaching is average. some faculty members are helpful and explain topics well, but others rely too much on old methods and do not engage students effectively. the curriculum is mostly theoretical and not fully updated with recent developments in the field. although a few subjects are relevant, the course as a whole does not feel very job-oriented. more practical exposure, updated materials, and interactive teaching would greatly improve the academic experience. | [placements]: the placement situation in the college is quite disappointing. very few companies visit the campus, and most of them offer low salary packages that are not suitable for the effort students put into their studies. there is no proper guidance or training for interviews, and the placement cell is not active throughout the year. students mostly have to search for opportunities on their own. overall, the college needs to improve industry connections and provide better support if it wants to offer meaningful placement opportunities. | [campus life]: campus life here is one of the strongest aspects of the college. students enjoy a free and open atmosphere where debate, discussion, and critical thinking are encouraged. the campus is often politically vibrant, with students actively engaging in social issues and progressive conversations. it feels like a space where diverse opinions can be expressed safely and respectfully. various clubs, cultural events, and student-led initiatives create an energetic environment that supports intellectual growth and personal development. | [anything else]: yes. worth it. you will gain a lot. so worth it that there is a saying that one can enter jadavpur by choice but can't get out of it. the horrendous campus and college life will haunt in all through your life, in a good way.

[college infrastructure]: there are all necessary facilities on the campus with a little bit of bad maintenance. airy and fairly ventilated classrooms, equipped laboratories libraries filled with books of all the courses offered by the university. | [academics]: i am studying btech mechanical engineering. faculty in the school of mechanical engineering is skilled and knowledgeable. i think the curriculum is updated because we are being taught all the latest technologies in the market. | [placements]: as per the experiences shared by my seniors, the university offers good placements. not sure about the percentage of students placed but last year around 2500 students got placement with maximum package of 8 lakh per annum. | [campus life]: quality people and quality environment on the campus.

[college infrastructure]: srm has wonderful infrastructure with well designed buildings and good facilities.the classroms are modern and spacious with projector screens.thev laboratories have the latest equipment.the library is large and can accomodate a large number of students.it is central air conditioned and has a wide range of books.food is fine and living spaces are average.the facilities are maintained well | [academics]: i'm studying ece with a specialization in instrumentation engineering and i find the quality of teaching to be good.the curriculum is updated on consultation with industry experts and includes all recent developments in the field.the course does make us job-ready. | [placements]: placements are excellent.many top companies visit the campus for placements.the college has a high placement record and a good number of core companies visit too.avg salary is 4lpa in my department.the process is conducted smoothly and adequate training is given. | [campus life]: campus life is awesome.there are various clubs students can enroll themselves into based on their interests.the campus is large and has several eateries serving a wide range of foods.we also have a in-campus subway(restaurant) the location is safe and can handle emergencies.its well connected to interior chennai.
college_name: SRM Institute of Science and Technology Chennai

review_text: [college infrastructure]: all the departments are well equipped. moreover the infrastructure is been re-created , renovation is under going and soon every classroom will be smart classroom soon. as well as canteens are concerned, they are good too. | [academics]: being an mtech student, you can't demand so much of guidance for academics, still teachers are very much concerned about it and they give their full potential to take classes and finish the syllabus. syllabus was quite updated. and library is really good, probably the best place in campus with all new edition books. | [placements]: since , nit jamshedpur is one of the oldest colleges in the city, placements are really good. almost all students of my batch got placed. the best thing is that you are going to get ample chances to prove yourself there , now its upto you , whether you can make best out of it or not. | [campus life]: campus is huge enough to make you lost into the place. its soothing and relaxing in evening time. you can really get into evening walks, cycling. there is a gym area, basketball court,badminton court as well and grounds. for sure you won't be disappointed with campus ever.

i choose applied electronics and instrumentation because it has both chances to get a job in core or it. in the gate also most of the chance you will get better rank because in the gate students are less in this branch all over the country. faculties are awesome they know everything they are specialized in their subject.annual fests are there in college but since covid times the college has not done any fests and only technical fest is only happening. our library is one of the best places in the college library is fully air-conditioned and there is all kind of books available there which you want they will give you.

computer science is the stream that is in the boom in today's time. the faculty to students ratio is 100:1. all the teachers are minimum phd which is why all the teachers are very knowledgeable. term exams were taken almost every 1.5 months and it was difficult to pass at times.college life is pretty good, the fest is done from time to time but only when the semester is of full size. they are conducted in month of september. classrooms are spacious and ac work just fine. extra curicular are there sports like cricket and football and many more.
college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]

review_text: [college infrastructure]: yes our college infrastructure is great.it has almost all the necessary facilities such as library,wifi, parking,smart board, proper desk,water purifier,clean canteen .the campus is very clean and beautiful and full of trees and well organised garden. | [academics]: i'm pursuing my bachelor's of technology hons. because i want to build my career in software background and i'm very interested in growth of technology development.the quality of teaching is very nice and the teachers are so supportive.yes my study makes me familiar to the commerce background which is very important. | [placements]: placements are so good and the average package offered by the college is the best as compared to any other college of ranchi. many students were placed in the mncs like tata and accenture, cognizant,byjus ,hdfc, icici banks and the highest salary offered was 24 lpa .

pros/cons: infrastructure is best as compared to other collage the collageg is new but it have it,s own campus enviroment is so good fresh air clean everywhere the collage doesnot have basic audiotorium . only 13 girls over 280 boys which is worst for a collage management doest not support the students at all | course curriculum overview: iiituna curriculum is industry relevent with good practical expouser more hand on project based learning and intreactive teaching can improve the curriculum more and make it more good for upcoming students
college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology - [IIIT], Una

review_text: [college infrastructure]: yes very good in college .. iam very happy to spend time in my college sastra.. my college food is very hygienic and very tasty healthy.. sastra life is very happy to spend in my college.. vey very happy .. | [academics]: quality of teaching is very good .. yen my college curriculum is uldated everywhere.. amd the personality development class iv very good and very smart.. iit is very special class on sastra campus.. . | [placements]: quality of placement is very good when compared to other colleges of kumbakonam .. sastra is very best for placement and other activities.. many culturals can conduct. lab facility are very good and perfecr.. | [campus life]: campus life is very good and very beat. but some rules and regulation are maintained so students does not like the campus.. our campus is very good . yes very safe.. other college students are not come in my college
college_name: Shanmugha Arts Science Technology Research and Academy Thanjavur

review_text: pros/cons: the campus encourages one to think differently, consider experimenting, and develop new perspectives. jadavpur university is an institution of eminence that excels in the academic world-- from stem to humanities. the educators have a wonderful approach towards their students and relationships and built for a lifetime. lack of campus security that invites extreme situations. trespassers misuse the space. extreme political activities at times that disrupt the campus environment. lack of infrastructure in places and no hygience. | placement experience: students become eligible for camous placemens from the third semester onwards. companies that have visited the departmet of comparative literature witth campus placements offers are svf, hoichoi, radio mirchi, papertrue, learningmate, among others. highest package offered by companies is 8.5 lpa percentage of students getting placements is 85% my plans after getting my ma degree is to go for a phd.

[college infrastructure]: could be better. hostels are old. cleanliness could be improved. people are nice. staff is decent. they try to do their best but they struggle to accomplish the same. no hot water. lot of bee hives and i've been bit multiple times. food is nice. | [academics]: i studied electrical and electronics engineering. the teachers are very good. professor prajof is very educated and dedicated to teaching. they have updated curriculum to include machine learning and artificial intelligence. | [placements]: 2024 was a bad year for placements based on previous average. good companies still showed up but a lot of the companies didn't show up. placement percentage in my class was 80 percent. highest salary went to electrical and electronics engineering. | [campus life]: campus life was good. i made very good friends and there were lot of events conducted regularly. they had both academic and extra curricular activities. we had super fun in all the events. we even had a beach, so no real complaints.
college_name: National Institute of Technology Karnataka Surathkal

review_text: [college infrastructure]: galgotias is known for it's modern infrastructure although in our time thing's were still settling down like hostel, food court, c block, lifts but now things have definitely improved, coming to hygiene part we never faced any cleanliness and hygiene issues. | [academics]: no doubt galgotias provide best faculties which are highly qualified and coming to curriculum part it is updated accordingly to industry you have to just focus on hands-on part and participate in coding competitions. | [placements]: galgotias is well ahead in market if you compare placements with other colleges, it will provide ample amount of opportunities. in my time time maximum students got placed in tcs, infy, wipro and cogni.

[college infrastructure]: the infrastructure in pes ring road campus is good. there is a be block of thirteen floors and a golden jubilee block . there are total 7 libraries in the campus. the canteen here is very large and spacious which could accommodate 1200 students at same time | [academics]: the academics here are updated . u won't feel too outdated. this is a autonomous college. u will have 2 internals and one emd semester exam . internals will be for 40 marks and emd semester exam fpr hundred marks | [placements]: the highest package in pes university is 1+ cr if u go to the website u will be able to see the information about the student who got 1 cr package the average package here is good compared to other colleges

[college infrastructure]: iit patna has a good infrastructure and having all the facilities that makes ur survival comfortable. the only thing that is not good here is hostel wifi. yes the living places, cafes, food are clean and hygienic. | [academics]: i am here for chemical science and technology and i hope it will be a blessing to my career. the quality of teaching is good but u have to work on you by yourself.the faculty and seniors are there to help u . | [placements]: i am first year now. so i can't tell you much about placement.but i heard that this year highest package was 84lpa from cse batch.around 90% are students are placed in every branch. and yes iit patna is supportive in every field. | [campus life]: what to say about campus lifer here. anyone will fatin love with this. there are uncountable activities to keep u busy and make u constructive. people think that it is in bihta, patna so it will not be safe but it's completely wrong.the campus is equipped with full security guards .

[college infrastructure]: yes our psg college of technology have all the necessary infrastructures , facilities,and equipments. we have eco-friendly campus with lots and lots of trees. and the campus is maintained very clean. the foods in canteen are hygienic | [academics]: i studied computer system and design because i interested in coding and software side. the quality of teaching is so perfect. yes the curriculum is updated every year according to the current trend. coding knowledge made my job ready | [placements]: the quality of placement is very high. 70% of students are placed in top multinational companies. highest package offered is 7l per annum. the average salary package is 3 l per annum. the placement process was so easy. | [campus life]: psg college of technology is best of all other colleges in terms of education, cultural and sports. it's a heaven

[college infrastructure]: the college is spread over probably around 250 acres. every inch of college is very beautifully designed. hostels are very affordable, with only $8,000 per semester. the mess fee is quite good and affordable .it's around 35k per semester considering mess has been purely vegetarian till now. overall, it's a great experience to be part of the iit delhi campus. | [academics]: all faculty members are wellqualified and they deserves to be stay in this college.iit delhi is among the top colleges in india, so you can't question the qualifications and experience of the faculty. the faculty-to student ratio is around 1:10. the course curriculum contains both core mechanical and modern computer knowledge that makes you fully ready for work in the industry. | [placements]: this year, the placement was a little low due to recession but in normal, the places were around 90% with an average highest package of around 20 lpa and the lowest probably at around 6?9 lpa. most students in my class prefer a software-type role. more than 500 companies register every year.

students become eligible to sit in on-campus placement drives from the 5th semester in the 3rd year onwards. college is known for its placement record all over india over 95% of placement is recorded for the cse branch and the rest of the students opt for higher studies or off-campus drives. more than 3500 students got placed last year. and highest package the college ever got was 52 lacs and the average package ranges around 6 lacs. some of the top recruiters are amazon, accenture, tcs, capgemini, cognizant, delloite, etc.kiit fest is the annual fest that is full of joy and excitement held in the month of january. the library of kiit is one of the biggest in odisha which has over 7 lacs books and journals. classrooms are well ventilated, big, and air-conditioned. kiit has two stadiums on its campus for sports events like football, kabaddi, cricket, basketball, etc. social groups like koris, khwab social service society, robotics club are there as per the student's interest.

the annual fest aloha, and tech fest amaya happen annually tech fest in april and annually fest in october. the library is there but not too big, and resources are there. sports are under construction they are working towards it. there are many different clubs where you can participate: coding club, hackathon club, music club, movie club, next tech lab and many more.from semester 3 students need to choose between higher studies and placement. many companies come including microsoft, infosys, wipro, and many more. 50 lakhs is the highest package offered. 100% of students get placed who choose to get placement. my plan is to get a decent package.

[college infrastructure]: iiit hyderabad has a modern, urban campus with compact yet efficient infrastructure. classrooms are equipped with smart teaching tools and comfortable seating. labs are cutting-edge, especially in ai, robotics, and data science. the library is well-stocked with physical and digital resources. hostels, sports grounds, and common areas are well-maintained. | [academics]: academics are research-driven and among the best in india, particularly in cse and ece. the curriculum is rigorous and flexible, with a strong focus on coding, innovation, and real-world problem-solving. faculty are highly qualified, approachable, and research-oriented. students can take part in open-ended projects from the first year itself. | [placements]: iiit-h has an excellent placement record, especially in computer science and related domains. top companies like google, microsoft, adobe, and qualcomm visit the campus. many students receive offers above 30?40 lpa, and research-based profiles are also common. internship and startup opportunities are strong due to the institute?s reputation. | [campus life]: campus life at iiit-h is academically intense but also fulfilling. students participate in clubs, coding contests, cultural events, and fests like felicity. the peer group is highly talented and competitive. the atmosphere fosters independence, innovation, and self-learning. hostel life and late-night coding culture are memorable parts of the experience.
college_name: International Institute of Information Technology Hyderabad

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the college infrastructure was good when we first came into the college , which was this year and now since the college administration is trying to improve the infrastructure and its taking a shape now, i am assuming that when our juniors come the infrastructure will be good! the wifi is good in some places and not good in some other places, library is spacious and has all the books which are required. | [academics]: every college has teachers who teach good and who do not teach good ..so even nmamit has good and bad lecturers. library has reference books which we can refer and study. teachers provide alot of materials from which we can study most of them are friendly..curriculum is moderately updated i can say.. everything we study is not gonna make us job ready we definitely have to acquire skills on our own in every college you go not only nitte! | [placements]: every year the placement record stays good ! the placement team abhyudaya always has the students back and it makes sure we are ready for the interviews and jobs..they will also help us find internships to work on too! for my cource the highest package was around 20 lakhs per annum this year average will be around 8 to 10 lakhs . | [campus life]: campus life is honestly very joyful , the people here in nmamit are such awsome people and the annual tech fest incredia is just a blast each year..this year masala coffee and shaan had come to give performances and it was awsome experience! and events keep on occuring every month and every week here so there is no way someone could get bored here! | [anything else]: i think nmamit as a whole is a good college honestly..if you are thinking of coming here..go for it..
college_name: NMAM Institute of Technology Karkala Taluk

review_text: even such as "incident" south india's biggest cultural fest happens in in our college people all over the country come here to attend the event. famous people are invited here during the fest. another technical event called engineer a smaller event compared to incident only technical stuff happen during event like project showcase etcalumni network and senior students really helpful they have been placed in various useful companies and famous companies such as google amazon flipkart goldman sachs apple visa adobe tcs uber swiggy the constantly guide juniors regarding the internships and all opportunities available in the companies

i chose this course because it was the best that was available at my jee rank. i also looked for potential opportunities for circuital branches at iitk and found out that there are sufficient internship and placement opportunities. the student-faculty ratio is quite high due to the recent increment in iit seats. all the professors are highly trained and hold various degrees like mtech, phd etc.the annual cultural fest antaragni is one of the largest fests in asia. so, is techkriti, the annual technical festival at iitk. both antaragini and techkriti are conducted in may or april of the year. pk kelkar library has tons of books with new books getting added every week. classrooms are equipped with projectors and sound systems.

review_text: [college infrastructure]: our college is quite old but then also infrastructure is good even now some construction work is going on of crc and few department, hostel are also average where u get various facilities, there is dispensary also | [academics]: our faculties are very helpful they guide us and made us familiar to coding languages our faculties are well qualified they try to teach us in easy language and are very helpful whenever we take any problems | [placements]: even in period of recession our college has median package of 13.14 lakh and avg of 15 lakh.our placement starts in may apr and our placement cell tries that all students get placed and tries that all students get internship | [campus life]: our campus life ks nice many event takes place and there is big ground in which u can play cricket, football there is tennis court, basketball court, volleyball court, badminton court

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the new campus has been developed on 100 acres of land at devghat, jhalwa, on the outskirts of allahabad. the architecture aims to transcend established design conventions and make a statement about the similarities between atoms and bits. instead of the traditional geometric lines, the campus and other buildings have been styled on patterns developed by internationally acclaimed scholar and mathematician, roger penrose. | [academics]: faculty members here are very good and cooperative. every faculty member has teaching experience of over 15 years in their fields. the course curriculum is relevant, and it prepares you not only in academics but also for your life. the course curriculum is just appropriate to make students ready for the industry. | [placements]: i absolutely satisfied. it has good placements, really good infrastructure and campus life. placements: approximately 70% of the students from our course got placed. the highest salary package offered in our batch was 14 lpa, while the lowest salary package offered was approximately 4 lpa. for our batch, top recruiters were byju's, hdfc, unacademy and sbi capitalam
college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology Allahabad

review_text: [college infrastructure]: inspite of having low fees from the students, ju provides all the necessary facilities, well equipped classrooms,laboratories. the central library of ju and has plenty of books. the campus is maintained clean and hygienic on daily basis. | [academics]: i'm currently studying be computer science and engineering. on the one hand theoretical part we has awesome faculties who really taught as good and explain all doubts and on the other hand practicals we almost have all facilities so we can do something with real world examples. | [placements]: ju has the speciality of astonishing placement record. nearly 93% of students got placed with highest salary package of 56lpa and the average salary package of 12 lpa offered with our course for this year.

[college infrastructure]: the college area is around 900 acres. there are computer labs, free wi-fi and a library. there is an apollo hospital on our campus. a total of 20 boys hostels and 6 girls hostels are available here. there are 12 different messes according to the food of different states. | [academics]: very professional and highly qualified professors were there. they were very helpful in academic subjects and were experts in their subjects. the teaching quality is awesome and totally digitized classrooms were available. the course gives us good practical knowledge. | [placements]: about 95 percent of students got recruited through campus placement. the average package of 6 lpa and maximum of 28 lpa were offered. cse branch got more placements than any other branch. for our branch, tata steel came with high packages. almost 80 percent students went interns with various companies.

[college infrastructure]: the college's infrastructure is truly commendable, providing an enriching environment for both academic pursuits and personal growth. its physical facilities are a testament to modernity and functionality. the campus boasts state-of-the-art classrooms equipped with the latest technology, facilitating interactive and immersive learning experiences. one of the standout features is the well-stocked library, a sanctuary for knowledge seekers. | [academics]: my college boasts an excellent infrastructure that truly sets it apart. from the moment you step onto campus, you're greeted by a modern and inviting environment that fosters academic growth and personal development. the centerpiece of our campus is the state-of-the-art library, a sprawling space filled with an extensive collection of books, digital resources, and comfortable study nooks. it's a hub for research and collaboration. the classrooms are equipped with cutting-edge technology. | [placements]: the national institute of technology rourkela (nit rourkela) boasts an impressive track record when it comes to placements. known for its rigorous academic programs and strong industry connections, nit rourkela consistently achieves high placement rates that reflect the quality of education it offers. one of the key factors contributing to nit rourkela's success in placements is its proactive training and placement cell. | [campus life]: nit rourkela, also known as nitrkl, boasts an enriching campus life that leaves an indelible mark on every student fortunate enough to experience it. nestled amidst the lush greenery of odisha, this prestigious institution offers a blend of academic excellence and vibrant extracurricular activities. the heart of nitrkl's campus life lies in its diverse student community. with students hailing from various corners of india, the campus is a melting pot of cultures and ideas.

[college infrastructure]: yes my college have all necessary infrastructure,facilities and equipments. my college has smartborad in classroms ,all necessary equipments in laboratory,all necessary book in library, 3 big ground ,1 big sport complex, fast wifi. all these facilities are working fine . | [academics]: currently pursuing btech cse.quailty of education is good yes curriculum is updated and include all necessary development in respective fields . the faculties are all phd holders with good experience in teaching field. | [placements]: quality of placement is very good 95 percentage of student are placed with pacakege of 7 lakh placement process is very feasible . every year 3 round of placement training happes in the college which keeps students updated with the technologies. | [campus life]: campus life is good .there are a lot of activities for students. notabily there is a bloc 13 which is division of student welfare which helps students to showcase their tallents in front of a crowd of over 40k students

campus life is the best part of dtu it's probably best in delhi, and academic pressure is also low. sports events and regular fests are conducted in dtu. there are many clubs and societies in which students can take part like debating society, cultural society etc.from the 5th semester students can apply for various internships and from the 7th semester they can apply for placements. 200+ companies visit every year and the highest ctc was 1.84 cr average is 24lakhs. 80% of students get placed every year and some do startups

[college infrastructure]: this nit has decent infrastructure consisting of all required amenities. a great campus with great state of art infrastructure. library is very big. laboratory ihas all the equipments, encouraging and facilitating students to take research. hostel food is very tasty and hygienic | [academics]: i am doing my graduation in mechanical engineering from this college. the quality of teaching is appreciable . teachers are highly educated and experienced. they motivate students to take research and entrepreneurship. the curriculum is incorporated with the requirement of the industry. | [placements]: placements at this college are quite impressive students get packages comparable to the best of the nits. the average placements offered is around 9 lakhs per annum. highest package offered was nearly 20 lakhs per annum. | [campus life]: campus life is very good. large cultural festival and many other sports events are organised here frequently. the campus is located at very nice place.
college_name: Maulana Azad National Institute of Technology Bhopal

review_text: nit silchar conducts various events comes under technical fest and cultural fest and administration provides a huge amount of fund for these fests. 1. incandescence:- this is a technical fest in which various projects are organised and students came to learn various subjects. different workshops are organised. 2. technoesis:- it is also a technical fest and almost same things are organised as they were in incandescence. 3. poshua:- this is a cultural fest which is organised during bihu( cultural festival of assam) dance, music and various activities are organised. all events are awesome and students of other colleges, scientists, artists, entrepreneur are invited.fee structure depends upon your caste and your economic condition. sc st students have to pay less money compared to general students. and in general category there are 3 divisions. 1:- annual income less than 1 lac. 2. annual income less than 5lacand more than 1 lac. 3. annual income more than 5 lac. every year fee structure changes so you should visit college website for fee structure. but it is affordable for every section of society.

[college infrastructure]: it has all the basic necessities. the campus and the hostels are really really clean and the all the buildings have college wifi which is decent. the library has a nice selection of books. most of the classes are air conditioned. | [academics]: the academics are focused as the faculty and the curriculum really focuses on assignments and evaluations very frequently to ensure that the students understand the concepts that are being taught to them. | [placements]: the placements are very good as the number of companies who come to chitkara is a lot. there is top companies like amazon, hp, airtel etc that visit the campus along with the mass recruiters and also lots of startups that require a lot more work but they compensate with the salary. | [campus life]: the campus life is very good
college_name: Chitkara University Institute of Engineering and Technology Jhansla

review_text: pros/cons: the college is an open campus which is very nice for the development of students the faculty is very friendly and backs up minor inconveniences basic amenities are been provided and cleanliness and hygiene is maintained i feel like they can do better with the infrastructure class schedule are something very hectic creating clash among events the hostel food is not upto the mark often leading health issues | placement experience: from 7th semester students are eligible for placement. many companies come in our college for placement like tcs, wipro, reliance,apl and many more. most students get placed with average package 4-5 lakh highest package in aec 48 lakh my plans after getting degree is getting another degree and then maybe phd

i chose btech mtech in it because i am interested in a career in the computer science sector. the course curriculum is quite relaxed and to the point. in iiit gwalior, we don't have common subjects in the first year. they directly start with advantageous core subjects. dsa starts in the second semester itself. faculty all have phds and are qualified. they teach using lecture slides and solving questions on the board. the quality of faculty and teaching method is on par with other colleges. the student ratio depends on batch, it's somewhere between 60-120. exams happen exactly as per the schedule and are fairly easy to pass. studying one night before the exam is enough to pass, given that the student has attended some of the classes. there is a 75% attendance criteria that some lectures strictly follow.feb./march. there is a decent number of books available in the library. classrooms are air-conditioned, and have one or two projectors. there is a sports complex with a swimming pool, 4 badminton courts, a few table tennis tables, 2 squash courts, 3 tennis courts, etc. there are many clubs run by the students such as the recreation/ journalism club(utthan), rotaract, ieeee student club, gdsc, aasf, etc. the only drawback is the limited student intake. this means everything here happens on a smaller scale.
college_name: Atal Bihari Vajpayee Indian Institute of Information Technology and Management - [ABVIIITM]

review_text: [college infrastructure]: dtu's infra will not disappoint anyone. in sports, there are football, cricket grounds, running track, lawn tennis court, , an indoor hall(badminton courts, pool table, table tennis), as well as various clg sports teams and gym. dtu also has to offer a vast library, offering books of almost every field. there are also medical and banking facilities available in the campus. all labs are well equipped except computer labs, which have very old gen systems, so better bring your own laptops. | [academics]: faculty is well qualified, but dont depend on them for studying as some dont even bother to take classes. only a few are really supportive & helpful. but dont worry, your peer group is whats going to push you forward. the curriculum is recently updated their own. but this also makes them independent. | [placements]: dtu is one of those clgs which has an excellent placement record. in comp specific branches, avg pkg for 2018-19 is abt 17l, median pkg is close to 15l, and placement is close to 93.7. the highest package offered was 1.08c(international), 45l(domestic). around 300 renounced companies came, including google, microsoft, amazon, codenation, adobe, urbanclap. all these companies also come for internships in 3rd yr and there are high chances of getting ppo from them. clg is also very supportive. | [campus life]: having a campus area of abt 160 acres really helps every dtuite, to boast. there is main canteen, then amul shop, and juices&shakes corner. there is also a night canteen( for all those late-night hostellers ;) ). then there are spacious and fully ac smart classrooms, and a big open-air theatre(oat) and a large auditorium, both where mostly all events take place. overall campus life in dtu is among those tier-1 colleges. but i feel, to enjoy it to fullest, hostel is a must.

review_text: [college infrastructure]: nit durgapur is good for infrastructure.all the facilities available in our college are great. wi-fi works well. labs and classrooms are very good. library is a good attraction. sports and medical facilities are great. | [academics]: teachers in our college are very helpful. the teaching quality is very good. the course curriculum is relevant to the industry. faculty give assignments to the students. students also pay attention to the classes very much. | [placements]: around 80% of students get placed in our college. the highest salary package offered is 50 lpa, the lowest salary package is 10 lpa, and the average salary package is 25 lpa. top recruiting companies are ibm,google, microsoft, etc. and many students got internships.

[college infrastructure]: this college is one of the best and oldest college in coimbatore. it has a beautiful infrastructure with large amount of area in the main portion of the city . the college has every equipment and facilities for classroom and lab. the canteen is also good and food is also in good quality. | [academics]: the college is autonomous and has many courses. i chose computer science field because i was interested in it. the curriculum is good and updated. the quality of teaching is also up to level and the faculties are also student friendly. | [placements]: the placements was also good . we were given enough training for placements. about 90% of the students in a class who opted placements got placed in a good company. the average package would be 8l per annum. it was bit challenging too.

[college infrastructure]: the infrastructure is good enough, however their are a few shortcomings but nothing major that will hamper the experience their. in the past 1 year a lot of infrastructure development work has happened to improve the shortcoming and give the university a more contemporary look and feel. | [academics]: most of the teachers are great, however the curriculum is outdated and needs to be modernised. more relevant subjects need to be added and outdated ones need to be removed entirely. also one need to understand that the curriculum is quite extensive and time consuming. one will need to study accordingly if you want to get good grades. | [placements]: 80% of the placements are in sales, so keep that in mind. their are rarely any core placements. however one positive is that almost all companies offer early joinings, this means that you won't have to attend the last semester but only give the exams. | [campus life]: their are a lot of clubs ranging from photography to drama, so their is something for everyone. the no of events happening is also improving as the administration is taking steps to improve the overall campus experience.

pros/cons: in my department all facilities are good hearted. all will speak tamil and english during class. so it will useful for government students - who studied in government school in their high level of education. in my campus life is so good. in my college days, one bridge is there. so we can easily go to hostel. otherwise it will leads to accident. because, my clg is there in main road. all seniors are good soul. they will provide guidance and their notes. so we can easily attend lab exam and ca exams. in my college, production department is very strict. if we did any mistake related to girls or drug directly they will dismiss for min 6 months. | placement experience: placement is very good. in my class around 60 - 58 peoples are place in mnc ( combination of core companies and it companies) remaining 2 persons not optin for placement. they have own plan to attend government exam. wipro, cts, goldman sangs, amazon, qualcom, texas instruments, teminos .... so on

pros/cons: the teachers are very good. he teaches us very well. labs are well settled. all the equipment works properly. there is facility of smart board in all classes there is no permanent campus here. all the buildings are built very far away. actually the weather here is good but it rains too much here which causes problems. there is a little problem in eating, especially for vegetarian people there are many options | placement experience: in our college, internships and placements start from the third year and many companies come to take on-campus placements.many companies like infosys, tata, microsoft, google and many other companies come to our college.if we look at the civil branch, the average placement is 15 lakh and almost 90% of the people get placement on campus.

[college infrastructure]: it?s really good to have so much exposure the food is also prepared very well and in a hygenice manner the library is also well equipped with all the books necessary and with many floors so that we can study there without any discomfort | [academics]: i?m studying btech here at lpu and the teaching has been realyy good and it is really innovative, th? curriculum is updated time to time and it includes all recent developments and this study also makes us job ready | [placements]: the quality of placements are really good and the amount students placed are very high, the college is really supportive during placements by helping us to get ready for interview and coding rounds .. | [campus life]: the campus is really good there?s a strict rule of no ragging that makes us feel safe and the anount of exposure we get over here is really good which helps us in many ways and the location is also very safe

[college infrastructure]: assam engineering college, established in 1955, had commendable infrastructure for its time. however, lack of consistent upgrades has led to outdated facilities, causing it to lag significantly in present-day infrastructure standards. | [academics]: the academic curriculum at assam engineering college is solid, especially for students preparing for gate, as it closely follows the exam syllabus. however, it hasn?t been updated to align with current industry needs. there?s limited exposure to emerging technologies, modern tools, and industry-relevant skills, which affects students? preparedness for the job market beyond competitive exams. | [placements]: placement at aec is decent, with many companies visiting. however, apart from a few psus, the presence of top-tier core or tech companies is limited, affecting opportunities for high-quality placements. | [campus life]: campus life at aec isn't great?it's not a closed campus, overrun by monkeys, lacks recreational spaces, has only one canteen, and there's no bicycle culture, making daily life dull and inconvenient.

[college infrastructure]: they have well maintained labs , libraries and sports grounds.the canteen food is hygiene and in terms of academics conduct various activities the college have auditorium and cinema like classroom for better visual with smart projector. | [academics]: they have an updated curriculum which is covered by well experienced and guiding teachers.the quality of teaching is very great. the teachers are understanding and helpful.it does focus on all-around development and can focus on practical and industry-driven experiences. | [placements]: there are many reputed companies who come for recruitment such as byju's , tcs and many marketing companies as well.on an average our college gets about 15lakhs of package per annum.. the ratio of students getting placed is increasing every year

review_text: [college infrastructure]: it's a good place. beautiful campus with all the high tech facilities. iit madras is a place which gives students the rich and valuable exposure to multiple avenues related to academics, co-curricular and extracurricular activities. each student discovers their true interests and passion and pursues the same. much of the campus is a protected forest, carved out of the guindy national park, home to many animals that are not commonly seen in a busy city like chennai. | [academics]: i'm studying integrated ma programme. my curriculum is very unique to that of engineering department. what we study here is liberal arts. the first two years at the campus might be hard but iit madras provides all the facilities you would need to get ahead. the classes here take only 4-5 hours and the rest 19-20 hours are to be spent helping yourself grow. you can study in that time, play any sport or take part in extracurricular activities. | [placements]: it depends upon the person which qualities he or she has. you should make sure toremain hydrated during the day; that is the most important thing...chennai weather is something to cope up with. the temperature at the campus is normally 3-4 degrees lower than the rest of the city because of the trees here. | [campus life]: all good people with heartwarming professors.

[college infrastructure]: iit jammu is one of the best colleges of india . equiped with ac classrooms and single occupancy hostels from 3rd year . the college also has a very good teaching staff . moreover the food of mess also tastes good . | [academics]: currently i am pursuing bachelor of technology in civil engineering . the professors are well educated and nice speaking . the curriculum is also well updated and keeps changing according to the necessities of the practical and field requirements . | [placements]: as iit jammu is a new iit the placements are good but a person has to work extra so that they are being placed in a nice company . as for my branch , civil engineering a lot of companies come for placement and the average salary given is 12 lakhs which will obviosly increase as the college becomes old and has more alumini network . but still a lot of hardwork has to be put in . | [anything else]: my experience at iit jammu has been wonderful . with one of the best views and nice friends the journey has been good so far .

there are no frequent fests or anything in our university. we have many libraries in our university according to our department. there are many books in the library that we want or need. they will provide all the books in the library. all sports are available.the fee structure is high but they will give scholarships to merit students in this university. they will give 75% of scholarships to merit students and 50% to average students. they will select students for placements according to their knowledge.

review_text: [college infrastructure]: there are so many old bulding which are used as hostel and academic building which was buikt in when this college started so there is bneed ti be build new infrastructure. howevet some progess is there for making new building. | [academics]: academic is good and course are time to time updated but the course are not totally job oriented . some lab eqipments are aldo not working properly it can not replaced for so many years. but there is upgradation is going on this. | [placements]: almost all students are getting placed from nit durgapur now a days placement percentage is incresing from previos few year. in 2021-2022 83.5 % student are placed and in this session more than 70% students are getting placed till december 2022.

review_text: [college infrastructure]: all lecture halls are air conditioned and we maintained , with audio visual and smart boards installed labs are equipped with specialized machines that includes different domain like computing , electronics , biotechnology , high end research labs a library with almost all the books with seating arrangements made for students with subscription to acm and ieee | [academics]: iiit offers all the technology related branches like it , ece , applied science , ml , ds the curriculum is rigorous and you have to perform in the college since my time here i have observed the coding culture is super good here , so if you have interest in that you can sit in the councelling | [placements]: the placements here are very good still in these years all the main and prominent companies visit our campus with good opportunities and package the placement cell is very helpful and and tries to get you an internship towards the end of 6th semester | [campus life]: i feel like the campus area is small although the life here is'nt less than what you deserve , students as wll as proffesors are really helpfull , all the basic emmeneties are provided inside the campus . also you can join different clubs and activites

review_text: [college infrastructure]: srm is excellent in terms of infrastructures and facilities. smart classrooms everywhere along with modern labs. a huge library consisting of books of different genres are present. books for college reference, competitve books, novels, etc you willnfind everything. wifi connection is also very good for online study. the hostels are pretty good and cleaned every single day. mess food is also hygienic. | [academics]: teachers are not always fully equipped with the study plan and can sometimes disgust you. but they try their best to clarify any single doubt you might have. however, the curriculum is not periodically revised and as a result college study doesn't really makes you job ready. however, this gao can be bridged by going through various moocs. | [placements]: i have never seen any tier-2 college providing such exceptional placements. even students who are not that skilled gets a placement. and if you really work hard you can land up to jobs offering 30 lpa. average package here is around 3 lpa while many gets above 5lpa. highest package touches 30 lpa. also, the college gives full support during placement season. | [campus life]: campus life is one of a kind in srm and if you too belong from an middle-class family, you will have an awesome time here. 250 acres campus with beautiful greenary, tracks to jog, sport fields, courts for various games like basketball, badminton, etc make it all the more worthy. as an icing on the cake, the fests are really awesome. two fests take place in a year, aarush (technical) and milan (cultural). both these fests are national level fests and sees participation of hundreds of people.
college_name: SRM Institute of Science and Technology Chennai

review_text: campus life is also amazing. students from different cultures, different district, different state and different parts from india are here. 50% are from andhra pradesh as of state reservation. rest of them from them are from rest of india. every festival is celebrated here, every national day is celebrated here. interaction between seniors and juniors is also very good. as such no serious ragging case is there. whenever there is a fest a lot participation is there from students even from teachers. academic schedule is also hectic.campus is newly built. hostels are superb like a 3 star hotel. hostels are kept neat and clean everyday. whole campuses air conditioned. campus development is still on progress may be by 2022 it will be fully functional. lap complexes also fully air condition and fully updated with latest equipments and pc's, a floor is given to each department. sports facilities are also very good.

[college infrastructure]: the infrastructure of the college is very good. the classrooms have good lighting and are also equipped with projectors and sound amplifiers. the cleanliness of the classrooms are well maintained. the laboratories are modern. the campus has a central library and the various departments also have their own libraries. gardens in the campus are well maintained. sports facilities such as basketball court, cricket ,football ground and indoor sports area is also present. | [academics]: i am a student of electrical and electronics engineering. faculty are well qualified and they teach very well. most of the faculty members are easily approachable .students are encouraged to conduct experiments, build projects and enhance thier knowledge. curriculum is updated on a regular basis. | [placements]: plenty of placement opportunities are available to the students. students get placed both in core field as well as in the it sector. placement training is also provided to students. electrical and electronics department achieved 100% placements in the latest academic year. | [campus life]: when i joined the college, on the first day i saw a few familiar faces in the classroom but the rest were new to me. but by the end of the year i had a classroom full of friends. various activities such as quizes, competitions, interclass games are conducted on a regular basis. the clubs in the college are very active.
college_name: NMAM Institute of Technology Karkala Taluk

review_text: [college infrastructure]: he college infrastructure is impressive, with modern classrooms equipped with smart boards and comfortable seating arrangements. the library is spacious and well-stocked with resources. sports facilities are also great, offering various outdoor and indoor options. the campus is well-maintained, with clean pathways and abundant greenery. | [academics]: the academic curriculum is comprehensive and covers all necessary topics in depth. professors are knowledgeable and generally approachable, often encouraging students to participate in discussions and critical thinking. the workload can be intense at times, but it ensures that we are well-prepared for the real world. | [placements]: the placement cell is quite active and supportive, regularly conducting training sessions, mock interviews, and resume-building workshops. many reputed companies like tcs, infosys, wipro, and deloitte visit the campus every year. a good number of students from cs, it, and ece branches get placed with decent packages.

[college infrastructure]: malhotra university infrastructure is awesome all the facilities are provide in the campus,wifi classes ,ac room hostel for boys and girls , franchises folding outlet in the campus,mess facilities provide for hostel students, smart campus with big buildings | [academics]: academics of galgotia university is nice , galgotia is ranked by naac a so they have goods in academics and focus on the student's development, the facullty member also phd holder , so they have fully knowledgeable about the latest trend. | [placements]: placement in the galgaotia university are best all the students are placed in all department, average package is 7 lpa and highest is 19 lpa ,all the mnc companies and start up companies are visited with good package also.

[college infrastructure]: yes, it has all the necessary facilities and equipment. library is full-fledged with books. living spaces are clean, and the food is obviously good & hygienic. sports centres are pretty good. there's enough space. | [academics]: the teaching quality is good, the course curriculum is also good it provides extra skill development courses, which are helpful for the students. faculty members are qualified and also cooperative. the college can implement live-coding classes and skills to expand our knowledge. | [placements]: the lowest salary package offered here is 2-3lpa, and the highest salary package offered is up to 20 lpa. but the maximum number of students will get a salary package of up to 4 lpa, and many companies offered this package. | [campus life]: it's not completely outside the city, at the same time, it's not completely inside the city. it's a lush green campus, which makes us appreciate it as there's no pollution. campus is also safe. so, campus life is kinda good.

[college infrastructure]: i was genuinely impressed when i first entered the chemistry department at jadavpur university after joining this year. the laboratories are well equipped with modern instruments that i had only read about during my undergraduate days. i appreciate the spacious classrooms and the extensive library resources available specifically for postgraduate students like me. however, being in the night shift means i sometimes face issues with limited canteen options and restricted access to certain facilities. the campus itself is beautiful and well maintained, which makes evening classes quite pleasant for me. the infrastructure is definitely a significant upgrade from my previous college experience. | [academics]: the academic rigor here has completely exceeded my expectations and pushed me to work much harder than before. i am learning from professors who are actively involved in research and bring real laboratory experience into lectures. the curriculum is intensive and covers advanced topics that genuinely challenge my understanding of chemistry at a deeper level. i value how teachers encourage critical thinking and expect us to read research papers alongside textbooks regularly. the semester structure keeps me constantly engaged, and i feel my analytical skills improving significantly already. this is exactly the kind of quality education i was hoping for when i decided to pursue my masters degree. | [placements]: although i am only in my first year, i have already noticed decent placement activities happening for senior batches. the university invites pharmaceutical companies, research organizations, and chemical industries for campus recruitment drives regularly. i feel optimistic seeing my seniors preparing for interviews and getting opportunities through college itself, unlike my graduation experience. the career development cell seems active and provides guidance on resume building and interview preparation to students. however, i cannot give a complete assessment yet since i have not personally gone through the placement process myself. from what i observe so far, the prospects look much better than what i had during my undergraduate years. | [campus life]: campus life here is quite engaging even as a night shift student i must say. the cultural fests and department events are absolutely massive when they happen, creating incredible energy. i attend afternoon to evening classes but manage to participate in major celebrations and programs. the college has a strong tradition of organizing grand events that bring everyone together enthusiastically. i have made solid friendships with batchmates during these occasions and regular study sessions. the vibrant student community makes being part of jadavpur university genuinely exciting for me.

[college infrastructure]: all the infrastructure made after 2014 are pretty good while those built before need renovation. we have well equipped and maintained labs, newly made hostels and academic buildings and an auditorium having a capacity of over 1000. | [academics]: academic life depends on the branch of study. some branches like ece and bt and mme are pretty chill while ee, me, ce are tough to deal with. moreover it also depends on the professor who is undertaking the course. course curriculum is well updated, absolute grading prevails and scoring grades is easy provided one studies methodically. | [placements]: this college has an overall placement percentage of more than 85% every year out of the total batch strength and all sorts of companies with profiles like software development, analytics, consultancy, business development, core engineering, psus visit the campus for recruitment. annual package offered ranges from 6-71 lpa . | [campus life]: good club events, annual fests, sports fests are held time and again so that the students are not bored. | [anything else]: although it's an nit, it is no less than any iit!

[college infrastructure]: the infrastructure is good and the campus is neat. a lot of greenery. two well equipped libraries and also a big football ground as well and there are also other grounds for various sports. all the blocks and classes are neat | [academics]: academic offerings are robust and diverse. expert faculty are available to guide you throughout your academic journey, they are dedicated and hardworking and have good knowledge of the subjects they are teaching | [placements]: the placements are good and it has a good placement records. they have good connections with the industry and dedicated career services ensuring that students are placed well and in good roles fitting to their capabilities | [campus life]: campus life includes a lot of competitions and events happening but it can be better

[college infrastructure]: my first experience while stepping into reva university was amazing! teachers are friendly! people are good and welcoming! the college is getting better and better! exams are written in tablets. attendance system is also amazing as we have to maintain 75% on an average and not subject wise! | [academics]: i am studying computer science engineering in reva university, 1st year, and i have to say that the teaching faculty is really good! yes, the curriculum is updated and includes all the recent developments in the field! skill development programs are also conducted helping us to improve our skills in subjects of our interest. | [placements]: the highest salary offered in reva university is about 24 lakhs per annum. given the fact that the college is new, the placements are good but will improve in the following years. placements in cse branch are really good. | [campus life]: campus life at reva university is very pleasant and fun! although, i would give it 4 stars and because of lack of intra and inemter college fests! that should be improved. other than that, from the hostel facilities to everything, reva offers a good engaging campus. the college infrastructure is also pleasing and is furnished with greenery all over!

[college infrastructure]: vellore institute has everything. it has air-conditioned computer labs, apple macbook for tech students, geological labs for civil students, electrical lab for ece & eee students, art & craft room, garage for the car teams, fully air-conditioned library & unisex gym. separate gyms also available for boys & girls in their respective hostels. multi-cuisine food canteen/eatery, swimming pool, small eateries like lassi house, georgia, dominos, etc. a one-step store present like groceries, stationary | [academics]: academics wise our college seems to be good.we have various copanies coming for placement and various events are being conducted .professors are student friendly and approachable.the curriculum helps us to be job. apart from that ,they also give knowledge about the general aptitude ,soft skill and technical skills that is needed for the job. | [placements]: almost 100% of the students get placements from our college. the highest salary package offered here was 30 lpa, and the lowest salary package offered was 4 lpa. the average salary package offered was around 6-7 lpa. if you are a cse student, then you will definitely get a salary package of above 7lpa. salary packages offered for mechanical and civil are comparatively low, but since jaguar and bmw have started participating in placements, the salary package is expected to increase. | [campus life]: vit campus is around 250 acres.there are a football ground, cricket ground, 6 basketball courts, a swimming pool & a lot more. there are also 4 gyms inside vit. vit also has different types of cafes, and the perk is dominos. there are two major events in vit called rivera and gravitas (technical event). both the events are conducted on a huge scale. rivera is a fun fest and is one of the best fests in whole india with the best singers and comedians performing for the crowd.
college_name: Vellore Institute of Technology Chennai Campus

review_text: [college infrastructure]: college infrastructure is good with high tech class and labs with all the equipment. library is fully furnished with latest books edition with all subjects and wifi is available at the every corner of the institute. | [academics]: i am studying a b.tech because i found the the quality of teaching and methods, the way teachers are tech with love examples and modern technology and study material are updated according to the standard of the market demand. | [placements]: placement are decent and all the students get opportunities to get placed in big firm with their ability and skills in which the teachers are also support the students and provide good opportunity with a decent average placement. | [campus life]: campus life is really a fun with different activities and learning and college provide a clubs where students can take part and show up talent and skills and its is plateform where they interact with different students and make friends.

[college infrastructure]: all the necessary labs and the equipment required for good understanding of the courses is there. there are smart projectors, smart boards and many other teaching aids that help in the further and better understanding of concepts. everything is timely maintained by the cleaning staff. | [academics]: academics have also been a rather major part in development of a student and the college does provide for a reputed set of professors and assistant professors and all the faculty also helps in the growth of the student. | [placements]: the placement stats of the college are also very nice as currently me being in 4th year i personally have seen around 80% of the batch already get many opportunities when it comes to placements and internships also.

pros/cons: since ntpc holds a stake in our college our placements are guaranteed. chattisgarh government has provided a lot of scholarship for students of our college and mostly to girls to encourage girl child education. many innovations and researches are promoted as our college believes in building it leaders. hostels are not cleaned daily and sometimes we are left to do it on our own. | placement experience: at iiit naya raipur from 2022 where 74 out of 180 students were placed in 2025 the number increased to 127. so placements are surging and so are the numbers are also increasing heavily as well. 10 lakhs it is after 2025 and by next year i wish it to be 15 or more.

review_text: the infrastructure of our college was really damm cool and the garden areas will be really awesome. life on campus was really good with the friends but actually, we don't have exactly any one classroom, we have to change classes for every class and even in our free time we have to search for the empty roomsthe fee structure was hiked here for middle-class students. but only the deserved ones will be getting into the college that thing i can assure you. there were different fee categories for different ranks that they achieved in their exam i.e., viteee. i don't have much knowledge about the scholarship.

pros/cons: excellent infrastructure with well-equipped laboratories, experienced faculty members the labs and infrastructure for ece are well-maintained active student clubs, technical events, and seminars that help in overall skill development and industry exposure. placement opportunities in the core ece domain are sometimes less compared to software sectors. hostel food quality and mess variety need improvement for better student satisfaction. administrative work like documentation and approvals can be a bit slow | placement experience: placement of my college is decent enough as per nits. from 7 semester students become eligible for campus placements. companies like micron, ge healthcare, visa, mastercard and more visited and offer 6 to 7 candidates depending on requirement. highest package is 57 lpa in cse branch and 35 lpa in ece branch. in cse placement percentage is 94% and in ece it is 84%. i will go for college placements and will give government exams and gate exam.
