review_text: campus life is quite boring with limited events happening in the college. the fest is also cancelled for 2022 batch due to covid 19. sports facilities are limited with no football ground. classrooms are well equipped with adequate amenities. library is well stocked. one can issue 3 books at a time from library.faculty:student ratio is quite good that is around 1:8. although, college focusses more on theory and rote learning than students grasping concepts. curriculum is tough and up to date and match industry standards. one can manage to score well by just studying a week before exams

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the college infrastructure is very good. the college has all the facilities like air conditioning, wifi, smartboard and sound facilities in the classrooms. laboratories have the best instruments and are available for use to students. | [academics]: when it comes to academics then the name iit is enough. the academics is very great. i am currently enrolled in civil engineering and in second year of the the four year b.tech course. the teaching quality is great. curriculum have all the developments in the field. | [placements]: the placements at iit bhubhaneshwar is outstanding. the average placement here is around 13 lakhs per annum in civil and the branch highest package is around 39 lakhs per annum. almost all the students get placed. the college is very much supportive.

review_text: i am currently admitted in information technology branch at dtu. i opted this branch just because of excellent placement opportunities. in this year, my batch strength is of 75. faculties are highly qualified, very friendly but you might find a problem in understanding their lectures as they are still using old technology to teach, board and marker/ chalk. but professors here are chill. exams occur at pre-decided schedule. cce method was adopted in online curriculum but since college is in offline mode again, so they might dis regard cce pattern and again introduce mid-term and end-term exams. exams are moderately difficult. but if you are consistent enough, you would find it easy to get passed.from 4th semester, students become eligible for internships. some 300+ companies visit dtu every year for recruitment. almost all the major tech giants come here, example amazon, facebook, google, microsoft, flipkart, paytm, de-shaw, code nation etc. highest domestic package is 68lpa and highest foreign package is 1.5 cr. average package in tech branches is approx 15 lpa and at core branches it is 10lpa. almost all students at tech branches get placed, however placements at core branches are not that great. i want a good placement from this college.

review_text: i choose btech because i had a great interest in computer science so i choose this course... as lpu is a known college among the private engineering colleges faculty of lpu are highly educated with phd qualifications and their teaching method is almost good. every semester there is an exam conducted it is a bit hard to pass if someone who doesn't study any thing from the syllabus as btech is already hard to qualifyaccording to me, the fee is good at the facility provided by the lpu so it's worth to cost to study... lpu offers a scholarship of up to approx.6 lakh per student. lpu also provide many facilities to underprivileged students the amount of the scholarship depends on the passing phase of the particular student. as i am a new scholar so i can't provide more info about the job and stipend

review_text: annual fest - bitotsav which is a cultural fest, tech fest - pantheon. library - well equipped but open until 8 pm only. classrooms- equipped with projectors and acs. multiple grounds for sports facilities with a gym inside the campus. a lot of clubs and a great fund by the college to manage the clubs.quite an expensive college with a cost of around 16-17 lacs overall for your b.tech degree but the return on investment is good as well so definitely worth it. there are scholarships based on your gpa from 2nd year and based on your jee rank in 1st year under the name gp birla scholarship.

students are eligible for campus placement from 6 semsters there are several big company visited for campus placement like google, microsoft, paytm, airtel etc, percentage of a student getting placements is about 90%, almost in every branch the ratio come between 80 to90% the highest package is 1,25 cr per annum and the average is 10-12 lakh per annum and the top recruiter is goldman sachs. my plans after getting a degree are to study further for a master's in my field and take good placement is also a decision after completing btech.the annual fest of nsut is moksha and it is held in the month of march, recently it is held between 24 to 26 march 2022, summit, not entrepreneurial summit,consilium21 annual business conclave. central library is available in out. several books are available in the library to study, good condition of ground and sports staff. cricket, football, basketball, volleyball,kabbadi, etc games are available at nsut there are so many groups available on instagram facebook run by unset students like crosslink etc.

this course intrigued me like no other. the faculty was very good according to the collegedunia website. the faculty members have ph.d. in their respective subjects from the best institutions and teach in a manner that is very easy to understand. term exams are every 4 months and passing is not very difficult.the campus is very beautiful and there is greenery everywhere. the social life too is very good. there are various clubs you can participate in. the annual cultural fest is impressions for 62 and converge for 128. the classrooms are well equipped.

review_text: pros/cons: infrstructure is well-built and beautiful. everything which a student might need is present there. various extracurricular activites and events are held there. you can take part in any activity or event if you wish to. different clubs are present, students can opt for the ones they wish to go. the sponteneous decisions taken by the authorities is what frustrate students, plans can change in short period of time. no centralized library, you are only allowed to go to your respective libraries. you can not even sit in the libraries of other disciplines. sometimes, the books you need are present in the libraries of other disciplines but you cannot borrow them. they don't allow to leave the campus befor 3:30pm. once you enter the campus there's no going back even if you don't have any class. you need a gatepass from your mentor which is very less likely to be approved. | placement experience: the placement of this campus are good. this university prepare you from every aspect which will be helpful for your placement. more than 60% of students get placed to companies. in last semster of the degree, companies come to offer you internships. the average package was 4 lpa.

[college infrastructure]: the college is well equipped with all sort of modern amenities. punch in attendance , fully ac campus, clean spaces with proper seating arrangements and a good wifi campus. all the furniture and stuff is in good condition. gym, football ground, squash court, badminton court , basketball court and tennis court are well maintained. food is good but sometimes its not up to mark like you know nothing is better than maa's food. hostel is good . | [academics]: i'm a btech student and i feel this is a really nice place to study. professors are cooperative so you can call, text or ping them up at whatever time you wish to and they will surely help you. its a place full of opportunities and its upto ypu how much you grab. | [placements]: i don't know much about placements as i have just finished my 2nd year. i don't have idea regarding the same. but more or less it depends on students capability . so if you are above average you will definitely be placed. as there are many tech/it companies nearby. | [campus life]: campus life is so so. if you are the one who want to join clg just for fun and bunk clg and roam here n there this place isn't for you. it has a attendance system installed at gate where you have to punch each time when you move in and out from campus and your parents/guardian receive a message regarding the same. clg has few on-campus hangout places such as maggi hotspot, kathi junction and 2-3 more.. sports facility are decent like sometimes it may become too crowdy but ya overall its good.

[college infrastructure]: good infrastructure for every courses. classes have all facilities students wants. world library with lots of books. every types of book available. other equipment and there function details aalso provide to students | [academics]: good environment for studies for students is available there. it can update himself in many ways that will help him grow in every aspect in student life. it is important to have this types of facilities | [placements]: they can help them to find better jobs in their respective industries. they can guide them for find the best jobs in industry. it will help them for every success in life which can change other life. | [campus life]: very good

[college infrastructure]: since assam engineering college is one of the oldest colleges in assam, the infrastructure is poor. the buildings are old,the laboratory materials are not good, bathrooms are not well maintained and same goes for the hostels too,wi-fi doesn't work properly , sports materials are not good. and regarding hygienty in hostels, it is really poor. | [academics]: it is good to some extent but people here don't talk about the recent technologies and developments . the method of learning and teaching is still traditional which is not beneficial at all. more improvised and advanced things must be taught in the college. | [placements]: the quality of placement is poor. around 30% people gets placed,i guess. the highest salary package offered is around 10-12 lpa,that too only in psu s. the average salary offered is around 3 lpa. the college was supportive for the placement process. | [campus life]: the college campus is good. but the government must take measures to control the monkeys,leopards,pythons,cows, which causes a lot of disturbances in classes, the leopards sometimes roams freely in the campus and sometimes cow dung is found in the classrooms. the students and the staff is average. it is located in a beautiful place covered with greenery. yes,it can handle emergencies to some extent.

[college infrastructure]: the college provides all necessary resources like library, gym, labs, food court, auditorium, amphitheater, classrooms, washrooms, parking area and etc. but the architecture is a little bit outdated. | [academics]: the college follows vtu curiculum which is a little bit primitive. so, the college provides good external resources like certifications, projects, workshops, hackathons, conferences, talks, student engagement program and etc to excel yourself | [placements]: the college provides good internship and placement oppoptunies. but it is upto you to put prepare and work hard acccordingly. there are a few ups and downs when it comes to placement. but it is really important to stay optimistic. | [campus life]: the campus life is really great here at rnsit. the college has a strict no rag policy so i personally hv not experienced nor have i seen someone go through ragging. i have really made good friendships in this college which i will cherish throughout my life

i don't know cause i am in first year. but i do know that our college provides internship. placements are quite good in our college as it is one of the finest in our country. also the ppo you get after internship is quite high. so all in all you are going to get placed one way or the other.yeah. our college is always ready to help the one in need. so the students whose family income is very low are given financial aid. but i don't think you get any scholarship just because you are good in acads. you yeah if you are in need this college will surely support you financially.

review_text: the college life at dtu is one of the best you can get, recently we have had the following fests: 1) aarambh(freshers party): it was a great welcome to us freshers with great performers like raghav chaitanya, forty-seven, and more. 2) yuvan(literature and film fest): it was a fun event organized in feb by the yuvan team, it witnessed great artists like rajat chauhan, gopal dutt, and more. 3) engifest: the cultural council organized north asia's largest college fest in feb with main stars: nuclear, sunidhi chauhan, and amit trivedi.the course curriculum seems good as of now, there is approximately 1 professor for every 18 students. the faculties are of course well qualified however the teaching method of a few of them is not good and students feel a gap connecting with them. the exams in dtu are of moderate to easy difficulty.

[college infrastructure]: the institute has decent level of infrastructure and well air conditioned lecture theatre. all the labs of every departments have been equipped with high tech facilities. also central library is meta centre for all academics related sources. and there are all kind of sports facilities of both indoor and outdoor games | [academics]: the institute has rigorous academic curriculum , which includ ebith theoretical and practical aspects of llearning. the faculty members are highly qualified and experienced in the respective fields. also modern and advance methord of learning is used to tell student about practicality rather than theoratical cramming stuffs. | [placements]: the college placements are superb for all the top most branches like cse,ee ece,it ,ice but on the other hand lower branch like bt tt have percentage very less as far on campus placements. one can expect top notch package only if he/she is from top branch | [campus life]: the institute is located in outskirts of jalandhar city in an pollution free campus . differents clubs conduct various activites to engage students and destress about studies. its has basics facilites dispencary , shops to fulfill day to day needs. central parks and gardens are major attraction to embrace its beauty. only negitive aspect is that campus is quite small but still it is safe and in good competitive environment of top creme layer of students
college_name: Dr B R Ambedkar National Institute of Technology Jalandhar

review_text: [college infrastructure]: all the facilities are very good, there is a separate library building with one of the best books collection in delhi area. the computer engineering labs are very well equipped with computers with very fast wifi networks. the classrooms are spacious and have good facilities like a whiteboard, projectors, etc. the college canteen or student center has a very well diversified menu and also there are 3 private canteens available on the campus. | [academics]: i am studying computer engineering which is one of the leading fields of age. the quality of teaching is very good. almost all faculty members are phd holders and have a very distinctive way of teaching. teachers have adequate knowledge of their fields. the curriculum has only recently been updated in 2016 including all the important industrial components into the syllabus of students thus giving them important knowledge to help their endeavours i future | [placements]: the college boasts of one of the best placements in the complete delhi-ncr region. around 150-200 companies visit each year for internships and placements. for branches like coe and it, the college has a 100% placement record from the very beginning. highest packages offered were 1.2 - 1.5 crores by google and microsoft. overseas placements also take place with very impressive packages. the average package of all the branches is around 12-13 lpa while average of coe and it branches is near 15-16 | [campus life]: the overall campus life in nsut delhi is good. every year two fests are organised. moksha the cultural fest which has a footfall of 20,000+ every year and have some amazing artists from all over the globe. and innovision the technical fest in october which gets a very impressive participation from teams all over the country. the location of campus is very good being in the civilised dwarka region and having proximity to metro station.
college_name: Netaji Subhas University of Technology New Delhi

review_text: pros/cons: gla university has the best infrastructure among most universities of north india. our university also has a temple inside and every festivals too are celebrated like janmashtami and diwali. no form of partiality or bullying or ragging is entertained at our university. management is little slow in taking the complaints seriously. hostel rooms are nice but food is little low not just in taste but quality too. | placement experience: packages aren't as good or big as nlus as 4-5 lakhs is median and just 25% students gets placements from university. most students though go for litigation and i am also going to do the same as this is how i want to build my career and take it forward. even llm is also an option and gla university is the one from where i will do, if i plan to do masters.

[college infrastructure]: college has all neccesary infrastructure facilities and equipment to provide a high quality education toits students. classrooms the classrooms are well equipped with modern facilities such as led projectora soubd systems and whiteboards they are also spacious and well ventilated laboratories re well equipped with lateat equipment for conducting experiments in various engineering disciplines they are also regularly maintained to ensure that the working is equipment libraries : the library hacces | [academics]: i am studying information science engineering and it is very good and intresting topic with the help of teachers and staff curriculum is also updated for latest topics of engineering world . our colle- nmamit is also de3ed university which creates its own syllabus based on experienced teachers | [placements]: very good placemnts in our college 2nd level college and top dream companies come to college and also some super dream companis highest salary package recieved in our college is 50 lakh rupees per year which is very high package i am not yet placed in 1st year and this university promises placemnts in good companies

review_text: fees are quite less if compared to the other engg colleges/university. as it's government engineering college fees is not that much. there is a scholarship scheme that provides scholarships on the basis of the tax slab their family comes in.faculty is quite educated in their respective domains but they lack a way of delivering their knowledge. the faculty is pretty good in nature. exams aren't that tough if you study even a day before you will easily pass through.

[college infrastructure]: iit gandhinagar infrastructure great for phd chem engg. labs got modern tools like reactors for catalysis work. library big, journals plenty, wifi fast. hostels ac, mess food healthy. sports ground, pool nice. summer hot, no extra ac. supports research good. | [academics]: academics solid, phd faculty teach catalysis, processes. coursework first, then thesis, seminars on real issues. strict reviews but flexible, funding for papers. guides help if you push. not easy, but publish in top journals. better than many for innovation. | [placements]: phd placements lean research, not mass jobs. many get faculty posts in nits, iits, or postdocs abroad, stipend 50k plus. r&d in reliance if networked. no big drives, depends on papers. average 8-12 lpa academia. good for passion, not quick money. | [campus life]: campus life calm, green 400 acres, fests like blithchron fun. chem clubs, seminars engaging. diverse crowd, friends easy. sports, gym, pool active, canteen cheap. ahmedabad near for breaks. heat tough, not party heavy but balanced. loved research vibe.

[college infrastructure]: yes all the necessary infrastructure facilities and equipment for help to study in chemistry lab.bio lab . computer lab in class rooms we have ac good seating arrangement class have projector also and hostel is also good for living and food as well | [academics]: the academic part is also well upto date , faculty are experts in their fields and study material offered is also well versed. books recommended by the faculty are easily accessible in the library or as e books | [placements]: the university offers good placements, but since there are no such good placements after bsc i didnt opt for them. will surely look for some after post graduation and probably from the same university

i always wanted to pursue btech in cse so that's why i chose it. there's 1 faculty course subject per 80 students but you will get a mentor separately who will help you out in many ways. the faculties are very qualified. most of them have experience in teaching at iit and nit. each of them is really helpful and will give an effort in teaching you. you will have mid-sem and endsem. basically, a semester last 5 months and the questions are mediocre.the annual fest and tech fest of cse were conducted in feb. you will get your semester books for free and the availability of books in the library will be too. the classroom is very good. you can't complain about it. sports and extracurricular activities are the main limelight of the college. there are a lot of social groups run by students like there's coding club and many more.

pros/cons: the infrastructure is very eye catching. the faculties are mostly friendly and understanding. the facilities in college is amazing and useful. some few faculties are not very approachable. the college has turned into a university now. no more kcet entrances and only through nucat | placement experience: placements satrt from the 6th sem with companies offering roles in various domains. top recruiters with high packages and an average of 10 lpa. around 90% of students get placed. after my degree i plan to do masters.
college_name: Nitte Meenakshi Institute of Technology - [NMIT] (Deemed to be University), Bangalore

review_text: pros/cons: teaching methodology is different from other universities and proffesors are also highly qualified proffesors who are teaching for all the subjects the infrastructure of college is well maintained and highly equipped labs also available there is no dislikeful characterastics in college environment everything is well and good | placement experience: after joining the course with 3 months of starting of the course the placements will be startedforthe students to apply for the placements. tier-1mncs companies are visiting for the college which are proving high package . the highest package got is 94lpa and average package is 10lpa. 95% of students got the placements.
college_name: R V College of Engineering - [RVCE], Bangalore

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the faculty and the infrastructure provided by the computer science and engineering department are quite impressive. there are separate rooms for lap practice and theory classes. the canteen in the mess is okay, and the facilities provided by the hostels are good because they provide wi-fi and hot water almost 24/7. | [academics]: since dayananda college of engineering is an autonomous college, the question papers are designed or set by the college teachers themselves, which helps a lot in maintaining a good cgpa throughout the entire four years. coming on the semester exam they are quite easy. | [placements]: ninety percent of the students were successfully placed in the fields of computer science and engineering. the highest package offered is fifty lpa. some of the top recruiting companies for computer science and engineering are sony, etc. there are separate rooms for internship training at the college.
college_name: Dayananda Sagar College of Engineering Bangalore

review_text: pros/cons: i liked lot of things like classrooms library auditorium sports complex and swimming pool and there will be complete freedom in the college there will be less number of food stalls | placement experience: the placements of our college was very nice the highest package in b-tech was 68lpa and the average package was 25lpa and the placement percentage of b-tech was 89%companies like microsoft,googleetc will come
college_name: IIT Indore - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITI], Indore

review_text: pros/cons: my overall experience at the college has been very enriching, both academically and personally. the civil engineering department offers a strong foundation in core subjects with supportive faculty who guide us through practical application and project work. the labs are well equipped and the exposure to software tools like autocad, m s project, primavera p6, bim, stad.pro, revit has been very helpful in preparing us for the industry. in peak hours there is a traffic congestion in outside the campus. need to solve these minor things with the support of traffic regulation board. | placement experience: campus placement at our civil engineering have been steadily improving, with students become eligible from the 7th semester onwords. sevearal reputed companies from the construction and infrastructure sector visit for recruitment and in my class already 4 of them selected at 7th semester itself and nearly 75% of eligible students securing placements and also there are lot od opening in off campus placements also. after completing my degree, i plan to pursue a caareer in construction management and pmc sectors.

[college infrastructure]: iisc bangalore has the best infratructure among the colleges in india. being one of the oldest colleges in india, it is maintained in such a great way that it seems new. all the facilities and equipment for the conduction of research is available here. | [academics]: i am pursuing doctor of philosophy in chemical engineering and will get a ph.d degree in the year 2026. till now, the experience of academics ghas been awesome. curriculum gets updated on a regular basis and contains all the recent developments in the field. | [placements]: the quality of placements is very nice. the average package offered is around rs 28 lakhs per annum and the highest package secures is rs 85 lakhs per annum. placement process is very fruitful and supportive at the college.

life on campus is a lot more lively and chill. you can find people literally anywhere as the campus crowd is a lot and the messy food is not that great. there are a lot of clubs which anyone can join to explore throughout and get engaged in various activities happening on the campus.the course curriculum is a bit obsolete and outdated. things taught are mostly of not that technological advantage to the students. the quality of the faculty is good they are mostly ph.d. holders along with foreign research.
college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology

review_text: course curriculum compared to another college, vit will be great. faculties are skilled and knowledged persons, they will communicate with students very well. it will be better to clear your course on the time , later it will be difficult to you , because there is rules and courses are offered at the particular time, you need to pay 6000 to re register your single course.the rivera is a famous event in our college, which is conducted every year, celebrities and famous singers are participated , there is also different events conducted by various clubs in the college. especially sangamam and tamili are tamil students

[college infrastructure]: labs: there are dedicated labs for each subject, whichever requires experiments. there is 24/7 wi-fi available throughout the campus. a central library is in operation that opens at 9 a.m. in the morning and closes at 10 p.m. it contains all sorts of books from magazines, newspapers, etc. in addition to course books, there is an application called m-library that has over 3,00,000 e-books for the assistance of students. classrooms are spacious, but the condition is not so good. hostel facilities: there are 5 boys and 1 girls hostel. | [academics]: the teachers are really helpful. you can reach out to the faculty anytime you want. the time table is also good. you will have 4 classes per week for a 4 years credit course. the institute also offers minor courses in each department. i'm not very sure about the industrial training. you will have mid and end semester exams in each semester followed by a viva also. they are moderately difficult. most people fail in mathematics generally with every branch having their particular difficult subject. | [placements]: in the years 2022?23, placements for the mechanical and computer science branches were 100% of the eligible students. as for overall placements, they were above 80% of the eligible candidates. the average package for cse was 14.3 lpa, and the overall package was 8.9 lpa. the highest package was 52 offered by cred, and the lowest was 3.5 lpa. the top recruiting companies were atlassian, goldman sachs, amazon, adobe, j.p. morgan, etc. the top roles offered were for sde. about 50% of the students also secured paid internships. | [anything else]: good placement.

[college infrastructure]: as the student of maulana aazad national institute of technology bhopal feel amazed to see the state of art infrastructure at bhopal here you will get best infrastructure amongst all the nits including sports complex labs classrooms etc | [academics]: as i pursued master of technology in digital communication from maulana aazad national institute of technology bhopal the quality of teaching and curriculum are above the average of nits with significant help to students for job | [placements]: the maulana aazad national institute of technology bhopal has comparatively higher packages than average of all nit?s definitely it is amongst top nits the placements process was easy and pleasant for me and college was supportive
college_name: Maulana Azad National Institute of Technology Bhopal

review_text: [college infrastructure]: dit university is a well known university in uttrakhand and is know for their academic and location too.it is situated between hills and has a perfect atmosphere.the academic of dit university is the best .the faculty are very friendly .every class has smart boards and lab are best and equipped with computer and all other instrument.as per my observations the hostel are very nice and hygien.it feels good to be in dit university | [academics]: i am studying computer science engineering and the quality of study is best.the faculty are highly educated and freindly and supports you in every aspect of life.the curriculum is always updated and the faculty helps to all the student in their intrest area by awaring them about the fields of devlopment.student are provided various type of training and workshop in order to make them ready for their future .by getting the above qualities they make students job ready. | [placements]: the quality of placement for the computer science student is excellent and as well as other branches also gets good placement but a little bit less in comparison to computer science.the highest sallary offered was 22 lakh from amazon to the student of computer science.the average salary offered lies between 4 to 5 lakh.the placement process is very healthy and pleasant.the college has a cdc cell which looks over student placement and is very supportive. | [campus life]: the campus life is good as the college is situated between the hills of mussorie and has a pleasant atmosphere all over the year.the senior are very supportive .there are many clubs technical ,art, literature and more where student can engage.the college has its cricket , volleyball and basketball team and provides student option to play all sports.the worst is that the college doesn't have a big sports ground as it has been made on hills.the teacher staff are very good.it can handle emergencies

[college infrastructure]: the hostel facility of christ business school is pretty good. it's well managed and organized, which is a good mess facility. rooms are double-sharing with sufficient space and good interior like cupboard or study table. other facility are also available like purified water, wi-fi enabled, washing machines, 24-hour open mess. | [academics]: the course structure of our college was designed in such a way that students get full exposure to industries or companies. they offer various specialized areas and i opted for marketing, and we studied stuff like digital marketing, global marketing, etc. | [placements]: the placement cell provides 70% assistance they guide the graduation students, and around 70% of the students were placed. the highest salary package offered was rs. 5.12 lpa, and the average was around 3 lpa. internships were also provided with a stipend of around rs.15k per month. | [campus life]: i laughed so hard in class i got kicked out. i once forgot to mute myself in a zoom class everyone heard me singing. | [anything else]: no

i chose this course because i love to write code. you will get experience faculty in this college the faculty are ph.d. holders with experience of more than 15 yrs or the term exam is about 2 months each. the teachers have been really helpful in our college.you can get an internship in the 3rd year in college most of all companies come to our college and give a good stipend of around the highest 2lakhs by sprinkler and an average of 54000 so if you are ready to do hard work then you can get the opportunity.

[college infrastructure]: i was consistently impressed by the expansive and highly organized campus, which feels like a fully equipped technical city. the electronics and telecommunication department had outstanding laboratories with current instruments for circuit design and signal processing. i appreciated the excellent air-conditioned classrooms and reliable network connectivity, essential for my final year projects. i believe the infrastructure is exceptional. | [academics]: i feel the core curriculum was comprehensive, giving me solid foundational knowledge in analog and digital communication. my professors were experts and provided clear insights into complex very large scale integration concepts. i wish the coursework had focused more on emerging fields like internet of things sensor design. i believe the teaching quality prepared me thoroughly for the essential engineering principles. | [placements]: i am satisfied with the placement opportunities i received, as the college successfully attracted top core companies and major information technology firms. the salary packages offered were very competitive for the electronics and telecommunication branch. securing a core engineering job was tough, requiring specific technical knowledge, but the training helped me succeed. i feel the placement support was strong and delivered excellent results. | [campus life]: i absolutely loved the highly dynamic and non-stop campus life throughout my four years. the technical symposiums and major cultural festivals, especially annual fest, were unforgettable. i was heavily involved in several departmental clubs and sports. i feel the residential life fostered an incredible sense of community and provided full personal development.
college_name: Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology Bhubaneswar

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the college provides good infrastructure which composes of projectors in respective classrooms, effective labs, work space , peaceful environment, vast area of playground for sports and a healthy time for other cultural activities. | [academics]: the quality of teaching of the teachers is very good, they are good in their respective field. my college taken some steps into the curriculum of the subject they designed the course industry oriented . the recent developments in the field as well as it makes job ready us. | [placements]: placements are medium to high . generally 70-80% students get placed every year.college is very supportive at the time of interviews and the placement process is pleasant . average salary of placed students goes like 10 lac per annum.
college_name: SRM Institute of Science and Technology Chennai

review_text: [college infrastructure]: college campus is fully green decent class room. lab are well mentioned, there is a college hostel which provides best facilities at low coast .annual hostel fees 55000.all the facilities are provided in college hostel like indoor outdoor gaming and all type function. | [academics]: according to academics this is the best college . experienced seniors faculties are available at any time for the students from high standard universities.good relationship is maintained among the students and faculty members . | [placements]: highest placement is 40 lacs and average placement is 10 lacs. maximum placement from bcom courses. few companies visit like byjus , paytm etc. average placement percentage is 60% . in maths honours placement is not very good.
college_name: International Institute of Information Technology Hyderabad

review_text: the social life is good in college. there is an annual fest bitotsav in which people come from many colleges to attend and many famous people come to perform. the name of the tech fest is pantheon which is the best in jharkhand. there are a lot of books available in the library but the library is not open 24 hours a day. the classrooms are air-conditioned and many sports facilities are also available. the club culture in bit mesra is great there is a lot of competition for joining elite clubsstudents become eligible for placements in the 6th semester when the companies start coming. there are a lot of companies coming with overall 85-90% placement overall. in the mechanical department, the average package of 2023 placement in 9 lacs and the highest is in oracle with 18 lac ctc. in mech, almost 90% of students got placed.

pros/cons: there is mentorship program for freshers so that can know the culture of campus there are various clubs for overall development of students like coding, robotics ,cultural ,arts there is also club for placements called "career development service'' old pattern course that cannot fulfill future demands of skills required not enough placements like first gen. iit's work to build sports complex and new academic block is running | placement experience: there is about 35% students placed . around 230 job offers . everage package :- 17.6 lpa highest package :- 53lpa i qm thinking to go abroar for my higher studies. this year microsoft has offered internship in our college .. i am trying for the same .
college_name: IIT Jammu - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITJ], Jammu

review_text: [college infrastructure]: yes, galgotias university has all the necessary infrastructure, facilities and equipments. there is projectors in classroom, all equipments and instruments for laboratories. there are 3 libraries, 2 of them are for students and 1 is for faculty members. it has all the required books, novels, newspapers in different languages,etc. there is wifi connection in the campus, chilled and purified drinking water. electric bicycles are for hostelers. everything is well maintained. living space is clean. | [academics]: i'm pursuing my btech in computer science and engineering. i choose this college due to its name and fame. the quality of teaching is quite good but there are also some inexperienced faculty members. they can irritate you because they are already irritated and confused. curriculum is updated and there is some subjects are not required for the placements but they taught these also. that is totally time wasting. not completely wht we stdy at college make us job ready, we have to prepare by ourself | [placements]: campus placements are not good enough but they have a high number of companies that visits college for recruitment as they said it in orientation programme. percentage is just like 40-60%. last highest package was of something around 1 crore but only one. average is just 4-5 lakhs. | [campus life]: campus life is good there are many activities like dance,sports, singing concerts, dj nights, movies promotion by celebrities. location is not good because it is far away from the city it is in jungle and there is nothing outside the campus after 6 pm. you will feel bored in hostel. and it's not safe for girls due to it's location.

[college infrastructure]: infra is kind of okayish, or i will say on the negative side, because nab, seimens lab, and ece lab are the only good infrastructure pec has right now, department lecture halls are not even air-conditioned, and those also lack the primary teaching required equipment, sports centers are like 0, almost no funding is given to sports by pec, living spaces are not worth according to the fee they charge, and food is now improving earlier people used to complain a lot. | [academics]: very poor really very poor academics, teachers don't even know how to teach,i can't use that words here. still literally, i was shocked by the level of teachers in a top tier college of india. however, my friends in nits,iits told the condition is same there too so nothing will happen even if you complain, pec's or any other college curriculum will never make you job-ready either its iit,dtu,pec,nit or cu, amity. | [placements]: this part is really unbelievable, how outstanding placements pec is having right now, overall average is 16.7lpa, where nit students struggle too much to get an internship, in pec if you work hard even a little but you will grab an internship,highest package was 83lpa on campus and 1.5cr off campus, apple,adobe,amazon like companies visit pec campus regularly for placements. | [campus life]: campus life depends on individual how he/she balance it out, you can enjoy whole day to tfullest while learning something or just enjoy or just study whole day so it depends on you from end to end, pec being in chandigarh will provide you with everything in which you can enjoy to the level that you can't even imagine if you are a jee student, dispancary is open but for only 10am-5pm, and it really will not help, doctors are unavailable or are like not ready to work, but pgi shares wall with pec.

[college infrastructure]: the campus of iit kanpur is very nice as it has all the necessary equipments and facilities which are required for proper study environment. the facilities like wifi, smart board all are fully functional and well maintained. | [academics]: as it's iit, the study process have been friendly and we enjoy studying this cse course. the quality of teaching is also easy and understandable. the curriculum is being updated timely with all the recent developments in the field. | [placements]: as asked from the seniors and as we've seen the placements have been decent . the placement cell brings new ideas to innovate the student's mind also the college maintains balance between study and sports by arranging many events.

[college infrastructure]: galgotia college infrastructure is very good galgotia college has separate hostel for boys and girls and their is good classes computer lab and science lab galgotia college of separate space for event | [academics]: galgotia college higher a well qualified and well educated facilities for teaching to their student students got good knowledge from their facilities past experience and the got good improvement in their learning and knowledge | [placements]: placement of galgotia is very good the company visited in galgotia in regular basis in good lp and they got good student for their company and some student got multiple of a letter from different different company the placement is average according to me | [campus life]: very good there are many celebrities visit in the regular basis to promote their movie and companies and there is many session held in galgotia college | [anything else]: i have a good experience at galgotia college and i update

[college infrastructure]: everything in infrastructure is good enough . it is modern and latest except the buildings since they have constructed it 10-15 years back . talking about food , it is getting better taste wise whereas there is no compromise in quality . | [academics]: talking about professors , they have enough knowledge but do not have enough knowledge how to deliver it . some professors are quite good at both and makes study a fun activity . while some professors just do their duty and completes the course . yes, the curriculum is updated and they also adds new opportunity to students to grow. | [placements]: placements here are quite good and one of the best in india . every year many seniors get crores of package in core as well as non-core inside campus and off-campus . about 93 percent of students get placement . highest package goes 4 crore . average salary over here is 15 lakh per annum which is quite good in comparison of other private colleges. | [campus life]: college life is just amazing . i feel it is best , since i have never experienced hostel and college life before . best features are : - campus , bengali culture , cycle culture , bengali sweet , its unique environment . worst features are : - academic activities do not get its solution quickly . supporting staff is very nice and helpful .

[college infrastructure]: the college has good infrastructure and facilities that aid in studies. the libraries are accessible to all and wifi is also available in hostel. the living spaces are also clean and hygienic and everything is well-maintained. | [academics]: i'm currently doing mba and the quality of teaching is very good. the teaching styles are different but there is a focus on practical aspects and examples while teaching. the curriculum is well updated and the course do make us job ready. | [placements]: the quality of placement in the college is really good. the salary packages offered are all good and comparatively higher. the college cell is very supportive of the placement processes and will guide you through each. | [campus life]: i have not gone to the campus specifically due to the pandemic but even through online there are many activities conducted for student engagements. fests and webinars included. the staffs and students are all supportive of each other.

pros/cons: i love the energy, students put forward in sports and the unity of the hostels towards it. i love the season when fests start occurring, techxetra is our tech fest name, and it used to be the largest fest, once upon a time in whole ne india i love how the hostel staff are friendly and understanding towards us. i disliked how the semester exam criteria is considered, 4 evaluations in one semester (4months) i disliked some professor, who were biased upon race, and use to make subtle remarks. | course curriculum overview: for a programme, most of the fundamental course help us build our foundations for applications in real life. it builds us, the knowledge we are required to have for solving issues related to this field. there are some department electives which we can choose as well as open electives which we can choose during the course of our programme, for us. that will be a total of 8 semesters. for doing best during the programme, i suggest, a student to do their best during the first 3 exams, since those exams are scoring but scoring in endsem becomes comparatively difficult due to extreme rigorous checking.

[college infrastructure]: the infrastructure and buildings of college is very imminent and nice. the mess building is huge and sufficient for the students to dine in properly. the college area is well developed and nature is such a thing that i would like to mention. | [academics]: the academics depends on your skills and urge to study. exams are pretty easy to pass. the marks split is 60 internal marks and 40 end sems which shows you internal marks matter the most to score good grade. | [placements]: placements are very good , my seniors and my cousins were from this college and they got placed in a decent company, they guided me the best way to get placed including skill development and other required skills. | [campus life]: the campus life in my first year was very nice and joyful. my friends and my seniors made it even better. i enjoyed my hostel life like how i wanted to enjoy so it was such a great experience. the campus is neat and clean. | [anything else]: nothing

review_text: i choose by seeing his placement of previous which is superb almost 85 percent child got placement which is remarkable there are almost 260 faculty members in this college and 1100 students... in which there is 62 student in one section..all the faculty are highly qualified some are doing their phd from iit..most of the faculty members are having the phd degree..exam held in the form of sectional test 1 and sectional test 2 and then 2 preuniversity test. make tough papers to solve so that students can do more hard workon-campus placement is good 27 students got placement in amazon at the 25 lakh per annum one student got the placement of 1.13 crore which is the best of this college. the average package is like 5-6 lakh per annum..last year 85 per cent of students got placement my plan is simple to study hard got the degrees and get the placement from the college

the fees here for my course i.e. bsc clinical psychology are comparatively less as compared to the other courses. the total fee for 3 years is 3.5 lakhs. the college provides scholarships based on the marks scored in the 12th standard as follows: 75%-87.9% = 25% scholarship, 88%-92.9%= 50% scholarship, above 93%= 100% scholarship, no internship is provided by the college.i chose this course because of my interest in psychology. the faculty here was good in the beginning but now it is average. the student-to-faculty ratio is almost 1:20. the faculties here are well-qualified but don't have much experience in teaching. the exams are neither very difficult nor very easy, we have to study everything to score decent marks
