i was interested in pursuing computer science since childhood. the student-to-faculty ratio is 15:1. the quality of education provided by the staff is fair enough for the fee structure when compared to other private universities in india. we have one mid-term and an obvious end-term, which are easy to pass with enough attendance(75%).there is a whole lot of fun stuff to do in the university other than studying. there is a whole block named "department of student welfare", which is where student clubs exist. student clubs are mentored by students themselves and are allowed to conduct fests with sponsorships.

[college infrastructure]: the college infrastructure is one of the best in all over india. college campus is of around 250 acres. campus environment is too good and has diverse life. classrooms are totally air conditioned and digital. | [academics]: in terms of academics, mit comes in top 50 all over india. all professors are well qualified and have experience of above 10 years. some of them might seem a bit strict. curriculum is also moderately hard. | [placements]: placements of this college are most attractive average package ranges between fifteen to twenty lpa and highest package is 56 lpa. placement cell provide global internship opportunities, internships are mostly performance based.

[college infrastructure]: nit durgapur is a great college in terms of infrastructure. laboratories has proper machinery and equipments that are fully functional. wi-fi facilities are available in whole campus. hostels are fully facilated and properly maintained. | [academics]: i had studied master of science in chemistry and got a master degree from the college in the year 2021. overall experience of academics was very pleasant and satisfactory. the curriculum is somewhat old and needs improvement. | [placements]: according to recent placement statistics of 2023 the placement is not very good. only 20% students got placement. the top recruiters were microsoft, tcs, ninja etc. the overall average placement was of around rs. 14 lpa.

faculty behaviour is very good and they also do not take admission more than their capacity. teachers are very well qualified and educated. the exam is of two types mid sem & end sem. mid sem held after 2 months of sem starting and end sem after 4-5 months of starting sem during this lab sessional, viva, quizzes going on for internal marking.the admission fee is 75 k sem fees are 176k the exam registration fee is 1k mess admission fee is 15k the mess fee is 22.5k hostel fee depends according to the hostel there are 3-4 types. of hostels 3 bed ac premium 2 bed ac premium 1 bed ac premium 3 bed ac common washroom 3 bed non ac common washroom 2 bed non ac common washroom

[college infrastructure]: institute infrastructure was out-standing,very pleasant.separate hostels for boys & girls with affordable fee.digital class rooms,central library with 10000+ books & 50+ national & international journals.advanced equipment in labs & workshops. | [academics]: i'm pursuing bachelor of technology in electrical stream,i like circuit theory that s why i selected this stream.teaching was excellent.professors & asst.professors are highly qualified helps in publishing journals.in my view curriculum is sufficient for getting job. | [placements]: placements are awesome,but less core related companies come for selection.many software mncs conducts on campus drives every year.highest salary package is 12 lac per annum for cse student.in my seniors batch 75% students were placed in campus drives. | [campus life]: very free campus,more knowledge can gain in symposiums.

pros/cons: as iit bhilai is a new iit so the infrastructure is really advanced and beautiful and it comes with new and latest facilities the mechanical domain of this college is really nice and the campus is equipped with very advanced experimental facilities as the batch size compared to older iits is smaller here the focus is on each student and their personal growth and preparing them for the future i think the exposure here is limited because it is a new gen iit but soon it will be sorted | placement experience: from 7th sem the students become eligible for college placements and i cannot recall any company that visited and did not make any offer . recently google came to our college for both internship and campus placement . highest placement this season is around 60lpa yet and it might also go up and the avg package is around 1000000 more than 80% of the students that sits in the placement drive gets placed. i am also trying to get placed next year from campus as this is my third year
college_name: IIT Bhilai - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB], Bhilai

review_text: first thing is that you have to clear the entrance exam called vitee. if not so you have a management quota. after that you will be called for your counselling based on your rank in the examination. there are no fixed seats or limited seats. these are decided into categories and depending on it your fees will be decided. higher category, higher fees per year. do check the website for all the documents required at the time of counselling, specially the demand draft. after that you will be instructed on how to present the documents on the day of admission, booking of hostels and messes, buses for day scholars.the college has its own cultural events and fests. if you are in the organising comitee, it is very good or else try to enjoy the festivals and events. there are a lot of restrictions in celebration of festivals and concerts. barricades are there between boys and girls. crackers are restricted. basically it is on the northside festivals such as holi, diwali and all. dahi chewda is not there on makar sankranti.

[college infrastructure]: the infrastruture is decent well it misses the college thing many facilitites lack but the infrastructure is decent, by the way there si no proper gym only one ground where you play cricket and one football ground | [academics]: the faculty is good to be honest they help the students and hardworking plus they help you in many things in your career guide you some are strict though and there are clubs as well to boost the cultural thing | [placements]: placements is decent seeing the current market scenario where its tough to get a job the average cse package this year was 7 lpa so its fine ,aximum students get placed those who sit for the placements | [campus life]: its my sole experience that my campus life was decent but the college vibe isnt there if youre find one then you can try for another one her eyuo are cutoff from the world which although helps you to study though
college_name: National Institute of Technology Arunachal Pradesh

review_text: [college infrastructure]: college have very big campus of around 501 acre and well equipped with all the all modern equipment and labs are as per modern requirement,lectures are taken in central lecture hall by the professors, and have digital smart bords and there is proper sports facilities for all indoor and outdoor sports in proper area called sports complex. there is mess facilities in each hostels and food are hygienic but surely you are going to miss your home food.hostels are well maintained and new also. | [academics]: i am currently in first year of btech chemical and biochemical engineering and commonly known as cbe and all the faculty in our department are well trained from renowned institute all over the world ,mostly from foreign. curriculum are updated and as per industry requirement and as you already know today is tech world so definitely you will get good jobs from here and don't forget that you are competing your graduation from an iits and you will definitely get good package from campus placement. | [placements]: in placement session of 2021 near about 70 companies came for the campus placement and offers near about 300 offer letter to the students with an average package of 28lpa and average package cse is of 40lpa,but in core you will get low package for good package you have to shift to it or finance.so, you will definitely think for it and it is going to worth it for you.

yes, the fees are a bit too much 135000 per year. the students do get scholarships based on their financial wealth from college and can apply for government scholarships, in food technology, the placement is a hundred percentage and almost everyone got a job the average package was 8lpa.i chose food technology because i had an interest in it. the student ratio is fine 23 students in my branch. all the faculties are well qualified and are highly supportive of students. the exams are held twice a semester mid-semester and end-semester that's it.

[college infrastructure]: yes, college has very traditional infrastructure. wifi facility is limited to professors only, food is average in canteen and hostels are small. infrastructure of salt lake campus is below standard. all the laboratories are maintained with modern and well equipped technological machines. | [academics]: in case of academics we have a very good academic background because we have many professor specially the ratio of the professor and the student is very good for every 4 student there is one professor so the ratio is quite good and we can get very good assistance from them. even in case of my department we have 75 professor and in each near me have 150 students so we have a ratio 2:1. this is the reason of getting a very good rank on various technical examination such as gate isro, cat etc. | [placements]: well, i am not really looking for placements since i would like to go for future studies. just like to say education is important, if you educate yourself properly with good knowledge then of course you will get a good job with sophisticated income . and to ensure that you get all kinds of supports and information to get good education the professors , seniors, teachers all of them are there. | [campus life]: being struck in the covid pandemic i failed to enjoy some part of my college life but i can say something for sure ju is an experience not to be missed student teacher relationship is so friendly that u can call a spade with them , thanks to our alum reach that none of our student goes unplaced ! ju is located in 18, raja sc mallic road kolkata, wb - 700032 is at a distance of 25 km airport , 10.2 km from sealdah , 14 km from howrah junction.

pros/cons: the lab and other facilities in the main campus is good. there will be neat and clean environment. the class time will be enjoyable and the canteen is available. the dress code like black shirts, jeans and t shirts are not allowed in this college. there in some partiality shown in students. treat everyone equal and mainly college should conduct culturals by their own and not by the students. | placement experience: the placements where not more for my branch. there was only for it related placements. eventhough the placed student for each company is less than 10 or 20 for each company, the applied students count was more than 5000 but the placed count for each company is 6 to 7. this is not a good placement.

pros/cons: i liked their course curriculum and management. they are student friendly , some professors are very friendly. many events are student driven only , that is important for management skills. the one and most disliked thing is that they don't even give 1 day gap in exams . sometimes their attendance policy is very hectic. | course curriculum overview: hmm , the course structure designed by them is very accurate and they first teach that course and then make compulsory to attend lab of same course in coming semester, this ensures that whatever we have learnt , we can apply that in real life as well.
college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD], New Delhi

review_text: [college infrastructure]: indian institute of technology banaras hindu university varanasi is very beautiful. rooms are generally small and are even 2-3 seater. mess and the meal quality is surprisingly good . some people prefer to stay near hyderabad gate in flats otherwise iit bhu hostels are the most fun place to live in. | [academics]: course curriculum is perfectly set for the students to learn from the basics and clear their fundamentals. iit bhu influenced me to choose this stream as the lab components, professors etc are best as it gets for this stream. we get to work on real time machines and to learn about the working very closely and professionally. | [placements]: the placement rate at iit bhu is great. too many recruiting companies, such as apple, google, microsoft, and infosys, come for internships and placements. the highest package was a ppo offered at 1 crores the lowest around 10 lpa and average around 20lpa.
college_name: Indian Institute of Technology Banaras Hindu University Varanasi

review_text: pros/cons: i did not like a lot of thing about the college only thing i like is that the attendance criteria isn't strict some faculties are pretty good and they have interest in what they teach relevance of curriculum might not look great in starting but in 3rd year it starts getting relatable and usefull the infrastructure is literally nothing the new campus/official campus is still under construction girls to boys ratio is negligible, there are like 10 girls in a batch of 180 people there is no as such campus life its a small campus with very less sports facilities | placement experience: students are eligible after 4th year, not the best in case of placements, from what i hear from seniors is that the placements are very bad yes you get a couple internship opportunities but not a lot fo good companies come for placement.the actual palcements stats are kind of hidden from students. the most and best packages are offcampus.
college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology - [IIIT], Sonepat

review_text: [college infrastructure]: college is very outside and inside even play grounds and hostels are very nicely maintained classrooms are sufficiently big specifically sports club and cultural clubs are very useful to bring you talents up. | [academics]: very good if you really want to study. lecturers are very patient they will repeat if you ask and the also explain briefly and the projects that they give in college are very useful to bring out your skills | [placements]: offers are definitely come but on ur performance not only cgpa ur attitude towards work matters on average 18 lakhs per annual will get .college will definitely support you to get job . highest package is around 50 lakhs

this year kiit celebrated kiit silver jubilee it was a 3 months celebration that included tech fest, star night, sports fest, and many more events this place is known for sports kiit has produced many olympians dutee chand who is the fastest runner of india in 100 meters do practice on the track of kiit itselfi choose this course because of the expected job growth in the computer science sector faculty: students ratio is 1:40 most of the teachers have done a phd and are qualified from iits and nits exams are conducted twice a semester and there is also the option for improvement exams

[college infrastructure]: my college has good infrastructure. college campus is big and has many bus for transportation. my college has all facilities for students. laboratories is well maintained and has all equipments.hostel is well maintained and room is for triple occupancy. | [academics]: i am studying btech computer science in all srm college institute of science and technology.quality of teaching is very good in my college. teachers are skilled and educated.staffs are also humble.my curriculum is updated and here what i study definitely makes me job ready. | [placements]: campus placement at my college are good.the average package for b tech cse is 4 to 8 lpa.the students become eligible for campus placements in 6th semester.almost all big companies visit our college.the highest package for 2024 was 25 lakh from cse branch.
college_name: SRM Institute of Science and Technology Chennai

review_text: [college infrastructure]: our college is good enough for all the facilities required by students and rich in infrastructure and all are well maintained. we are provided with wifi in hostels and in college. we have library with all books available and a pleasant environment to study. our hostels and classrooms are clean and tidy but the way we walk for college is fully covered with crow shit, that's the only thing bad in nitc. | [academics]: for me as a cse student our faculty have rich knowledge about subject. studying well or not depends on students and the doubts regarding subjects can be clarified by the respective faculty during particular session. and yes, we will be perfectly ready for any cse related jobs. the curriculum gets updated with time. | [placements]: placements will be 100% for cse students. but for a person to sit in placements should have minimum of 7 cgpa, 6.5 rarely, so that he gets placed. the maximum salary offered this year is 40,00,000. the process goes pleasant and easy if we are confident enough to face it. our college is very supportive for placements. | [campus life]: campus is good in every factor includes sports, clubs. students interested can participate in any fields. and every year technical and cultural fests are conducted which are popular through out india,they include many events in which we can participate. staff are very supportive. it's a very pleasant to leave here surrounded by trees and greenery.

[college infrastructure]: the infrastructure of the college is awesome as it has well equipped labs and workshops or respective departments. classrooms are very neat and clean along with facility of air-conditioning. college is having library in each department and moreover there is a central library also. specific sport ground is there for all sports as well. hostls are top class with 24 hour power supply as well as wifi. | [academics]: facilities teaching here are from various nits iits so they impart a very good knowledge. curriculum is based on gate syllabus. it is based on the recent job trends also. whatever company ask for they provide everything to their students. | [placements]: gla is famous for its quality placement. more than 90% students got placed every year. highest package is of 22 lpa and it goes to computer science engineering student. average salary offered is 5.08 lpa. the preparation for the placement is awesome here. they make their students job-ready. college is very supportive in placements. | [campus life]: college is situated well outside the city mathura. we used to went out for places like agra delhi jaipur and mathura itself to enjoy the weekend.

[college infrastructure]: i has a limited resources of outdoor games . its infrastructure is average. the all resources, facilities, are in average but the garden and cleanliness of environment is so good . the all equipment just like games equipments and others are so good. | [academics]: i cant describe my feelings about the sessions . the whole academic sessions are just like heaven for me. enjoyments and learning with applications are so memorable for me. i was feeling proud to be and iitian . | [placements]: my exprience is very good entire the whole session . the placements are average in rate but all about it is very usefull for us and for our life. i am very lucky to have a opportunity in this college .

bca is close as possible to btech. with a similar curriculum. and also with less money. bca cost 3.5 lacs for 3 years while btech is 12 lacs for 4 years. faculty of computer application here is meh! not soo good not soo bad. there are 2-3 teachers who are loved! whatever they'll teach you in the class you can't forget that. exams are hectic here first practicals then theory. mid-terms and just after midterms end terms. assignments, seminars, mini/major projects. it really gets hectic during exam days.you get summer fest. there are many quiz events going on every other day. if you're a social you can easily get friends to create your group and have fun. participate in different programs, sports etc. the campus is not huge but it's pretty with its lush greenery. classes are totally digital. lecture theatres are huge! and quiet with any to every amenity. there are several groups spe, dbc, and dance clubs. variety is there. you just have to be active.

the faculty is very good as they ask for doubts and also the teacher in a very good way i am very satisfied with them. students are very more as it is a university in each semester, cat 1 and cat 2 exams are there and then et (final exams happens ). one thing that is bad is that 75 percent is a must otherwise uhh have to pay 5500 for one subject debarred.the fees of my course bca are not as much as it is affordable and the job opportunities are also there. but there is also a very much crowd as you know the competition is also very tough in a computer science course. scholarships are there in our college for reserved categories but i don't have any.

from the 5th sem we will also get trained for scholarships and companies start visiting us based on our percentage and performance we get placed and it's better to get more than 75 cgpa more than a percentage. the highest placement salary is 15 lpa and the average lpa is 3 to 4 for btechthe fee is 1.9l per year and the hostel fee depends on what type of room we choose, we can get a scholarship of up to 30 per cent by lpunest exam and you can get a fee concession by applying for a need base scholarship.

[college infrastructure]: college has all the necessary infrastructure to pursue a bachelor's degree in the field of information technology and so much more. however, i would like to add that there is always scope for improvement given that mainframe computers could be introduced in our college. that being said, there is a separate dedicated incubation lab for our department and labs funded by the likes of microsoft and sun microsystems amongst many others. all amenities are provided and environment is hygienic and clean | [academics]: information technology. quality of teaching, is in most cases top notch. however, there are some cases where attention to detail would be appreciated, especially in theoretical subjects where there is not much scope for practical experience. curriculum is up to date with recent developments in the field. what we study in college, with basic practice, makes one job ready. | [placements]: quality of placements is what vit can take pride in. they have consistently had good placements with multiple 20+lpa offers from various software companies and even peaking at 45 lpa by microsoft. apart from cse, it- the core branches also get into some super dream packages from core companies. generally 99+% students in our college are placed by the end of 4 years. average salary offered considering all branches is 5 lpa at least | [campus life]: campus life is one of the best parts about vit. it is where one makes the memories to cherish for a lifetime. pre-covid we used to enjoy each and every moment spent in college. students can engage in club/chapter associated activities that happen after 7pm generally. however, a restriction is imposed by which students not having night slips have to enter hostel by 9:30-10pm. they can roam inside the hostel but not outside. the campus is totally safe and equipped to handle emergencies of all kind

[college infrastructure]: netaji subhash university of college is the bestest college for students. classes of this college is superb. proper ac classes are provided with projectors. libraries in this college is very big with around more than 5000 books. | [academics]: i'm studying mba in marketing and business analytics. this college is known for its mba. quality of teaching is best. all the professor's are phd and very good experience in teaching. academics are good . | [placements]: placement in this college is normal. students who've potential can easily take placements in this college. average placement salary is 7lpa - 15lpa in this college. good for mba students to took admission in this college.
college_name: Netaji Subhas University of Technology New Delhi

review_text: [college infrastructure]: galgotias university have all the necessary infrastructure , facilities and equipments. classrooms are digital having digital boards and are air conditioned. laboratories are well equipped with all the required devices and equipments. libraries are digital and are well maintained. | [academics]: b tech computer science and engineering. quality of education is teacher are supportive. all the teachers are well qualified. in first year i don't think curriculum is updated and includes all recent developments in the field. | [placements]: placement are good at galgotias university. highest salary package offered is 1.5 cr. it was offered in b. tech computer science and engineering. average salary package is 5-6 lac per annum. college is supportive for placements.

[college infrastructure]: the facilities provided by the college are great whether its e-library to online classes or providing wi-fi facilities. everything was available in the institute. the hostel facilities of the college are also great as they have various different types of hostels with good mess and medical facilities. | [academics]: the teachers in symbiosis institute are really helpful and guide us at every step of our journey. as the teachers are themselves from the industry background only, hence they give us a great examples from the industry as well. the course curriculum is really good as they provide us with a lot of information. | [placements]: the symbiosis institute itself is a big brand when it comes to the placements of the college. the college provides us with great placements which include amazon, federal bank, amdocs, and many other companies. the highest package recorded by symbiosis was 24 lac.

pros/cons: the amount of resources iit bombay provides is awesome. the professors clarity over the subject they teach and their intuitive explanation skills are good. the clubs and tech teams provide deep domain knowledge and keep us constantly active in some or the other events. the hostel services can be improved and also the mess food . there can be more interactive sessions so that the students are able to know each other. the transport facilities in the college can be improved. | placement experience: the campus placements at iit bombay are good , we can apply for any company irrespective of our branch students are eligible for placements from the 7th semester onwards there are no companies that visited iit bombay but did not offer the highest package ever recorded was around 4.5cr, it was in fintech ( developing algorithms) 75-80 percent of the students get placed every year my plan is to sit for placements in the companies iam interested in after my degree
college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB], Mumbai

review_text: [college infrastructure]: nit durgapur is situated in the heart of the the city .it enjoys location benefits being in the heart of the city.coming to the infrastructure the infrastructure is pretty good and improving day by day.a new mega hostel is recently constructed which is called hall-14 which can acomodate 1500 students.the campus is lush green and environment friendly.also classrooms are equipped with air conditioning ,proper sports facilities are there.there is a central library and laboratories are well equipped | [academics]: the academics here are good and return on investment is good .i am currently studying integrated masters course in chemistry which includes engineering subjects pretty much.i got to get it through jee mains.the teaching is good but it can be better.our professors are all phd and have vast experience in their field.curriculum is updated according to industry standards and attaracts a lot of recruiters ,even our placements are going to commence and various things are done which makes us job ready | [placements]: the placements are good but it can be improved.cs and it guys get great placement .for core departments companies cone but they are low in number although a handsome number of projects and internship opportunities are provided to students.for the courses 80-85% students were placed in cs,it followed by decrease in core branches.average salary here is 9 lpa and highest package was 32 lpa.the placements here is 70-80% for all branches.the placement process comprises of apti,techincal and hr round | [campus life]: the campus life is pretty good.its a dream college .the lush green environment adds to its beauty and it has a sprawling 360 acre campus.everyday we make memories be it nightout, eating .every moment spent here is itched in our memory forever.a ton of events are going on now and then.our cultural fest nityamini(formerly recstacy) is celebrated with great zeal where famous celebrities come .it is located in heart of city and support staff is supportive.overall a worthy place to be.

[college infrastructure]: the campus has spacious classrooms with projectors, a moderate-sized library, and computer labs for management students. hostel facilities are available near the campus, with basic amenities like furnished rooms, hot water, and housekeeping services. | [academics]: the teaching style is a mix of theoretical concepts and case discussions, which helps in understanding management basics. the curriculum is updated but still more academic than industry-oriented. attendance is 75 percent needed for appearing in exams. | [placements]: the lowest package offered is about 3.5 lpa, while the highest package recently touched 18 lpa. some students also managed to secure off-campus packages up to 25 lpa. the top recruiting companies include names like aditya birla capital, kotak life, future generali, jaro education, and berger paints.

[college infrastructure]: college infrastructure is too good in srm university faculty members are too friendly all management they have good placement last year 41 lack per annum package srm gave best placement in private university | [academics]: in srm provide best quality of study there is many fest organised in srm and every 2-3 month they provide best study they provide all good facilities college infrastructure is too good in srm university faculty members are too friendly all management | [placements]: the quality of placement is too good they provide many companies come to our campus they provide in bulk job they provide good salary package of the company is too good last year 41 lack per annum package | [campus life]: campus life is good
college_name: SRM Institute of Science and Technology NCR Campus Ghaziabad

review_text: [college infrastructure]: not well equipped labs. not even well equipped sports facilities due to lack of space. even hostel located at far distdance nearly 15km from campus building. mechanical bracnch is only available to mtech and phd students and runs in container like classroom | [academics]: many teachers did the phd but onky few of them how to express a particular topic effectively. there is always a race to grab books from library due to insufficiency of books. although all the classroom and labs equipped with acs but it not helps to improve the academic. | [placements]: many companies come for cse and students only. even if a students from another branch has good coding skills,he maynot allowed to sit on that placement drive .many of companies come here with 5-8 lacs package and only some got around 12+ lacs packages. | [campus life]: it has some good aspect but many bad aspects. its hostel located in jungles of tdi for juniors but we enjoyed there at our own costs by organising various events. girls to boys ratio is very steepe. and the campus is located far from delhi .

the curriculum at lpu is very well organized and everything is well planned before the start of the lesson. i enrolled in the b.tech computer science and engineering course and chose this course because i have had a great interest in coding since my school days and today the field is very popular. at lpu, the cse section has about 100 students in all categories and skills are also not enough to teach everyone. in lpu all intelligence has a ph.d. owner and knowledge. in each semester, there will be 3 class exams, mid-term and end-of-year exams. if you fail to get at least 40% in one subject you will be offered to reappear in that subject.campus life will be one of the most wonderful moments of your life. there is a dsw (student and social services) to organize all events and host any programs. in the lpu there are many festivals run like one india country, esports tournament, cricket tournament, and many festivals like code writing competitions and much more. and the lpu library is one of the largest libraries in india. in all classes, there are projectors and computers for better information. at lpu you can join student clubs to do after-school activities. at the lpu celebrities also visit to promote their films.

kiit university offers a diverse range of undergraduate and postgraduate courses across various fields, including engineering, law, management, medicine, fashion, and others. the course curriculum at kiit university is designed to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of the subject matter, as well as practical skills necessary for their future careers. the curriculum is regularly updated to reflect changes in the industry, ensuring that students are well-prepared for the demands of their respective fields. the faculty-student ratio at kiit university is reportedly good, with a relatively high number of experienced faculty members compared to the number of students. the university has a team of well-qualified and experienced faculty members who are passionate about teaching and helping students achieve their academic goals. they are known for their dedication, professionalism, and expertise in their respective fields.the computer science department at kiit university has a high placement rate, with a majority of the students being placed in top companies. the average salary package offered to the students in the computer science department is around 5-6 lakhs per annum, with some students receiving higher packages based on their skills and performance. some of the prominent recruiters include amazon, ibm, microsoft, dell, tcs, infosys, wipro, and capgemini, among others. the university has also seen a surge in the number of startups visiting the campus in recent years, offering students with exciting opportunities to work in innovative and emerging areas.

[college infrastructure]: the classrooms are well ventilated and have full ac, and the labs also well decent. the library's sports facilities are also good, but the hostel is quite decent. in the first year, you are given a three-seater room, but in the next, you are given a single room, and you need to adjust to the mess. | [academics]: the teacher is helpful, but the majority of the work you need to do on your own as there is no spoon-feeding and the course is quite difficult as it has both a computer and electrical branch course, and the exam is of moderate level if, in case it is hard, relatively high marks are given, which makes it the same. | [placements]: the percentage of students placed is about 90 to 92%, as the course is quite difficult to study, but the highest package is about 64 lpa and average is about 20 lpa and the lowest, i don't have an idea. the top recruiters are google apple microsoft. internship are also there; you need to make project and based on the project you are selected for internship.
college_name: Malaviya National Institute of Technology Jaipur

review_text: pros/cons: i like my college atmosphere which is very good the football ground will be very good too the placements will be good so i like it i dislike the hostel food which is very bad i don't like the outing restriction which is before 10 pn i don't like the labs because i'm weak in it | placement experience: from 7th semester we are eligible for placement activities. many of the companies like tcs, abb, accenture etc. visited our campus. and 10 members got selected. the highest package will be 18 lpa. and the average will be 8lpa.

the scholarship is based on the rank you got in the lpunest exam. if you get a seat in nit,iit after taking the admission in this university you will get a stifund too . fee structure 85k per sem as i got a max scholarship in lpunest exam.the annual fest and tech fest are conducted regularly. sports are there if you interested to learn them. the infrastructure is too good. nearly 600 acres the campus was constructed. the classrooms are too good and clean.

pros/cons: the coding culture of iit patna is insane. seniors are very helpful to you if you ask for help. extracurricular activities are also good. you can easily develop one of your talents here in iit patna placement scenario is also goated. the average placement of last year of my branch which is computer science was 17lpa. the boy to girl ratio of iit patna is bad. there is approx 80:20 ratio of boys vs girls. the main campus of iit patna is a little away from main town which can be a problem sometimes. weather can be extreme a lots of time if you don't have a habit of it. | course curriculum overview: the exams are conducted once in midsem and then endsem. with quiz conducting avg. per fifteen days. being an iit, iit patna has a rigorous curriculum but it's manageable because students are also pretty good academically. more coding related subjects can be added and non-coding related subjects can be removed.

review_text: [college infrastructure]: good infrastructure it has big canteens and the class rooms are specious there are trees around the campus which make the infrastructure more better for studying it also has a big cricket ground and others sports are also there | [academics]: teachers in psychology are amazing the are very supportive and keep motivating me a lot it feels very helpful and the classroom come with advance projector which makes learning easy it hads very good academics overall | [placements]: the placements here are good for psychology it's less but there are good ones they have started psychology recently so placements are good compared to the start i am very happy to see my seniors get such nice offer

review_text: [college infrastructure]: shiv nadar university has a lush green campus, equipped with state-of-the-art infrastructure. snu has well-furnished hostels, working labs, equipped pantry, play grounds, student activity centers and a fully functional health care facility. | [academics]: the faculty at shiv nadar university follows the updated curriculum and follows all internationally recognized textbooks and study material. academic learning is designed keeping in mind the requirement of the job market and future prospects of the students. | [placements]: as snu is a new university, it usually struggles to offer placement to all domains except engineering. the placement for natural sciences, humanities, business, etc is not up to the mark. students usually suffer to crack the high ctc roles and dream organisations. | [campus life]: snu is usually known for its campus life and night life. people usually say that shiv nadar university never sleeps. food kiosks, labs, 24*7 study room, hostels are always open for students to explore irrespective of the time.

i chose btech mtech in it because i am interested in a career in the computer science sector. the course curriculum is quite relaxed and to the point. in iiit gwalior, we don't have common subjects in the first year. they directly start with advantageous core subjects. dsa starts in the second semester itself. faculty all have phds and are qualified. they teach using lecture slides and solving questions on the board. the quality of faculty and teaching method is on par with other colleges. the student ratio depends on batch, it's somewhere between 60-120. exams happen exactly as per the schedule and are fairly easy to pass. studying one night before the exam is enough to pass, given that the student has attended some of the classes. there is a 75% attendance criteria that some lectures strictly follow.feb./march. there is a decent number of books available in the library. classrooms are air-conditioned, and have one or two projectors. there is a sports complex with a swimming pool, 4 badminton courts, a few table tennis tables, 2 squash courts, 3 tennis courts, etc. there are many clubs run by the students such as the recreation/ journalism club(utthan), rotaract, ieeee student club, gdsc, aasf, etc. the only drawback is the limited student intake. this means everything here happens on a smaller scale.
college_name: Atal Bihari Vajpayee Indian Institute of Information Technology and Management - [ABVIIITM]

review_text: [college infrastructure]: infrastructure is only the thing that is good at dtu. obviously it is best as conpare with many colleges. it have big sport complex, manyyy food outlets new outlests are made and central library. classrooms have ac and projectors. overall infrastructure is great. | [academics]: each semester spans about 4-5 months. for example, if the semester starts in august, the final exams are usually held in november. this schedule determines the frequency of exams. the difficulty level of exams is quite manageable as the questions are typically based on what the professors teach in class. most questions are application-based but still straightforward and easy to solve. however, the curriculum does not fully align with the skills required in the industry, which is a common issue across many colleges. for instance, in the computer science program, students study a variety of programming subjects, including web designing, dbms, operating systems, computer networks, and cybersecurity-related topics. additionally, there are elective subjects that allow students to explore areas beyond their core branch. these electives are often easier, particularly those from the humanities stream, making them a popular choice among students. while the curriculum covers a broad range of topics, it lacks a direct focus on the practical skills that are in high demand in the industry. | [placements]: dtu is renowned for its placement opportunities, with over 400 companies visiting annually, including top names like amazon, microsoft, zomato, and apple. students with a cgpa of 6.0 or higher become eligible for placements in their fourth year, while those below 6.0 have limited chances. high-paying roles, especially in computer science and it, often come from international companies, with the highest package reaching ?1 crore. the average package is ?7-8 lpa, with roles offered in software development, data analysis, consulting, and core engineering. success largely depends on skills, cgpa, and communication abilities. my focus is on gaining expertise and practical knowledge to enhance long-term employability, with or without campus placements. | [campus life]: my campus life at dtu has been a mix of excellent facilities and individual effort. the infrastructure is a standout feature, with air-conditioned classrooms, projectors, a well-stocked central library, and a massive sports complex supporting various activities. dtu hosts fest like engifest (february) and yuvaan (october-november), which offer great exposure but can feel crowded due to participants from other colleges. social life on campus is individualistic, with students focused on personal goals, though there are many active clubs for coding, robotics, drama, and more. these clubs enhance skills and provide a sense of community for those involved. overall, dtu provides ample resources and opportunities, but making the most of campus life depends on how actively you participate.

aurora: this is a cultural festival held over a period of three days in the month of february/ march. aurora should not look upon as just a cultural event but as an attempt to promote the friendship, teamwork and alliances. our tech-fest name is infotsav: our college organises events that acknowledge technological innovation and it is conducted every year. our library is the central biggest library in which over 30000+ books are available & our library boasts almost 80 journal titles both from india and abroad. amenities in classrooms such as projector, laser light, chairs, fans, ac, black & whiteboard. we have a sports complex in which you can play all the sports such as badminton, table tennis, squash, one basketball court, one cricket ground & one football ground. in iiitm gwalior there are various club such as rotaract (which work for social welfare), uthaan(recreational club), sgm(helps poor children by teaching them), e-cell & ieee.i have chosen this college because of my interest in cs/it field and i want to pursue this interest. faculty: student ratio is 1: 20 due to the fewer number of branches in our college there is less intake of students and that's why our faculty: student ratio is well maintained. in iiitm gwalior all the faculty members are phd holders & have a grip on their concepts and teach us in the best way. generally, minor exams are conducted in the span of 2 months and after that major exams are conducted after 2 months then the next semester will be started. and if you give attention towards the classes and solved reference books then scoring a good cgpa will be a piece of cake for you.

review_text: [college infrastructure]: lovely professional university has one of the best infrastructure among all private universities in india. lpu has 8 boys hostel and 6 girls hostel which is really awesome. there is 24 hour of wi-fi facilities in all the hostels and even in classrooms too. classrooms are digital and fully ac. labs are fully functional with all the modern equipments installed in it. there is a big sport's ground in which you can play all the sports. university is well maintained. food served here are hygienic. | [academics]: i am pursuing b. tech from cse here. i opt to choose this course because this course is in much demand of the industry and job aspects are quite high. the faculty in the college are very down to earth and are always ready to help their students. they help the students beyond their capabilities. they support us even to tackle mental stress. there are many courses offered in my cse department which is in much demand of the industry like data science, ai & ml, cyber security, iot, web development. | [placements]: talking about the placement, then i must say it's average+. students who study well and is focused on the placement, then no matter what, he/she will get placement for sure as lpu provides you that much needed platform to do better in your career. almost 80-85% of the student got placed. the average package of cse is 4.9lpa and the highest package offered last year was 42lpa from microsoft. | [campus life]: as my rating suggests, campus life is really amazing here. you can engage in several student organisation from which you will get the management and leadership skills. you can learn some extra things too by devoting your time towards extracurricular activities like how to play piano, learn to play guitar, learn how to act etc. students and support staff are very friendly in nature and they will guide you if seek any support from them. lpu is located at phagwara in punjab. campus is quite safe.

the annual fest is engi fest is one the greatest fest that i saw tech fests like e summit and cultural fest are held by yuvaan and every month there is some event held. sports faculties are not that much good all the societies are run by the students like e-cell for entrepreneurshipcompanies mostly are tech-based so the role offered there is mostly based on coding and non-tech the companies majorly focus on getting industrial experience so that depends on company to company and project like. problem-solving of a startup are generally in trend

[college infrastructure]: university provides all necessary infrastructure such as laboratory, mac labs, texas instrument lab.lpu also encourages sports and it has indoor sports complex with olympic size swimming pool. food is hygienic too. | [academics]: i?m in my third year of bachelor of technology in computer science and engineering. lpu provides skill oriented programs to catchup with new age technology. faculties are well educated and highly qualified. | [placements]: lovely professional university has tie ups with all the major players in their respective field so it gives golden opportunity to all candidates to get good placement or internship to get industrial experience | [campus life]: lpu has 600+ acres campus having students from all states of india and 55+ countries which guves me exposure and opportunity to grow and build connections. lpu location is safe and it is connected with all major cities as it falls on gt road

various scholarships are being provided to the students. the cost of the course is also not so high. there is a minimal price hike. financial aids are also being provided to the students who belong to the financially weaker section or have very good academic grades. overall the college is very good.the campus life is very good. there are many clubs and various societies in my college. students can easily find their niche and interest and can follow their hobbies. there are many books in our library. the library facilities are very good.

review_text: pros/cons: the excellent of faculty support was good and the infrastructure was also good.but the college building are slightly bad with fully patch works . any doubts are accepted and cleared by the facilities.mentorship was flow goodm every person took a test of communication and technical skill training and learning. | course curriculum overview: flexi learning was playing a crucial role in every student's life in the college. because of full unknown teaching staff are there in the enrollment,when i ask to my seniors. curriculum was so fast 45 + 45 days once we wrote the exams.

[college infrastructure]: college is located at very good location. infrastructure is very good.approx all facilities are available.college have proper equipments in in laboratory. college library is full of books of different streams. sports facilities are very good in the college. different type of sports like cricket, football ,basketball ,table tennis are played in the college. college hostel have wi-fi with excellent speed. college also have smart boards and modern teaching equipments. | [academics]: i am studying btech cse in the college. academics are very good and teachers and faculties are very talented . curriculum is very updated and it includes all the recent developments in the field. laboratories are very good. theoretical as well as practical knowledge is given properly. faculties always stands for the students and for their welfare. they all will support students either inside the class or outside the class. | [placements]: quality of placement is one of the best in entire state of uttarakhand. rocks under percent of the students were placed. highest placement was above 30 lakh per annum in cse department. average salary is approx 4 to 6 lacs per annum. placement process is very easy and college very supportive.

from end of the 5 semesters, one is eligible for campus placement. the name of the companies visited are - atlassian ,microsoft,amazon,flipkart,adobe ,reliance,zs associate ,time, larsen and turbo,tata motors ,maruti suzuki ,goldman sachchs ,tata steel. highest package offered is 54 lakhs by microsoft and average package offered is 14 lakh. almost 100% students get placed . my plan is to go for higher studies .mechanical was the only branch i was getting in my rank.the institute was good so took admission here. faculty to student ratio is about 1:12 all the faculties are very well qalified the professors have doen their phd from iits . they teach application based only. each module has a quiz .rigorous study is needed to pass.

[college infrastructure]: the infrastructure of my college is one of the best in india. it is located on 850 acres of land, and the campus is fully landscaped with greenery. there are 60 buildings, each dedicated to a specific branch or course of study. there are also eight boys hostels and six girls hostels, which offer a variety of room options, including single, double, triple, and quadruple occupancy, both air-conditioned and non-air-conditioned. | [academics]: the teachers are very open and willing to clear doubts. they are available in their cabins in the respective branch blocks from 9am to 5pm. we can book an appointment for clarification. there is also a mentor for every 10 students from different sections. the marking scheme is simple: ca=25 marks, attendance=5 marks, mid=20 marks, end=50 marks. | [placements]: in my batch, 80% of students were placed. the highest package was 42 lpa, and the lowest was 4.5 lpa. infosys, tcs, and other mncs were among the recruiters. internship opportunities were available, with 70% of students securing internships. some of the roles offered were penetration tester, software developer, and golang developer.

pros/cons: dtu has small but a campus of stature which has best classrooms and faculties. placements are so good that du students migrating ratio to dtu is increasing. syllabus is same as du economics hons and still the way it's taught at dtu is more advanced than that of hansraj or srcc. students ratio between outsiders and locals is very rare as 80% or more students are local students. | placement experience: jp morgan, goldman sachs and others which dwells in various economies are some of the recruitment organisation which has come to dtu time and again. for economics hons the placements have been in the range of 5-6 lpa.

review_text: [college infrastructure]: jadavpur university is one of the best government engineering colleges in india. its college infrastructure is very good.classrooms are clean , laboratories have enough number of equipment, libraries have more than enough books.there is free wi-fi available in the campus.foods in the college canteen is hygienic and tasty. | [academics]: as mentioned earlier it is one of the best colleges.most of the professors here are iitians.they possess very good quality of teaching.our curriculum is very much updated with time.the syllabus is gate friendly and it makes us ready for job placement. | [placements]: placements in jadavpur university is very good.in 2020 one of the students got 65 lpa package and the average placement statistics was nearly about 8 lpa. placement process is much easier than other colleges. | [campus life]: you will never find an independent campus life in other colleges than ju.students get free water and wi-fi service inside the campus. the teachers are very helpful to students.it is loacted in south kolkata .

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the infrastructure of the college is average. however, environmental planning department has good infrastructure. classes are air-conditioned with comfortable seats and wi-fi. the canteen food is affordable and good. the hostel rooms are developed to accommodate one student per room. however, due to lack of rooms usually, 2 people share the room (it's on a consent basis, if the room is allotted to you, then it's your wish to give consent and accommodate other classmates and no discount on fees will be given for consent). | [academics]: academics is also good over here. the teachers and trainers are so experienced. they provide us so many good resources. they also provide us many opportunities related to internships and many other opportunities. | [placements]: placements every student gets a chance for summer internships on behalf of college and some may choose to do on their own. we work under development authorities in the internship processes. we constantly are working on projects which help us learn to analyse better. the experienced faculty is a great help to us during all our projects and assignments.

[college infrastructure]: infrastructure is decent. hostels vary accordingly from block to block. hostel are allotted based on your cgpa and branch. college building is a decent one. buildings have been divided acc to the branch. | [academics]: research is given high priority. there are many courses which you need to complete which are not compulsory in other colleges. some subjects which aren't required by a specific bench person are not allotted to them. but ffcs is both a good and bad thing depending on your luck. | [placements]: vit is known for it's placements. good companies come.if you are from it or cse branch you have high probability of getting placed in a good company. that is what people join vit for. a better future. | [campus life]: restrictions are a part of college life. you will lack experience of many things due to this. you are not allowed to go out after 7. girls have a long list of restrictions which makes a person feel chained.

[college infrastructure]: infrastructure is designed to meet both academic and extracurricular needs. each department has well-maintained labs and research centers that allow students to gain hands-on experience. the computer labs are updated with fast internet and the latest software. the central library is a peaceful space, equipped with a mix of physical books and e-learning resources. hostel facilities are secure, with clean rooms and a hygienic mess. | [academics]: faculty are known for their teaching quality and dedication. they focus not just on delivering the syllabus but also on helping students develop problem-solving and critical thinking skills. many professors involve students in research projects and encourage them to present at national-level conferences. guest lectures and industry interactions are organized frequently, giving students exposure to practical applications of their studies. | [placements]: placements are considered among the best in tamil nadu. it giants and product-based companies both recruit in large numbers. the average salary package is about ?5 lpa, with many students landing roles at ?10 lpa and above. microsoft, amazon, oracle, and zoho are some of the dream companies that visit the campus. the placement training programs focus on coding, aptitude, and soft skills, ensuring students perform well in interviews.

review_text: [college infrastructure]: college infrastructure a german architectured, design of the campus is very cool, there is a very big building, there is a separate administrative block, there are separate blocks for each department, classrooms are well architecture with high speed wi-fi facility, smart board projector are fixed laboratories, and seminar hall, there is very cleanness. | [academics]: there is a strict focus on the academic by the college, because from the past 8 year the college has maintained its position under the top college for result, i am doing my btech in mechanical engineering, and i am now in 2nd year, the curriculum is updated and syllabus is also updated from my first year the 'k' series for the syllabus was introduced the very first time, call is not only make you job ready but also they make you able to stand in the corporate world. | [placements]: outstanding placement highest package was 27 lakh per annum it was from our senior batch, and most of the student get 10 lakh rupees package per annum, and at the last the average packages 3 to 4 lakh per annum, total hundred percent student get placed so there is not any minimum package, college supportive, placement is not easy and pleasant if you do not work well. | [campus life]: college campus is very good everything is a best feature according to me but one thing is very bad interactive activities are less majorly you will see here is a fresher party and farewell party apart from this there are technical fest only organised by the societies and clubs of the college, which will make you zestful sometime.

review_text: the fee in the college is totally based on the categorie and it is the best opportunity to study at an affordable fee an institute like the national institute of technology mizoram. the fee is catogree-wise distributed as if i attach some data for gen agree and family income is above 5 lahks then the fee for 6 sem is 67300/- for the student family income between 1-5 lakh per year the feel is 25683/- for 6 sem and for the student having family income less the 1 lakh per annum then it will be 4850/- all benefits will be avoided as per the income certificate issue by min tehsildar or collector of there district. scholarship is provided by the govt by registering them on nptl portal and applicable students can get the scholarship as per the govt norms.as the campus is still temporary in the main city aizwal bt the campus is quite beautiful and greenish all around even the entire aizwal is full of greenness. annual fest is khus aamdeed, the library is quite good i say it provides every needed book by different author and journals are also there and daily newspaper in a different language. classrooms are good enough as per the temporary campus classroom are fully furnished and attached with smart technology sports facility is also good and every sem there is a sports fest organized for inter and intra nit. some linkedin and social media societies are there which are been operated by students to give the perspective of their branch and different opportunity and competitions.

[college infrastructure]: the infrastructure of our college is excellent. our college is wi-fi enabled. it has good environment. the food served in the mess is best. they serve non-vegetarian food items. they serve snacks evening time | [academics]: our teachers are friendly . they teach us programming languages and other subjects very well. if we work hard, we can easily pass any subject without backlog. the teaching quality was excellent and pass percentage was 90% | [placements]: the highest salary package offered is 15 lpa. the lowest salary package offered is 3 lpa. students have to find internships by themselves. we have applied to the aicte platform. they provide in top mnc companies

[college infrastructure]: infrastructure is good at chitkara . all necessary equipments are available. classrooms are equipped with acs except for one block. projectors are available in every classroom. wifi is also available in computer labs . all these are well maintained libraries are equipped with 5-6 desktops , books and is spacious. square one is a food corner building fully air conditioned and neat and clean with hygienic food .campus is also green and clean. | [academics]: i am pursuing be cse and i am studying it because of my interest in coding. quality of teaching is outstanding. yes curriculum is updated every semester and for every new batch based on demanding technologies . yes what i study makes me job reliable . | [placements]: placement is 100% here until student leaves the job on his / her own choice . highest salary package was around 30 lakhs in cse. average package is 4-5 lakhs. i am not placed yet so dont know about placement process . i am in third year. | [campus life]: campus life is good. its best features are : neat n clean campus, classrooms, parks etc. fights and brawls here are less due to strictness. sports complex is very big with many sports available. food corner is best and cheap.drawbacks: u have to cone in uniform. fines for violations are very high. strictness towards study is much. mass bunks are not allowed.many saturdays are not off here. schedule becomes hectic for students with many exams 4 fas 3 sts 1 end term per sem.

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the hostel has all the basic facilities including a washing machine, water cooler, and other basic amenities. meal quality is also very good. the registration process for the hostel starts on the first day of arrival and it's quite affordable. | [academics]: i've chosen this course as it is a circuital branch hence a lot of scope in terms of internship and placements. faculties and tas are very helpful also they are very good at doubt-solving. the student-faculty ratio is around 50:1. | [placements]: campus placements are really awesome in our college, 23.8 lpa is an overall average package. for the electrical department, 25 lpa is the average. many big companies come to our college like google, microsoft, atlassian, etc. | [campus life]: some of the annual fests are anwesha, celesta, infinito, tedx, and many more. books are available in the physical library as well as in the digital library. some of the social for coding, and many more.groups are hoot club, njack for coding, and many more.

the fees here for my course i.e. bsc clinical psychology are comparatively less as compared to the other courses. the total fee for 3 years is 3.5 lakhs. the college provides scholarships based on the marks scored in the 12th standard as follows: 75%-87.9% = 25% scholarship, 88%-92.9%= 50% scholarship, above 93%= 100% scholarship, no internship is provided by the college.i chose this course because of my interest in psychology. the faculty here was good in the beginning but now it is average. the student-to-faculty ratio is almost 1:20. the faculties here are well-qualified but don't have much experience in teaching. the exams are neither very difficult nor very easy, we have to study everything to score decent marks

[college infrastructure]: srm is one of the renowned colleges in terms of infrastructure and facilities. it contains a variety of facilities like outdoor gymnasium, sport fields, basketball courts, and courts for lawn tennis as well as badminton. apart from this, it also has state of the art laboratories with smart classrooms. the hostel facilities are good and clean. mess food is pathetic in terms of taste but is hygienic. | [academics]: the quality of teaching is really upsetting because no practical knowledge is provided. only bookish knowledge is given. curriculum followed is an old one and we hardly get to know recent trends in the market. by attending only classroom lectures, you can never be job ready. | [placements]: srm provides great placement opportunities but you need to know how to grab them as well. core companies do visit but in very limited numbers. placements are dominated by software companies. irrespective of whichever branch you are from, you need to learn coding to get a good offer. average salary offered are between 3-4 lpa while the highest is around 30 lpa. core companies offer somewhere around 4-5 lpa. the college is also very supportive during the placements. | [campus life]: campus life in srm is awesome. you can't deny that ever. with its national level fests, inter college competitions, technical and non-technical clubs, srm stands out from its competitors in this genre. especially the wonderful campus that it beholds with lush green greenary, one can have the best time of his/her life. safety is seen seriously, and we the students never feel unsafe once inside the campus. the college also takes good measures to handle emergencies.

review_text: the annual cultural fest is moksha held in march every year. it is a 3-day event held with lots of competitions, events as well as cultural nights. the tech-fest is resonance held in october every year. the books are well available in the library. classrooms are well-ventilated and well-furnished. sports and extracurricular are well supported. there are various societies for students as per their interests.i chose this course because i was interested in the computer science and engineering department. the faculty: student ratio is nearly around 1:10. the faculties are well qualified from prestigious institutes like iits. exams are held every 2 months and one can pass if he/she studies well.

[college infrastructure]: the facilities and infrastructure are fantastic here .the course is well designed, and it is as per the industry needs. there is lot of exposure to real world projects. ramiah institute of technology hostels are best in terms of living it?s very hygienic and also very decent. the canteen is the best part of the college the food served is very affordable and very delicious and of good quality. | [academics]: msrit faculties are very good there. proctor keep timely updates with guardians and students about academics and any other problems. best thing is one can clear any type of doubts from any faculty of that subject. msrit provides good quality education more subjects to learn more people to meet more opportunities to learn more chances of finding better placements and great academics for students to grow and learn to become a better human. | [placements]: placements in ramaiah institute of technology are good. almost every students of computer science and information science branches gets placed. the placement session generally starts from july 1 week, and companies visit until the end of the fourth year. the average salary package offered is around 8lpa. top recruiting companies here our morgan stanley, jpmc, paytm, phonepe, sap labs, cisco, american express, amazon, adobe etc. | [campus life]: msrit has a very good environment. if you will join msrit, you will be extremely privileged as msrit is in very close proximity to bel road which is about two minutes, rock from the college campus. you can enjoy your weekends, even weekdays in quality restaurants and more near the college.

[college infrastructure]: it is a 300 acre campus with all the necessary infrastructure, facilities and a few more are under construction.all the closed spaces are fully air conditioned and the wifi connectivity is fairly decent all over the campus.most of the classrooms are equipped with a projector,screen and a green/white board.everything is hygienic and well maintained except for the restrooms,sometimes.there is an indoor sports complex as well as outdoor courts. | [academics]: i am pursuing b.tech. in e.c.e. .no specific reason,i had pcm in my 12th therefore i went with the most conventional option.the quality of teaching is fairly decent.the faculty is highly qualified from reputed institutions.personally speaking classroom learning hadn't been great having said that the profs are approachable you can meet them in person for any difficulties faced during the session.the curriculum is upto date. the multi disciplinary system enables your to choose the subjects. | [placements]: from resume building to mock interviews cdc guides you through the placement preparation.according to the stats 85% of the batch of 2020 got placed with an average package of 7.5 lpa ,the highest package of 31.5 lpa in 2019 and is mostly bagged by computer science majors with exceptional skillset. placements happen in two levels;a written test followed by an interview.the college has over 320 placement partners as of 2020.placement preparatory sessions are conducted department wise. | [campus life]: there are clubs of different genres like cultural,social,technical and sports you can apply to any of these or start a club of your own.there's an active external participation in the events organised in delhi ncr region.various other events keep happening.it is unsafe to travel alone late in the evenings.railway station, bus station are comparatively close by than the airport.you need special permission to travel during odd timings it's tricky but can seek wardens' help during emergencies.

review_text: [college infrastructure]: iit kharagpur is having one of the best infrastructure of all colleges. the campus is in coordination with the nature. we even have lakes and a beautiful canopy. as a kgpian i can proudly say that we have the largest campus which is really hard to maintain but all the halls, library, auditoriums, classroom complex, sports complex are well equipped and well maintained and are so hygienic. the campus has the wi-fi network available all around the campus. we even have a national digital library. | [academics]: i'm a first year undergraduate student currently pursuing mechanical engineering at iit kharagpur. our curriculum has recently got updated and new subjects like diy, es and english lab were added which are very necessary in this competitive society. the faculty has tons of experience in their respective field and are always ready to help students out. | [placements]: as a mechanical engineer graduate at iit kharagpur i can proudly say that this year the maximum number offers have been made to mechanical engineering(122) which is followed by ece. and most of the offers were made to dual degree candidates followed by mtech students and then to btech students. | [campus life]: a dream come true . academically and extra- curricula wise, there are lot of things to explore out here . you can find an amazing peer group coming from every corner of the nation. in a span of 4 years we not only learn our respective course but also sustaining with people of different culture, skills and personality. we have many clubs and societies which help us improve our communication skills, leadership qualities and team coordination.

[college infrastructure]: the college has overall a good infrastructure with labs having all the necessary equipment, sports fields, library, hostels, clinic, water treatment plant and lecture theatres. they are all well maintained. | [academics]: cse branch has a great curriculum covering topics like data structures, analysis of algorithms, database management and much more. there are many options for program and other electives such as machine learning, computer security, cloud computing, etc.. these topics can surely make a student job ready. apart from computer sciences, there are subjects like physics, value ethics, economics which i believe is important for a person's growth and understanding. | [placements]: the placements are quite nice with students securing both on campus and off campus jobs in mncs like google, amazon and microsoft. the institution has its own training and placement cell which helps students in preparation for interviews and recruitment rounds. average package is around 9-10 lpa of all branches with 11 lpa for cse alone. placement percentage is around 92-94%. | [campus life]: i haven't been able to visit and experience the campus life due to covid scenario but as far as i can tell from what the seniors have told us, it is beautiful and we all equally crave returning to the campus. the greenery is quite soothing with the infrastructure. we have clubs for all kinds of activities. more information can be found on the website, but one minor disadvantage is that it's a little far from the city. although we have buses for transportation from and to the city.

pros/cons: i like the green environment around me as there are around 1500 tress within the campus. the faculties that accompany as are very skilled and very interactive the campus provides good placement oppurtunities in all the fields one of the thing that id ont like int he campus is the fees for my course is high. i dont think so all the students out there can afford it. i dont like the penalty system that is been implemented on the students who fail to pay the fees within the given duration. as i have completed one year in this college i felt there is some religion based descrimination within the campus, which i do not like. | placement experience: for placements students are eligible from 5th semester. there are number of companies coming for placements, as i am still in third sem i dont have much idea about it. the highest package got in our college was 15 lpa.
