life at campus depends mainly on u. there are 2 annual fests i.e. kannada habba and 8th mile. there are many books available in the library related to the course. classrooms are smart classes. sports are also there. like there is a cricket ground, basketball court, badminton court etc. there are about 20 clubsthe placements at the college are very great. every year big companies come to go to the college like google, qualcomm, accenture, goldman sachs etc. and they have opportunities to almost every branch and the placement committee is also very helpful

[college infrastructure]: infrastructure of college is good.very good facilities are available for our college. wi-fi, labs, library, and all necessary facilities are provided to the students. the quality of food facilities in my hostel is good. medical and sports facilities are also available for students and staff. | [academics]: teachers are helpful and qualified. their teaching quality is also good. the curriculum of my course is relevant and students are always ready for industrial visits. the semester in my course is easy and the pass percentage of class and college is more than 90%. | [placements]: college placements are good.students of our placed in good companies in india are more than 80%. and the highest package offered was 2 lacs per month. top companies in india like mahindra and tata are hiring students from our college.many students also selected for internships.

[college infrastructure]: the college has good infrastructure, facilities.the classrooms are in good condition with fans.labs are well equipped and maintained.the entire area and surrounding of college is clean and hygienic. there is sports club and dance room as well. | [academics]: the academics is very rigorous with a lot of practical learning. exams are conducted twice every year and are pretty difficult to crack. the societies and clubs are very good and they provide a lot of exposure. | [placements]: the quality of placements is good.if you are skilled and complete the eligibility for placements,then you can get good placements in big companies.the average salary you can get is 3.5lpa.the placement process is easy and the college is supportive.

there is an annual fest called bharat dhwani celebrated in october. and also there is a technical fest called ukash. there are also some big and small events. there is a decent library in nit jalandhar you can find many books related to your course and there are also many journals and novels in the library. all the class is a centralized air conditioning system, and there are also one projector and one smart board in every lecture hall. there are many sports clubs like athletics, football, volleyball, etc they also organize inter-branch, inter-hostel tournaments. ncc, nso, and nss are also the main clubs there. there is a debate club where you can enhance your communication skills. there is also a prayaas society where students volunteer and teach the local kids. zeal society is also one of the famous societies.i got this course due to my rank in jee mains exam. and specifically choose this branch because it is a circuital branch and also there is programming culture involved in the curriculum like c language, dsa, etc. in the main subject we got 2 teachers, one is the main teacher who takes classes and the other takes the tutorial sessions for doubt solving. in lab classes, we get again 2 teachers. all the faculty is ph.d. qualified and also involved in many types of research. there are 3 exams in a semester 2 minors and one end sem there is almost 1.5 months gap in each exam.
college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the campus infrastructure is so good , i totally love it . it is all the facilities , there is a even a stadium for the games and the class rooms are also so good . and there is a swimming pool and other facilities also there . | [academics]: the quality of the education is really appreciable in the college . so many talented teachers are there , if any doubts are there they will clarify us clearly and teachers also helps in personality building. | [placements]: every year 90 percent of the students got selected in the campus placements . our college provides the job offers from numerous reputed companies and in addition to that internships also . the package also pretty good.

there are 4 major events in our college - pantheon, bitotsav, vajra and hack-a-bit. pantheon is the annual technical fest, bitotsav is the annual cultural fest, vajra is the annual sports meet and hack-a-bit is the annual hackathon. they are organized with great pomp and splendour and eminent bollywood personalities perform in the closing nights of these fests.the course curriculum has been updated recently to match the recent cbcs system of education. in the computer science stream, modern topics of machine learning and artificial intelligence have been added. the exam structure is very rigid. we get grades and they are used to calculate our cgpa and sgpa. there is no relative marking.

[college infrastructure]: akgec's infrastructure is quite good, maintained and is in continuous growth. all classrooms have fans and labs have fans but ac is only in computer labs. there is a huge central library, wifi speed is quite good and is unlimited. although college is always clean but boys hostals are not up to the mark. | [academics]: most of the teachers are excellent in teaching and academics is good except some. but it is satisfactory. they will fill you up with all the theory required but be to job-ready you have to do some extra effort on your own. one thing i don't like this college is they mentally force is to attend classes. we should have our own free will to attend classes. | [placements]: placement is good, if you work hard enough along with t&c department there is high chances of campus placement. they also provide pdp classes for our personality development. average campus placement package is 3-4 lakh but students also get 5 lakhs and 8 lakhs.
college_name: Ajay Kumar Garg Engineering College Ghaziabad

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the college's campus is very huge and the infrastructure is astonishing to look at. the college is near the highway so most of the things are easily available to you due to better connectivity with the city and market. | [academics]: the academics is supported by very well-experienced teachers so that if you're willing to do research it will surely help you in your future endeavors and betterment. it gives you great experience practically and theoretically as well. | [placements]: the placements are so great for the cs students that they should join it without any second thought because it offers you great and immense opportunities in the field of cs. for other branches, it is average.
college_name: SRM Institute of Science and Technology Chennai

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the infrastructure at nsut is very impressive and meets needs of student faculty. the college offers lush green campus well maintained facilities like running track canteen library student activity centre and a beautiful temple | [academics]: academics of nsut is manageable , professors are good , they give a lot of assignment .f you are attending their lecture properly , then you just need to prepare for exams since most of the syllabus will be completed by then in the class , most importantly they will tell you important points. | [placements]: placement of nsut is at par ...it is by far considered one of the top engineering college competing with iits, nits ,this year the average placement was 20 lpa.many prominent companies came to nsut this year like google , apple.
college_name: Netaji Subhas University of Technology New Delhi

review_text: pros/cons: studies are good here. some faculty members teach good and some do not teach that much good. no auditorium, sports complex in the college. proper facilities aren't there for playing games which are supposed to be played in indoor courts like badminton. | placement experience: i think students from 3rd year are eligible for campus placements but i am not sure about it. companies like amazon, deloitte, tcs etc visited our college for placements and internships. after getting the degree i plan to do masters in abroad.
college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology Sri City - [IIIT], Sri City

review_text: [college infrastructure]: overall interest structure of university delhi ncr campus is really what i am well said aspire with the type of buildings they are the times the marvels projector ac fans those the use furniture is also really good | [academics]: i am studying bachelors of computer application from christ university delhi ncr campus chosed it because i wanted to do something in computer background computer science background quality of the teaching from this campus is not satisfied according to me | [placements]: replacement of christ university delhi ncr campus is really bad it is new campus so the companies that visit over very few my number and almost only 2 to 3 students get placed every year so it is below average

it's an iit.indian institute of technology (iit) bhilai was established in the state of chhattisgarh by ministry of human resource and development in the year 2016.iit bhilai is presently housed in its transit campus at government engineering college (gec) raipur, chhattisgarh.very nice. despite being a new iit, it had all that one can expect. amazing student culture and lots of sports and cultural activities are organised regularly. clubs for all types of arts and technologies are also there.
college_name: IIT Bhilai - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB]

review_text: i choose this course because i was interested in that field and also keen to learn new things. the ratio of faculty and students was 4:1 which is quiet good. the professors were quite well qualified and had good knowledge plus teaching methods. there were 2 mid-semester and 1 end-semester exam. exams were moderate to difficult.companies visit the campus in the 6th semester of b.tech. and many companies visited the campus for around 4 months like google, microsoft, zomato, amazon, capgemini, unacademy, etc. the highest package was 55 lakhs and the average package is 12 lakhs. around 92% of students got placed.
college_name: IIT Jodhpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITJ]

review_text: [college infrastructure]: yes, all facilities are well maintained. the living space is clean and hygiene. 24*7 library facility plus wifi . the entire campus is bliss to eyes. food courts in campus, garden for refreshment and sports n gym in each campus . | [academics]: amazing set of teacher , who are there for you both online or offline way. professors are well educated and experienced. regular quiz n assignment for better understanding. curriculum is updated and according to latest ugc norms. | [placements]: placement scenario is excellent in cse branch. the highest package of this year approx. 29 lakh pa. and average is around 6-5 lakh pa. around 140 companies participating in placement with 4000 job offers.
college_name: Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology Bhubaneswar

review_text: there are lots of fest like the biggest fest is sanskriti(9 days), and also we have ciippus. our fresher's event and also we have a tech fest called srijan. where anyone can perform and show their talent in tech, robotics, etc. also, we have an individual departmental fest..so you will enjoy it a lot. the library is also good. we have 2 libraries 1 is located at the jadavpur campus the other is in the salt lake campus. also, there are lots of opportunities for sports and extracurricular activities. like -photography, music, dance, acting, etc. there are almost all the clubs are available like photography clubs, dance clubs, music clubs, mountaineering clubs, coding clubs, etc.the fee structure is too cheap. you can complete your 4 years of a bachelor of engineering for only 11000 rs. for it department it's around 30k rs. if a student still can't afford this. that's why we have free ship. also, you can get svmcm scholarship(60k per year) if you are eligible. also, we have scholarships for sc, st, obc. now come to the placement. the average package is around 7-8lpa. you have many opportunities as you can go to the it sector, core field, and consultancy companies like pwc. also government jobs if you are interested.

being a 1st year student i haven't had an internship yet, but seeing the seniors we know that some tech giants come to the college for internships and overall placement rate is pretty good. though most of the companies are tech based so it's mostly beneficial for the electrical departments, but you should know one thing that this is the 2nd best college in india for civil engineer after iit kgp.the faculty is pretty good with a sufficient student to professor ratio. most of the professors hold a phd degree and many research scholar work under them. though not excellent but the faculty is getting better with the passing time provided that the college has been recently given the status of an ini, it's getting better day by day.
college_name: Indian Institute of Engineering Science and Technology - [IIEST] Shibpur

review_text: [college infrastructure]: yes, college have all the necessary infrastructure and facilities. wifi will be available in the hostels, projectors are available in the classrooms. food is hygienic and the spaces are clean. all the classes are well planned and well maintained. | [academics]: i am studying b.tech in computer science and the every professor is qualified with the phd. the curriculum will keep on updating for every few years and this will not make that much job ready for job you have to prepare on your own. | [placements]: placements are quite decent. around 80% people get placed every year. highest package would be 1cr. avg salary offered will be 5lpa. the placement process is not that easy. college is supportive in get you a right job. | [campus life]: there are many events will be happening in the campus which every one can participate. the environment is quite decent in the campus.
college_name: Vellore Institute of Technology Chennai Campus

review_text: [college infrastructure]: christ university has all the necessary infrastructure, facilities and equipment required. it has multiple lifts in all the blocks. the classrooms are well equipped with a screen projector and white boards, with ample seating space. | [academics]: mba in christ follows the trimester pattern, which is more hectic than the usual semester pattern. curriculum is changed from year to year which makes it updated and helpful to the current batch. quality of teaching is excellent. | [placements]: my seniors have an average placements of 9lpa. the placements is still on going for the current batch. mutiple internships oppurtunites are provided which can be converted into pre placement offers during placements. | [campus life]: numerous events keep happening at the campus.

pros/cons: the faculty are good theyare well educated all are phd holders the ambience and the nature of the college is awesome and is in more than 700 acera the ratio of boys is more than girls and students come here from many countries and states i didn't like the food here need to improve a little bit same food and where ever u go there is a charge if 15 rs it's a good college fees is also good enough | placement experience: u will get eligible for placement from the 6 sem and the most of the companies are top companys like google microsoft phonepay razorpay and etc and the highest record breaking package is 3cr and recentl the highest package was 2.5 cr and the average pacakege is around 10 lakh per annum all of them from this college get 100% placement guarantee and i have plans to do cds afcat after my degree

the fee of b.tech cse is 120000 per semester if no scholarship is applicable. and will vary according to scholarship. the scholarship is more for other branches of b.tech. approx. 74400 is the fee for the slot 1 scholarship.from the 6th semester the students are applicable to sit in the placement there are a lot of companies visit to lpu. but most of them are mass recruiters. approx. 90% of students get placed every year at the university.

[college infrastructure]: talking about dtu overall, the college have have enough resources for every one in college right there including infrastructure for events, classes, faculties, and equipments for every practical lab(but some equipments doesn't work).now for mechanical it have all laboratories you need for your degree including softwares for designing, analysing your designs,etc. coming to faculty, they are willing to help those to the fullest,who wants to learn new things from them.otherwise they good in classes | [academics]: i am studying mechanical engineering. some teachers are pretty amazing and teach you with intrest giving real life examples.but few teachers just come read and go. curriculum is somewhat that needs an update as it is the same the have been taught for years now. mechanical engineering syllabus is extensive so it gives you an idea about all the sectors of core jobs in the industry but your specialisation is your in your field of interest subject in which you want to get a job. | [placements]: placement for mechanical is somewhat okish. but their is a good news, you can sit in placements for other roles in college too. almost everyone is placed in various of job roles. because many different types of recruiters come to college. be it as big as amazon, microsoft or for mechanical like bajaj,benz,etc. the average offer made in college is 15 lpa which is preety good. | [campus life]: campus life is what that make dtu stand apart from other colleges. it got fests, good campus. good library. societies that are best of their kind when it comes to college circuit. you can join society of your interest like dancing, singing,drama, or be it a very good sports teams of dtu you can have them all.or just chill at oat and play in sports complex now and then. teachers support those who wants to learn from them. location is good every transport options is available from here.

[college infrastructure]: the college have all the necessary infrastructure and equipment. it is one of the best college for engineering in the country. the library in the college is best. the classroom have all the facilities which includes smarboards and wifi. the living space is really clean and food here is hygienic. | [academics]: i studied mtech in mechanical engineering from here because of my interest in the field of engineering. the quality of teaching here is best. the curriculum updated every year and all this factor makes you job ready. i work for government after the course. | [placements]: the quality of placement is best in our college. almost every got placed in the company they desire. the average package was near 9-12 lpa and highest was 30lpa. the placement process is easy and pleasant. the college give all the support in placing .

[college infrastructure]: the campus is very big. it has all the facilities. it has the best library and four academic blocks. it is full of greenery. the hostels are air conditioned. we have the biggest gym in the country called marena. apart from that we have student plaza where we get everything. | [academics]: we have frequent tests. good online portal to conduct tests frequently. we have some of the best faculty in the department. we have epad to write our exams which is super sustainable. the curriculum is well upgraded. we have student projects which include manas, rugved, etc. these student projects make real time machines and robots. | [placements]: i'm still a first year in the college so im not well equipped to give good insights about the placement scenario. but from what i have heard from my seniors the placements are pretty good. our college's highest package is 54 lakhs. | [campus life]: our campus life is the best. our campus is a prime location it is super close to beaches and waterfalls. manipal has hired zolo scholar to organise our events around our campus and take care of the hostels. they do an excellent job.

[college infrastructure]: well, you may know iiit vadodara is established in 2013 and doesn't have its own campus till now. then where are classes going? our classes are going at gec gandhinagar (government engineer college). where are our hostels? our current hostel is in sabar institute of technology which is around 30 km from gandhinagar. for permanent campus we have to wait for 5 to 6 years. | [academics]: teaching quality is good because professors are experienced. our curriculum is updated and include all the recent developments in the field. we have good coding culture. everyone try to compete with each. we have coding club and all other clubs. | [placements]: placements are outstanding. last year highest package from our institute was about 15 lakh/yrs and average package was 6 lakh/yrs. placements in this year 2019-2020 are way more surprising, because the highest package offered is 36 lakh/yrs and average package is 11.9 lakh/yrs with 100% placement statistics.
college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology Vadodara

review_text: [college infrastructure]: quite old campus but the new academic building are built well, and the hostels overall are also quite solid. nothing to complain about and safety is quite good with proper grounds and facili so quite satisfied with the infrastructure | [academics]: very exhausting academic pattern and excessive and rigid grading system and a lot of workshops and labs with very strict attendance policies. | [placements]: alumni network is gold but placements avg only good for computer branches for other branches is satisfactory overall a good placement rate. | [campus life]: the best campus life for sure | [anything else]: campus hostel restrictions are a bit mismanaged

in the first year , if u are able to find any internship based on your skill. also you will got 1.5 month holidays between your 1st year ending and 2nd year starting .at that time you can avail for your internship. there are some specialisation which offers confirm internship to everyone student.campus life is good and enjoyable. you can explore lot of things here and your experience with be amazing. all the books are available in collage in library. there are lot of clubs which organisation different activities actively.

[college infrastructure]: infrastructure is great they have library, square one, badminton court,football ground, cricket ground and every thing a campus must have. it is very clean just that the food in not so good but still better than other colleges. | [academics]: academics are great . great teacher for every subject.just they focus on problem solving less in the early years of the course which they are improving now recently. uca batch is great with coding and get more attension. | [placements]: placements are great for this college 100% placements. it is like ocean of opportunities. you will get a lot of opportunities.if you are in uca you can expect great company. even non uca has great opportunities
college_name: Chitkara University Institute of Engineering and Technology Jhansla

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the department has a state-of-art infrastructure pertaining to its labs. various research facilities are available along with the iit delhi supercomputer for major computational work which was used in drug discovery for covid. the campus has a good wi-fi speed with airconditioned and technically equipped classrooms and one of the best libraries in the country. iit delhi boasts a great hostel culture with inter-hostel games and hostel festivals. mess food is good and eatable. | [academics]: teachers of the departments are most qualified in their respective fields. these are well-trained faculty members with international relations and good quality research outcomes and citations. the curriculum is rigorous starting from basic biology to bioinformatics and genomics. exams at iit delhi are conducted thrice in a year known as minor 1, minor 2, and majors. the evaluation is spread across quizzes, minors, majors, and lab activity. the pass percentage is 30% generally. | [placements]: almost everyone who opts for placement is placed in sectors ranging from core to non-core, from consulting and finances to the technical and analytics. many students opt for higher studies and most get admissions at top universities in and around the world like the ivies. the startup culture is also making its way with 4 startups from the 2019 batch of biotechnology alone. the highest salary package offered is around 30 lpa and the lowest salary package offered is around 5 lpa-6 lpa.

[college infrastructure]: college infrastructure is good with facilities. classrooms are big enough along with projectors and smart boards. central library is filled with books from all engineering courses. it also has a large reading room. labs are good but a little small for a institute level lab. hostels are separately made available for boys and girls. canteen serves lunch and snacks at the campus. | [academics]: all the teachers have a doctorate degree in their expertise. most of them allow the students to interact with faculty during the class. there are two class tests and an end-semester examination in each semester. | [placements]: placements have been good last year but only in non core companies as none of the core companies visit the campus in recent times. most of the jobs offered are for roles of programmer, software developer, data analyst by the it companies. average package offered was 5-6 lpa.

[college infrastructure]: the facilities and infrastructure available in our course and department are best and maintained neatly, and classrooms are kept clean. each classroom and lab is provided with wi-fi and has a well-stocked library. the facilities in our hostel are excellent | [academics]: the teaching faculty is very well experienced, the course curriculum is relevant to the subject and there are many industrial visits to the students for easy understanding of the subject. the semester exams are held in a very strict manner and the pass percentage is 90 percent. | [placements]: our college has best placement opportunities and most companies come forward to hire the students. the package range starts from 8lacs to 55lacs per annum and companies such as accenture, wipro, deloitte, etc. and many students got internships at amazon, flipkart, etc. and the roles offered are intern and developer.

[college infrastructure]: class rooms are well equipped ducts, wifi, smart board. they are big and spacious. the mess food is hygienic and tastes okayish. library was enough when the strength was low. now with a batch of 500+ each. they have to allocate classrooms as study area. hostels are clean and the rooms are well equipped with ducts. in boys hostle some of the students get the opportunity to live in single rooms from first year onwards however with increase in batch strength this may change. | [academics]: exam questions are similar to gate questions. teaching is not that good though. have to refer online videos (nptel, udemy, coursera, youtube) to understand the concept. labs are very good if you actually study. grading system is hard. if you dont study you will definitely get a fail/backlog in that subject. they have recently added artificial intelligence, data science , machine learning and many other industry required skills in their curriculum. yes subjects taught were useful for placements. | [placements]: placememts are very good. average package: 11.89 lpa (batch 2020). highest package: 58 lpa; median package: 10 lpa. more than 50% have 10lpa + package. visit lnmiit official placement site for more information. | [campus life]: best 4 years. we had regular coding contests by coding club cybros in every 2-3 weeks which helped a lot in enhancing coding skills. quiz club, literary club, had a lot a cultural activities to have fun which helped a lot to overcome stage fear. cultural activities drama, fashion walk, dance, weekly monthly drawing fun activities like thumb painting, charcoal drawing , sponge painting, face painting and many more. there were debates organized by our literary club. many fun quizzes.
college_name: LNM Institute of Information Technology Jaipur

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the infrastructure of the university a big plus point. the campus size is 68 acres, which is not fully functional. the classrooms are advanced and fully air conditioned with podiums, projectors and fingerprint attendance. the library is tech savvy too. the hostels although fully air conditioned have small rooms and communal washroom. sports complex has a gym, swimming pool and various courts for squash, basketball and crickets. | [academics]: academics in general is good, with peers ranging from competitive to lax. the general expectation from faculty is that they are at best going to provide excellent guidance and feedback. most of them are iit graduates or former teachers there. the curriculum of the university is the major reason i decided to choose this university. we also have a bennett hatchery where like minded people from different disciplines come together to work on startup ideas. | [placements]: according to the latest placement stats of 2017-2021, 2 students bagged a package of 30 lakhs from amazon, another for 28 lakhs. there were some students who got placed for 25lakhs at service now and others for 20 lakhs at comm vault. average package was around 6.59 lakhs for btech cse. i am not sure if the placements were off or on campus but some good companies do visit campus. the placement cell is active and provides us regular guidance. | [campus life]: campus life is very vibrant clubs and event occur all year long. other than that they have fests like zenevia and euphoria. there's not much gender segregation and the campus is safe. the students are required to be in their hostels by 11 pm though. and please don't be a day scholar here. just not worth it, the college makes sure to make them enough uncomfortable so that they have no option but to join hostel.

being in first year, i don't know that much but i have heard from my seniors that the course curriculum is outdated. many of the faculty members are not as good. however, few of them are quite good. most of what you learn here is from your friends and classmates. its not academically very rigorous, which gives students time to pursue their interests and see which field suits you the most. our college also gives a great industry exposure. many alumni of our college are working in top mncs like google, facebook, amazon, etc. some of our alumni are now ias, ips. some are doing mba from from iims. some doing research(number of these are few). founder of paytm is an alumni of dtu.you can easily get a loan. there is an sbi bank inside the campus, making it more easier for you. also there is a need based scholarship, that you can apply in first and third year. but this scholarship cover only your tuition fees. it will not cover the hostel fees as well as the maintenance fees(not sure about maintenance fees). also there are few other merit based scholarship, but generally the amount that one gets of such scholarships is low.

[college infrastructure]: the college infrastructure is old, the lecture halls except the nab are kind of run down, and the college itself is in a desperate need of a renovation. the boy's hostels are also run down. the food is ok but the hygiene is kind of bad. | [academics]: the academic structure of the college is very versatile, being a first year its kind of overwhelming at first but its designed to help you grow different areas, especially incorporating communication skills into the curriculum is a great step. | [placements]: placements are awesome. the average package of this year for electrical is 15.2 lpa and for cse it is 24.7 lpa, and for ece it is 18.3 lpa. highest package (off campus) for electrical is 1.5 cpa and highest package (on campus) is 64 lpa for electrical and 84 lpa for cse. | [campus life]: the campus life is quiet. 150 acres of campus and only 50 acres is in use, rest is the residential area, in short the college doesnt have much to offer if you want to roam around a big campus. and whatever you do, don't join drams.

[college infrastructure]: college infrastructure of our university is good. buildings are well build. laboratories are equipped with the necessary equipments. they tries to provide as much facility as they can. but only hostels are equipped with wifi there is no facility of wifi in college. but overall its good. | [academics]: academics is average. as you are now a grown up child and at this point you have to study by yourselves so the academics doesn't bother you much though some of the teacher in the institutions are good while some just come to take salary they provide us with notes and leave. but some of them works on you and make you job ready! | [placements]: placements is the plus point of the university. university has special department for placement which work their best to get you placed. they try to bring every possible mnc they can. so its upto you how you study and get placement. average package from my stream goes from 5-6 lacks per annum. | [campus life]: campus life is good. students come from all across the india so you get to meet nice crowd. also some international students come here to study so you get to know about international culture too which is quite good. also the college is quite safe . security guards keep an eye on everyone.

fees per semester are nearly 1.2 lakhs for the general category whose family income is more than 5 lakhs. and for general category students with a family income between 1 to 5 lakhs and all obc students get a remission of 2/3 of tuition fees which are 1 lakh per semester. and whose family income is less than 1 lakh will get full remission of tuition fees and a 100 per cent tuition fee waiver for sc /st / pwd students. and there are lots of scholarship here for students.life on campus is fun but academics here at iitk are more difficult than at other iits. fests time is too much enjoyable there is a total of 3 fests but something keeps going on campus for a year. lecture hall has ac and a lot of places on campus to study. sports and other facilities are great and there are too many clubs, societies, and teams run by the student body to discover and live their passion.
college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]

review_text: [college infrastructure]: my collage have very good infrastructure many facilities like e-cell and other clubs are their for students grooming and co -curriculum activities and also have labs and different sports areas for sports lover. | [academics]: gla have best faculties for students, and their teaching style is very relevant and easy to understand for all types of students. and the curriculum is updated and relevant with respect to future needs. | [placements]: gla has like 90 percent palacements records many big companies and mncs hire students from the university with average package of 4lpa for ug. maximum packages go to the mba or btech students which is approx 36 lpa | [campus life]: collage campus is huge and have many buliding with different departments and clubs for singing , dancing , sports business , entrepreneurship and many more and also have well mentioned hostels for boys and girls.

[college infrastructure]: jaypee institute of technology, noida boasts modern labs and research facilities as it's best feature, providing a strong technical environment. the worst feature is the limited green space , which can make the campus feel less vibrant. | [academics]: jaypee institute of technology, noida, is known for its solid academic curriculum, especially in engineering, with knowledgeable faculty. however, teaching quality can vary by department. the college is generally supportive but may sometimes lack personalized guidance. | [placements]: at jaypee institute of technology, noida, the average placement package typically ranges between ?4-6 lpa, with higher averages in computer science and it. the highest salary packages can reach around ?30-40 lpa, usually offered by top tech companies or multinational corporations during campus placements. | [campus life]: campus life at jaypee institute of technology, noida, is vibrant with various cultural, technical, and sports activities. located in a well-developed area, it's generally safe with 24/7 security. the institute is equipped to handle emergencies, with medical facilities and quick access to nearby hospitals. | [anything else]: jaypee institute of technology, noida, also benefits from its proximity to delhi-ncr, providing students access to a range of professional opportunities and cultural experiences. the campus has modern amenities including hostels, a library, and recreational facilities. while the infrastructure is strong, some students may find the campus environment less green compared to other institutions. overall, it's a well-rounded choice for those seeking quality education in a dynamic setting.
college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology Noida

review_text: [college infrastructure]: my college have all the necessary infrastructure, facilities and equipment. the building is divided into three divisions for the ease of all the departments. the classrooms are well aired and spacy. every laboratory in our college has very good assistants and they maintain all the equipments. the canteen is clean and the food is hygienic. the living spaces are also clean. the campus lacks unlimited wi-fi usage and provides as low as 34mb jio-net to each phone number. there are no smart boards. | [academics]: i am studying b.tech in biotechnology. the quality of teaching is average as some facilities excels in their subject while others are average. yet, we have regular classes. the curriculum is well updated and includes most of the recent developments, yet also lacks a few compared to colleges in other states. yes, what i study makes me job-ready. | [placements]: our college, being a private one, has great chances of placements. almost 70-80 percent students are placed, while 10 percent of the students always opt for research in universities abroad. as far as i know the highest salary package offered was 16 lacs per annum, though i am not sure about the course. highest possibility of it being b.tech in engineering & architecture. average salary is around 4-5 lacs per annum. i am still not placed. | [campus life]: college campus is fairly big with 3 main structures: the cme, cb and ict, connected through foot connectors. the central building has a vast auditorium. beside the engineering building there is a vast space and in the corner is standing the mba building. there are two main playgrounds. for food, one can head to the canteen or the caf?teria, situated near the heritage academy. once, inside the campus, it never feels unsafe, but the location of the college is remoted from the main city.

the campus is lush green with good outdoor sports facilities. there are over 300 societies that conduct regular events and actively participate in the state to international-level of events. various sports competitions are held regularlyfees for the first year are 2,19,000 with a hike of a few percent every year. e-district and merit cum means scholarships are provided which can be availed by students to fund their degree. the internship starts at the end of 2nd year

[college infrastructure]: the infrastructure is one of my favorite things about the college.it is clean and spacious. the college has everything you need from a mini local shop to high tech lab. the wifi service is impeccable. | [academics]: i'm currently pursuing b.sc economics (data science).experienced staff and the curriculum is up to date. the syllabus is tough, but it is far better than the syllabus of all the other colleges having this degree. does this make you job ready?depends on the skills you attain other than the academics. | [placements]: since i am a second yr student i am not very aware of the placements . but i have to say that science major placements are not as attractive as engineering . the average package for my major was 6 lakhs with the highest package as 10 lakhs, which is a fraction of the engineering placements. it is a pity that our major is dominated and placements are less emphasised. | [campus life]: we have the most beautiful campus covering about 250 acres. it is lush and well maintained. we have leisure parks and 4 to 5 canteen inside the university itself. the mid day strolls are my favorite . the students are amazing and interactive. the staff are experienced and approachable. the location is safe and we have hcl clinic inside the campus for any emergencies with ambulance if the situation demands. | [anything else]: your college life will be worth it in shivnadar campus. we get to learn a lot and get first hand exposure. we have about 20 clubs and number of events all around the semesters. you will never be bored (even with exams ????) you get opportunities to stand out and show your talents . you will be able to handle anything and everything on your own. you will be ready to face challenges . with the right people and right mindset you will love shivnadar university!

pros/cons: college campus and the environment, availability of essential things sports and other activities had good infra | placement experience: the placement in colleges was good but the salary for the core job work was very less, maxing at 23000 at max, but the pay for other job roles was much higher, so getting a job from college was not difficult

[college infrastructure]: as n. i. t sikkim is a new established collage, so there are not all the equipment available. but maximum equipments are available. the infrastructure of n. i. t sikkim is good and living spaces are also clean. there are almost all the facilities available in the classroom. | [academics]: the academics are good in nit sikkim. teacher are having a good nature. they put their lots of effort in teaching to the students. the study of nit sikkim makes the student ready for job. yes, curriculum is updated and includes all the recent developments in the field. | [placements]: the placement of nit is really impressive. 90% students got intern from nit sikkim. minimum 70 percentage needs to sit in the exam of placement. the average salary that the companies will offer you that is 4lacks. | [campus life]: campus life is really good in nit sikkim because of the weather of ravangla. you will definitely enjoy the weather of college?. the location is neet , clean and green. the weather you like most here.

pros/cons: the crowd is decent here. you'll get both some who are good in study some not so good but overall the crowd is good the university is serious when it comes to academics. this can be a negative part for some but positive for some. you can sit for placement in the main campus too if you have good score in semesters. it opens more opportunity. the college is strict when it comes for academics. it feels sometimes you're in school. placement is decent. it can be improved i guess. | placement experience: the placement in our college is decent. the average is 6 lpa whereas the median is 9 lpa . the highest package offerd was 32 lpa but it can go more because the placement round is still going on for some companies.

[college infrastructure]: the infrastructure of college is good and well maintained, it has fully advanced classrooms and also have the smart library and also provide the hostel facility. there are many sports centers like cricket ground, basketball court and football ground, etc. | [academics]: as i talk about my personal experience about academics they have highly qualified and well disciplined faculty and the quality of teaching is next level, faculty are more helpful if you need any think about academics they will provide at any time. | [placements]: as i personally experienced the placement of bca there is less count of company came for placement in the campus, and the placement percentage is quite low in bca department, personal point of view the student have not to depend on the campus placement. | [anything else]: in this university the course fees for any course is decent.

[college infrastructure]: the college infrastructure , facilities and equipment of my college are very good . there is a library available in the university . the campus is wifi enabled and and the overall hygiene is really high . | [academics]: the faculty members are really experienced in their respective fields . workshops and guest lectures are organised to ensure industry readiness of students . there is a library available in the university . | [placements]: the placements in my college is good . every year 70 to 80 percent students are placed at the best companies . the college supports the students in the placement procedure. there is a dedicated placement cell. several trainings and guest lectures are conducted by the placement cell to help the students.
college_name: SRM Institute of Science and Technology Chennai

review_text: the highest package in our college for the 2023 batch is 1.20 cr per annum which is more than that of many iits and nit. average package stands at 11.2 lpa. which is good a figure to get. in 2023 a total of 191 companies visited for the placement drive. many students here are interested in higher studies in foreign countries.euphoria is our annual college fest. our university has a very big library with nearly 15000 books available offline we are also provided with an online library. all classrooms are fully centralised with a/c. our college encourages sports, we have a very big ground and indoor stadiums.

this field is the best in this university..there are soo many sports and clubs to choose from there is football, cricket, basketball, volleyball, kabbadi, literally every main sport is here..and soo many students participate also..there are many clubs as well..from management to coding to graphic designing etc.from the 3rd-semester students start to get internship opportunities. there is on-campus placement at 4th-year companies come and take students and give them an average package of 6 lakh per annum, i will also try to get an on-campus job as 90% of students to get on-campus jobs here

[college infrastructure]: iit delhi is one of the most well-maintained among all the iits. the campus size is almost 320 acres. there is excellent sports facilities like cricket, football, hockey, lawn tennis. the central library is there where students find ideal environment for studying. | [academics]: the curriculum & grading system at iit delhi is probably one of the most rigorous & strict among all the other collges in the nation. scoring a good cgpa is really very difficult as all the students are very talented & competitive. professors are really knowledgeable. | [placements]: iit delhi is one of the best in terms of placements in the entire nation. the placement process is also very rigorous & competitive and hectic. the process is conducted by ocs (offices & career services iit delhi) from 1-15 dec of every year. (in phase 1). phase 2 is concucted in the even semester.

[college infrastructure]: college facilities and infrastructure are upto the mark. infrastructure includes all the necessary facilities like electronics ,wifi, smart board, library, seminar hall, auditorium,canteen at every corner, basketball court. | [academics]: academic structure is good but sometimes labs are not available on time therefore practical exposure is less. faculty are always on time and utilise the lecture hours fruitfully. they provide hands on activities. | [placements]: placement is average as if i talk about management department, i've only seen or heard about marketing placements. there are very less opportunities for hr and finance domain. other than that many of our seniors have placed in a very reputed company. | [campus life]: it's very exploring.apart from studies,we get lots of exposure and also there are many fests, guest lectures and activities like ncc which makes the college life enjoyable. and there are many clubs which gives practical knowledge. | [anything else]: no

[college infrastructure]: college has superb infrastructure and facilities like a free library, air-conditioned classrooms and wi-fi on each floor of hostels. it also has an air-conditioned facility in hostel rooms. facilities we get in hostels are induction and fridge facility on each floor, unlimited wi-fi on each floor, air-conditioned hostel rooms, laundry and very clean washrooms. mess food is average, but the canteen facility is available, which serves very tasty food. medical facilities are available 24x7. | [academics]: teachers and staff members here are very helpful, qualified and knowledgeable. the teaching quality is excellent. the course curriculum is very relevant, and they update their syllabus every year according to the industry demands. it makes students industry-ready. | [placements]: roles like full-stack web development in mern and mean and android development in flutter entrepreneurship are offered here. the highest salary package offered here is more than 40 lpa, and the average salary package offered is 5 lpa. almost 60% of the students get placed in the 3rd year. the lowest salary package offered is around 3 lpa-4 lpa. top recruiting companies here are microsoft, adobe, facebook, etc. | [campus life]: there are many clubs in which students can participate like the dramatics club and students association and study circle, the debating society of pec, music club, quiz club, robotics club, rotaract club, the various editorial board.there are two main fests in our college that occur every year, named rangrezz and explore.
college_name: Chitkara University Institute of Engineering and Technology Jhansla

review_text: [college infrastructure]: iit roorkee is amazing. it does have great infrastructure. great library to study, hostels with gym, lawns, courts to play. plus, sports ground is huge, we have courts for basketball, tennis, badminton, volleyball, squash, etc. mess food is better. it's not that bad. the place is hygienic and posh. | [academics]: i am in product and industrial engineering branch. my branch have courses of mechanical and management department. anyone who wants to pursue for management, higher studies, this branch is good. some courses are little outdated, but we get to learn from them. | [placements]: we have decent placements in our college. highest package was offered to cse branch, average package is of 22lpa . in my college, we have placement and internship cell which handles this entire process. they help to make this process easy for students. | [campus life]: campus life is awesome in roorkee, especially in spring season.james thomason building, ece tower(at night), are main attraction of campus :)

[college infrastructure]: jaypee institute of information technology, noida has decent infrastructure with modern facilities, but the campus is not very huge and feels somewhat limited in space. classrooms are well-equipped with smart boards and wifi, and laboratories have sufficient equipment for academic work. the library is well-maintained with adequate books and digital resources, though seating space can feel limited during peak hours. | [academics]: i studied engineering at jaypee institute of information technology, noida, and my overall academic experience was quite good. the course curriculum was well-structured and aligned with industry requirements. most of the faculty members were knowledgeable and supportive, and they explained concepts clearly. practical learning through labs, projects, and assignments was given good importance. regular tests and evaluations helped in maintaining academic discipline. | [placements]: placement opportunities at jaypee institute of information technology, noida are really good and consistent every year. a high percentage of students get placed, especially from cse and it branches. many reputed companies like infosys, tcs, wipro, accenture, amazon, and other tech firms visit the campus. the average package is decent, and the highest packages go to top-performing students in technical branches. the placement cell is very supportive and helps students with training, mock interviews, and guidance throughout the process. overall, the placement process is smooth and satisfactory. | [campus life]: campus life at jaypee institute of information technology, noida is quite dull compared to many other colleges. since the campus is small and has limited open space, there are fewer recreational areas for students to relax or hang out. cultural fests and events do happen, but they are limited in scale and frequency. student clubs and societies exist, but overall engagement feels low. due to academic pressure and lack of vibrant activities, the campus often feels quiet. overall, campus life is not very exciting and could definitely be improved.
college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology Noida

review_text: [college infrastructure]: the permanent campus of iiit kottayam is situated in the serene area of valavoor, kottayam. the campus is still developing but includes essential facilities such as well-equipped labs, classrooms, a library, hostels. since it is a newer institution, some infrastructure aspects might still be in progress, but there has been consistent development. classrooms and hostels are equipped with high-speed internet connectivity. many classrooms have smart boards and projectors. computer labs, electronic labs, networking labs, and iot robotics labs. hostels are cleaned daily and mess follows strict hygiene protocols with regular checks and audits | [academics]: faculty members often have advanced degrees(ph.d., m.tech) and significant industry or research experience. use of smart boards, multimedia presentations, and interactive software to facilitate engaging and dynamic learning experiences. regular quizzes, assignments, and projects ensure consistent engagement and understanding. the curriculum is periodically reviewed and updated to incorporate the latest developments in technology and industry practices. strong focus on core technical skills, including programming, database management, networking and system design. | [placements]: the quality of placements at iiit kottayam has been generally positive, with significant number of students securing positions in companies. the placement cell is proactive in bringing a variety of companies to campus, covering sectors such as it, finance, consulting, and more. on average, around 85-90% of the students are placed every year. the highest salary package offered in recent years was approximately rs.58 lakhs per annum. this offer was typically made to students from the computer science and engineering (cse) course. the average salary package tends to be in the range of rs. 10-12 lakhs per annum. the placement process is well-structured, beginning with pre-placement talks, followed by aptitude tests, technical interviews and hr interviews. the college provides robust support throughout the placement process. | [campus life]: the campus includes modern facilities such as well equipped laboratories, smart classrooms, a library. high-speed wifi is available throughout the campus, and classrooms are equipped with smart boards and projectors to enhance the learning experience. being a relatively new institute , some parts of the campus infrastructure are still under development. located in a more rural settings, students might find fewer immediate access to urban amenities and entertainment options compared to metropolitan campuses. various technical clubs such as coding clubs, robotics clubs and electronics clubs. regular workshops, seminars, coding competitions. annual cultural festivals and events that include music, dance, drama, and art competitions. facilities for both indoor and outdoor sports. students come from various parts of country, fostering a diverse and inclusive environment. the administrative and support staff are responsive and efficient, ensuring the smooth functioning of the campus and providing assistance to students as needed.
college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology Kottayam

review_text: [college infrastructure]: infrastructure is pretty good. it has lush green surrounding. all labs are air conditioned and are also equipped with latest technologies. there is wifi in every hostel and academic building but they don't work in academic building because they have jammer there. | [academics]: academics related college is average but around 40% of the professors have masters degree from well known colleges from india. most of the study is done by digitally. curriculum is not upto date but some faculties are very good in there subjects. | [placements]: for computer science engineering students there has been 100% placements from past few years and it students also has very good placement report but apart from these two field other field has not so good placements. | [campus life]: campus life is good in when its evening but its very chaotic everywhere and students can engage themselves into various activities or games and also every day there is are shows organised by the clubs. college is situated in a small town where you don't have access to ola or uber. you have to take autos to go anywhere else and they charge way too high

[college infrastructure]: academic buildings are more or less modest. many lecture halls are old- fashioned with the traditional blackboard & chalk (which is not bad at all). though many of these support projectors, not many professors make use of it. the acoustics within the lecture halls is average & students sitting at the back generally find it difficult to grasp the lectures efficiently. lack of air conditioning makes it seemingly impossible to sit in these halls during the scorching hot summers.the hostels are nice | [academics]: many a times people have struggled to find books which they need in the library. it may be due to highly disorganized way in which the library keeps the books. one can find a mathematics books in the physics section, a chemistry one in computer science's section etc. there's no specific place to look for a particular book even with the reference from book catalog. besides, there seems to be a dearth of popular course-books. hence, their availability is governed by the first-come-first basis. | [placements]: engineering institutes in india when it comes to placement. the placement rate has consistently been more than 100%. best companies like facebook, microsoft, google, amazon, oracle, l&t, tata steel, reliance, infosys etc visit the campus and recruit candidates every year. the average ctc package varies for different branches, but is pretty decent. due to lesser amount of academic workload many people also prepare for and qualify other competitive exams like upsc, cat, gre etc. | [campus life]: the students are undoubtedly the biggest asset to the institute. various activities are organized in iit-gymkhana by the students, right from the scratch. kashiyatra, the annual cultural festival of the institute attracts, although not much, but still ample crowd from universities across india, to witness the awesome display of talent by the iit (bhu) student fraternity inspired the lifestyle of the holy city. various clubs like western / indian music club, cine club, literary club etc function.
college_name: Indian Institute of Technology Banaras Hindu University Varanasi

review_text: [college infrastructure]: mnit provides a good infrastructure. lecture hall complex 'vltc' is claimed to be asia's largest lecture hall complex. laboratories are not very good as it has older equipments. central library provides very good resources and environment. it has a single football ground which is also used for cricket,kabaddi and athletics. there are three volleyball courts,two badminton courts,three lawn tennis courts but no swimming pool :( . hostels are old.some of them need to be renovated. | [academics]: i have opted for civil engineering. although, i am in my second semester so i can't tell much about my stream as we learn common subjects in first year. i am not much satisfied with the curriculum. more projects and practical knowledge should be given. students and professors (not all) are mainly concerned for exams and study according. we didn't have any industrial visits in our first year and neither have i heard from my seniors of civil branch regarding industria visits. | [placements]: a lot of companies come and offer jobs but they are mainly offer jobs in non core. placements for cse,ece,meta and chemical are good. average placements are 6-7 lpa for core branches. scenario for placements is good but it needs to be improved. on campus internships should be made better. | [campus life]: mnit is situated in heart of pink city. wtp and gt(favorite and common place for mnitians to hangout for) are just at walking distance. there are lot of activities,mainly club meetings! we have two big fests namely sphinx and blitzchalag.
college_name: Malaviya National Institute of Technology Jaipur

review_text: [college infrastructure]: nit suratkal is a 250+ odd acre campus with great legacy of producing engineers from various fields like mechanical engineering. great infrastructure in campus electronics and communication engineering is a great course in the campus | [academics]: electronics and communication engineering in the campus is a good demanded field which has great faculty with phds from foreign universities and older iims. projects play a key role and btech projects provide insights of course clearly | [placements]: placements are pretty decent in the campus. for electronics and communications engineering one can expect an average ctc of 9lakhs per annum with highest package ranging to 21 lakhs per annum. one can expect good life here | [campus life]: outstanding campus life in the middle if city and easy access to various facilities. the bonds and the networks you create are life lasting and you?ll cherish them forever. nit suratkal is a brand in itself and it reflects in campus life
college_name: Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology Surat

review_text: [college infrastructure]: classrooms are clean and well maintained some classes have smart boards some not .library closes soon it is a problem in library .yes living spaces are clean there are sports centre in college but not well maintained | [academics]: i am pursuing my b.tech in information technology because this field has good scope of career and a lot fields to learn and discover . curriculum is not updated it is old they teach us economics and constitution and so many subjects of no use. | [placements]: placements are good than any private college . at least 70-80% students were placed and the heighest package was of 63 lakhs.average salary offered in around 7-8 lakhrs . i don't know about placement process in deep

[college infrastructure]: yes my college has all the necessary infrastructure,facilities and equipment.my college has wheelchair wheelchair accessible entrance,canteen and supercomputing centre.i have free wifi in the library.also maximum no. of important books are available in our library.the classrooms are very neat and clean and the desk and benches are very good.yes the food are hygeinic and living spaces are clean. | [academics]: i'm studying bachelor of technology in electrical engineering department .i choose this because from my childhood i want to be an engineer.sobi choose this as my fashion.i love the circuit diagrams and circuit problems.so i choose electrical branch. the quality of teaching is moderate.teachers teach us the all the new things which are important for us. yes i think my curriculum is updated .it includes all my activities such as participation in events.yes i think my study makes me job ready. | [placements]: placement in our college is average.most of the students from electrical and mechanical branch get job in many big companies .many mechanical students get job in psu .the electrical stidents gets jgovernment job.this year two/three students of electrical and mechanical students get 17 lakhs package.the average package of mechanical is 6 lakh and electrical is 6.5 lakh.minimum 40-50% of our students were placed in good jobs.the placement process is moderate.it is pleasant for me and suportive . | [campus life]: campus life is good .you can participate in all the activities like volleyball,basketball cricket football chess tabletennis tennis etc in college campus.you can also participate in coding club,dramaclub,robotics,bihu club dance clube etc so that you can develop your other activities .the students teachers and support staff are very good, they are supportive.the location of our college is very good.it is located near a hill so that you can adventure in this field.yes it can handle emergencies.

no annual fests are conducted here since this college has more than 60000 students. i enjoyed only one fest which was 2 weeks long and held in the month of february 2023. this was held with the inauguration of the college's silver jubilee. a student will get the books every semester for free from the college itself. sports and extra-curricular activities are held every month in the college. you can participate in any of the sports or activities hassle-free. there are many student groups which are known as student society. i was in two societies named kamakshi-heforshe and microsoft students community.students become eligible for campus placements from the 5th semester. many companies visit the college such as amazon, cognizant, high radius, capgemini, adobe, csg, jp morgan, delloite, etc. the average package is around 8.5 lakhs. this year (2022) highest package offered was 58 lakhs. almost 95% of students get placed every year. my plan after getting a degree is to get placed with a salary of around 20 lpa.

nice placement s opportunities according to the placement cell officials of the vit university, the placement record of the university has been the highest in all the programs in recent years. some of the corporates such as ericsson india global services pvt. ltd. general electric, renault, oracle, volvo, hexagon, qualcomm to name a few visit the vit university to render placements to all the graduates. the article on vit university placement 2020 carries all the official records of placement in the previous years.vit as the name suggests is an expensive private college with various branches in vellore, chennai, and many more cities. it is a good college to get in if your father has enough money in their banks. the academic programs here, are well distributed for the students to gain enough knowledge and experience in their 4 years of college life. the first year all are planned to study the same, 2nd year and thereafter, all the branches divide and spread its hands.

[college infrastructure]: gla university infrastructure comprising of air conditioned ,it enables classrooms,conference room,faculty cabins etc the gla university has various centres of excellence and the whole campus is wifi enabled | [academics]: i am studying mba in gla university mathura.quality kf teaching in gla university is excellent all the faculties are quite good.the caurse is relevant .it helps students to get industry ready. all the faculties are qualified. | [placements]: the highest package offered to management students stood at inr 19.8lpa ,respectively the overall average package offered dunrig gla university placement in 2023 was inr 6.3lpa more than 3000 offers were made | [campus life]: good

[college infrastructure]: the college has a good infrastructure. there is a wi-fi network in the college. all labs and classrooms of the college are clean. there is a big library where we can go and study there without any disturbance. the college has its canteen, where good food is provided at a low price. in case of emergency, there are also medical facilities provided in the college. there is also a sports ground for indoor and outdoor games.environment here is well and good and atmosphere was awesome. | [academics]: teachers here were more talented in our course subjects, and they know how to treat the student. college faculty had lots of experience of about 10-15 years. faculty teaching quality was excellent, which we can easily understand. faculty members are friendly with the students and they solve every problem .faculty members in the college were helpful to solve the problems of students in an easy way. | [placements]: in our batch, there were 200 or 250 students. almost 75-85% got placed in our course. i also attended the interview and got selected for the second round, which is a technical round.google, amazon, microsoft and developers team companies visited to recruit. almost 5-7% of the students got an internship from our course in companies.placement cell has provided us with enough guidance and support.

there are serval clubs like poetry club, natraj dance club, ninad singing club, codebusters, gla voice, etc. which are connect their fest in every month. here the library is very big something 400000 of books collection .here brief and extracurricular activities on the campus are much goodgla university provides its scholarship rs of 25000 to those students who get 80% and above marks in pcm in class 12th. and those students crack the jee examination with a percentile of 80 or above. gla university also provides them with scholarships according to their, marks.

the curriculum is between tech and design .it's more like computer science and design .faculty is good and the university is trying for the best. the electives are from the 6th semester and are great. from graphics to industrial all types of electives are available.campus life is great. the campus is huge and nsut has many events going on. there are many societies you can join . moksha rezonance is a great fest all over north india. the sports facility is available at the hostel as well university sports complex.

my course is computer science engineering and according to its placement i selected this course because i am interested too much in it main faculty and guest faculty both are there. hod and top faculty are best in their qualification and also guest faculty are passed from iit and have mtech and phd. course fees: inr 210000 per year. internship opportunities are available on campus but there is a thing for boys and girls both are different a girl can get internships easily but for a boy, it's too difficult to on campus you have to be petty good at coding and with a gpa of 9 +. so about the fee, i would like to say it's a bit costly as other govt colleges are much more cost-effective but this jac delhi college is not. college + hostel+mess for my course is 12-13 lakh for 4 years which is costly and for scholarships, there is one merit-based that is available for everyone.nsit delhi offers a 4 year btech program with 10 specializations. cse is the most popular branch of the btech program. it offers two minor specializations in ai and data science that can be opted for by a student, yielding them a btech degree in cse with ai or data science. admission to the program is based on jee main scores and jac delhi counselling. the faculty members of nsit delhi are highly skilled and qualified professionals to provide the appropriate guidance and learning experience to the students of the institute. most of the faculty members are postgraduates while some of them are ex-civil professionals and phd holders.

[college infrastructure]: the facilities of dms are the same like central iit. the campus is wi-fi enabled, there are many sufficient labs, and dms has a very separate library apart from the central iit library. the medical facility is the iit hospital, and the campus has a central sports ground for football, basketball, badminton and tennis court. the classrooms are air-conditioned and well managed. the quality of food in the canteen is good.same goes for the hostel rooms just like all government college hostels. | [academics]: the faculty is well qualified and very knowledgeable. they use a lot of case studies and real-world problems to solve all the problems and give good lectures to the students. teachers are friendly and very helpful. the student to faculty ratio is 40:1 approximately. there is a good amount of an industry exposure through business lectures from business leaders of the industry. also, a global field study trip is organised to give industry exposure to the students in foreign countries. the course curriculum is useful and extremely flexible as per student's needs. | [placements]: probably,the average salary offered was around rs. 12-13 lpa. approximately 80% of the students got placed. the highest salary offered is usually rs. 21-24 lpa and the lowest offered to be rs. 8lpa. may be 15-20 students have pre-placement offers from the summer internship processes. the companies are visiting the campus are ey, deloitte, google, rockwell, shell oil, etc. the roles offered to the students were the project manager, business analyst, marketing and sales, etc.

placement is very good,once you come to delhi technological university then do not think much about placement just focus on studies because college placement department work very hard for placement of its students and you will definitely get good placement here if you have talent.our course curriculum is very good not many college courses are updated as ours. faculties are also very good and they are very good at teaching and explaining but at last all depends on you how careful are you about your study. labs are enough good,very well equipped.

[college infrastructure]: all infrastructure, facilities are present, all of which are well-maintained, hygiene is top notch. libraries are spacious with variety of books present. laboratories have all necessary equipment present. food is hygienic. | [academics]: i studied electronics & instrumentation. teaching quality is average. curriculum was not up to date. classroom teachings are not enough to be job ready. rote learning is more emphasized upon, also that fetches you marks, but experiential learning takes a backseat. | [placements]: placements opportunities are more for the computer and it branches. for the other branches, some companies visit, but with very less compensation. highest package that i know of was more than 30 lakhs. there were various mass recruiters who also visited, who offered around 4.5 lac package. college was supportive in the placement journey. | [campus life]: campus was very big with top-class facilities. staff is very friendly. lots of sports activities are there. location is safe, kmc hospital is near to the campus. | [anything else]: overall experience was good. most importantly, you have to focus on your academics, and take part in all the competitions that are organized.

[college infrastructure]: during be days mess food was not very good. rooms are spacious enough and well ventilated. library is very big and lots of books are there which can be issued. laboratories in our department are well maintained and can be accessed any time. | [academics]: its one of the best college in the country to study the subject. teachers are well trained professionals having industry and research oriented background. programming is made really easy and we aere taught from the very base | [placements]: payed internships are provided by the college in reputed companies. more than 90% of the students were placed. median salary was around 9lakhs. highest was above 50lakhs. lots of reputed companies comes like amazon, microsoft, etc.
college_name: Birla Institute of Technology and Science Pilani

review_text: [college infrastructure]: nit kurukshetra boasts comprehensive infrastructure, ensuring students have access to state-of-the-art facilities. classrooms are equipped with modern amenities, including smart boards and high-speed wifi, enhancing the learning experience. laboratories are well-maintained, fostering a conducive environment for practical education. the library is a treasure trove of knowledge, stocked with a vast collection of books and digital resources. the sports facilities are extensive, promoting physical well-being and fostering a spirit of competition. hostels provide clean and comfortable living spaces for students. the institute places a premium on hygiene, ensuring that the living conditions meet high standards. the hostel environment encourages a sense of community among residents. | [academics]: i am studying ece and the quality of teaching at nit kurukshetra is generally commendable, with a focus on academic excellence. the faculty is often experienced and knowledgeable in their respective fields. the institute emphasizes practical learning through well-equipped laboratories and encourages faculty-student interaction. nits typically strive to keep their curricula updated to align with industry standards and technological advancements. this ensures that students are exposed to the latest developments in their fields of study. the aim is to make students not only academically proficient but also job-ready. the curriculum is designed to provide a strong theoretical foundation along with practical skills. through internships, projects, and industry collaborations, students have opportunities to apply their knowledge in real-world scenarios. this approach enhances their employability and readiness for professional roles review_text: there is a 100% placement in vit, vellore. all sorts of companies visit college every year. this year 502 companies visited the campus. jobs can be in the it sector (3.5 lakh pa), in dream companies (>5 lakh pa), and super dream companies (>10 lakh pa). the highest package was 30 lakh pa offered by microsoft and the average package was 5.75 lakh pa. vit also gives internships to students. there are internship expos conducted and everything is quite good. students may also do internships in start-ups from vit itself. i myself haven't done any internship yet but surely will this year.the course curriculum in vit is one of the best in india for all branches. students can make their own timetable from the second semester. this includes choosing your own faculty, place of lecture, and class timings. exams in vit are divided into two cats (continuous assessment tests) and one fat (final assessment test). apart from this, there are digital assignments and quizzes in all subjects. students may also opt for additional learning if they fall short of marks in a subject.
